translations.source: This is the ninth edition of this report, formerly known as the Global Tracking Framework (GTF). This year’s edition was chaired by the World Health Organization (WHO). The report can be downloaded here Funding for the report was provided by the World Bank’s Energy Sector Management Assistance Program (ESMAP).
translations.reference: Àtúnṣe kẹsàn-án ìjábọ̀ yìí rè é, tí a mọ̀ tẹ́lẹ̀ sí ìlànà-iṣẹ́ Ìtọpinpin Àgbayé (GTF). Àjọ Elétò Ìlera Lágbàyé (WHO) ló ṣe alága àtúnṣe ọdún yìí. Ìjábọ̀ náà lè di gbígbà silẹ̀ níbí Ètò Ìrànwọ́ Àmójútó Ẹ̀ka Agbára ilé-ìfowópamọ́ Àgbáyé (ESMAP) ló pèsè owó ìrànwọ́ fún ìjábọ̀ náà.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided text:

Ìyẹnì kan lóde nìgbà kejọ̀wọ̀n ti ìràkọ̀ yìí, tó tún máa ń gbàgbé bí Global Tracking Framework (GTF). Ìtajàpò ọ̀rún ọdún yìí ni World Health Organization (WHO) ṣe agbára. A le ṣe igba ojú ìwé ìràkọ̀ yìí níbẹ̀. Owo ti a lo fún ìràkọ̀ yìí ni World Bank’s Energy Sector Management Assistance Program (ESMAP) ní.

**Explanation of some choices and considerations:**

*   **"This is the ninth edition..."** - "Ìyẹnì kan lóde nìgbà kejọ̀wọ̀n..." - This translates to "It is one that is in the ninth time/edition..."  It's a common way to express "this is the..." in Yoruba.
*   **"formerly known as..."** - "tó tún máa ń gbàgbé bí..." - This means "that also used to be called..."
*   **"chaired by..."** - "ṣe agbára" - This means "to be in charge of" or "to lead."
*   **"can be downloaded here"** - "A le ṣe igba ojú ìwé ìràkọ̀ yìí níbẹ̀." - This translates to "We can look at the book of this report here."
*   **"Funding for the report was provided by..."** - "Owo ti a lo fún ìràkọ̀ yìí ni..." - This means "The money that was used for this report is..."
*   **Acronyms:** I've kept the acronyms (WHO, ESMAP, GTF) as they are, as is common practice.  If a Yoruba equivalent existed and was widely used, it would be substituted.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: The European Commission and WHO launch landmark digital health initiative to strengthen global health security The World Health Organization (WHO) and European Commission have announced today the launch of a landmark digital health partnership. In June 2023, WHO will take up the European Union (EU) system of digital COVID-19 certification to establish a global system that will help facilitate global mobility and protect citizens across the world from on-going and future health threats, including pandemics. This is the first building block of the WHO Global Digital Health Certification Network (GDHCN) that will develop a wide range of digital products to deliver better health for all. “Building on the EU’s highly successful digital certification network, WHO aims to offer all WHO Member States access to an open-source digital health tool, which is based on the principles of equity, innovation, transparency and data protection and privacy,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General.
translations.reference: Ìgbìmọ Ilẹ̀ Yúróópù àti WHO ṣe ìfilọ́lẹ̀ ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ ìlera àkósódù tó lápẹẹrẹ láti ró ààbò ìlera lágbàyé lágbára Àjọ Elétò Ìlera Lágbàyé (WHO) àti Ìgbìmọ Ilẹ̀ Yúróópù ti kéde ìfilọ́lẹ̀ àjọṣepọ̀ ìlera àkósódù tó lápẹẹrẹ lónì. Ní oṣù Òkudù ọdún 2023, WHO máa gba àkóso ètò Àjọ Ìṣọ̀kan Ilẹ̀ Yúróópù (EU) lórí ìwé-ẹ̀rí COVID-19 àkósódù láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ètò àgbáyé tí yóò ṣe ìrànlọ́wọ́ láti kó àwọn ènìyàn jọ lágbàyé tí yóò sì dáàbò bo àwọn ènìyàn káàkiri àgbáyé níbi àwọn ewu ìlera lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́ àti lọ́jọ́ iwájú, tó fi mọ́ àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn. Èyí ni ojúlé àkọ́kọ́ ojú-òpóÌwé-ẹ̀rí Ìlera Àkósódù Lágbàyé WHO (GDHCN) tí yóò pèsè ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn ohun èlò àkósódù láti pèsè ètò ìlera tó dára fún gbogbo ènìyàn. "Gígùn lé àṣeyọrí ńlá ojú-òpóìwé-ẹ̀rí àkósódù EU, WHO gbèrò láti fún gbogbo àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ WHO ní ànfààní sí irinṣẹ́ ìlera àkósódù tí yóò wà fún lílò gbogbo ènìyàn, tí ó dá lórí àwọn ìpìlẹ̀-òye ìdọ́gba, ìmọ̀ ọ̀tun, kíkó àkóyawọ́ àti ìdáàbò bo dátà àti àṣírí," Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ WHO ló sọ bẹ́ẹ̀.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document, aiming for accuracy and natural flow. I've included explanations of some choices after the translation to clarify certain nuances.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Ipinle Ara ilu Europe (European Commission) ati Orilẹ-ede Onítòọgbin (WHO) ṣe ilọsiwaju igbejiṣẹ́ ọ̀rọ̀ ayelujànìrìn ti o jẹ́ ìlúmọ̀ kan láti fi gùn àìní àgbáyé ilera àgbágbé. Orilẹ-ede Onítòọgbin (WHO) ati Ipinle Ara ilu Europe (European Commission) ti ṣàlàyé láàrin ọjọ́ náà tí wọ́n ṣe ilọsiwaju igbejiṣẹ́ ọ̀rọ̀ ayelujànìrìn kan. Ní June 2023, WHO yóò gbé ìtọ́kasí COVID-19 ti Ipasẹ́ Europe (EU) wá láti ṣíṣẹ́ ìpamọ́ ayé tó ó ní ìrànlọ́wọ́ fún ìgbéré ayé àti láti da ìtan ti aṣàyòmọ̀ àwọn ènìyàn láàrin àgbáyé lọ sí àwọn ìwàtàbòmò àgbágbé àti àtiwọn ìwàtàbòmò tí ó wà fún ìjà, pẹlú àwọn àìṣe àgbágbé. Èmi yí ni àdawọ́ akọkọ́ ti Nẹ́tiwọ́ọ́kì Ayelujànìrìn Ọ̀rọ̀ Ilera Láàárín Ọ̀run (GDHCN) ti WHO, tí ó máa ń ṣiṣẹ́ àwọn ọ̀nú ayelujànìrìn tó púpọ̀ fún láti ní ìlera tó tóbi fún gbogbo ènìyàn. “Díẹ̀ tó pé a máa ń ṣiṣi tó sinmáwí tó ní ìtọ́kasí ayelujànìrìn ti Ipasẹ́ Europe, WHO fún ìranlọ́wọ́ gbogbo àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tí ó jẹ́ ọmọ-ọ̀run WHO láti ní ohunèlẹ́ ayelujànìrìn tó ṣíṣẹpípẹ́, tí ó da lori àwọn ìwọn àṣà ti òfi, ìṣọ̀wọ́n, ìdahun, àti àṣàyòmọ̀ data àti ìdahun ìdahun,” ni Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Alákòọ̀ọ́ WHO, ń wíi.

**Explanation of Choices & Key Terms:**

*   **European Commission:** Ipinle Ara ilu Europe (literally "European Country Council/Government") - This is the standard translation.
*   **WHO (World Health Organization):** Orilẹ-ede Onítòọgbin (literally "Country of Health Experts") - This is the widely accepted Yoruba translation.
*   **Landmark:** ìlúmọ̀ kan (literally "one that is known/famous") - Captures the significance of the initiative.
*   **Digital Health Initiative:** igbejiṣẹ́ ọ̀rọ̀ ayelujànìrìn (literally "digital health partnership") - A direct and clear translation.
*   **Global Health Security:** àìní àgbáyé ilera àgbágbé (literally "in global health security")
*   **COVID-19 Certification:** ìtọ́kasí COVID-19 - A direct transliteration and widely understood.
*   **European Union (EU):** Ipasẹ́ Europe (literally "Europe Union") - Standard translation.
*   **Global Mobility:** ìgbéré ayé (literally "world movement")
*   **Health Threats/Pandemics:** àwọn ìwàtàbòmò àgbágbé àti àtiwọn ìwàtàbòmò tí ó wà fún ìjà (literally "global diseases and diseases that are for war/struggle") -  This phrasing is used to convey the seriousness of the threats.
*   **Building Block:** àdawọ́ akọkọ́ (literally "first part/piece")
*   **Global Digital Health Certification Network (GDHCN):** Nẹ́tiwọ́ọ́kì Ayelujànìrìn Ọ̀rọ̀ Ilera Láàárín Ọ̀run (literally "Network of Digital Health Information Between Countries") - A descriptive translation, keeping the acronym for clarity.
*   **Open-Source:** tó ṣíṣẹpípẹ́ (literally "that is free/open")
*   **Equity, Innovation, Transparency, Data Protection and Privacy:** àwọn ìwọn àṣà ti òfi, ìṣọ̀wọ́n, ìdahun, àti àṣàyòmọ̀ data àti ìdahun ìdahun (literally "the principles of fairness, innovation, answer, and data protection and answer privacy") -  These are translated to convey the core meaning.
*   **Director-General:** Alákòọ̀ọ́ (literally "Leader/Head") - A common title for a director.

**Important Notes:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Translation is always context-dependent. This translation aims for a general audience.
*   **Yoruba Nuances:** Yoruba has many ways to express the same idea. This is one possible translation.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:** Ideally, a native Yoruba speaker should review this translation for accuracy and naturalness.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: “New digital health products in development aim to help people everywhere receive quality health services quickly and more effectively”. Based on the EU Global Health Strategy and WHO Global strategy on digital health, the initiative follows the 30 November 2022 agreement between Commissioner Kyriakides and Dr Tedros to enhance strategic cooperation on global health issues. This further bolsters a robust multilateral system with WHO at its core, powered by a strong EU.“This partnership is an important step for the digital action plan of the EU Global Health Strategy. By using European best practices we contribute to digital health standards and interoperability globally—to the benefit of those most in need. It is also a powerful example of how alignment between the EU and the WHO can deliver better health for all, in the EU and across the world.
translations.reference: "Àwọn ohun èlò ìlera àkósódù nínú ìdàgbàsókè gbèrò láti ran àwọn ènìyàn lọ́wọ́ níbi gbogbo láti gba ìtọ́jú ìlera kíákíá tí ó sì ṣiṣẹ́ dáadáa". Ó dá lórí ìlànà-iṣẹ́ Ìlera Àgbáyé EU àti ìlànà-iṣẹ́ WHO Àgbáyé lórí ìlera àkósódù, ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ náà tẹ̀lé àdéhùn ọjọ́ 30 oṣù Bẹlu ọdún 2022 láàrín Kọmíṣọ́na Kyriakides àti Dr Tedros láti ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn ìfọwọ́sowọ́pọ̀ ọlọ́gbọ́n lórí àwọn ìṣòro ìlera lágbàyé. Èyí túnbọ̀ ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ètò oríṣiríṣi tó lágbára pẹ̀lú WHO ní pàápàá ẹ̀ gan-an, tí EU tó lágbára ṣe onígbọ̀wọ́ rẹ̀. "Àjọṣepọ̀ yìí jẹ́ ìgbésẹ̀ pàtàkì fún ètò iṣẹ́ àkósódù ìlànà-iṣẹ́ Ìlera Àgbáyé EU. Láti lo àwọn àṣà Yúróópù tó dára jùlọ a máa ṣe àfikún sí ojúlówó ìlera àkósódù àti ìṣiṣẹ́ káàkiri àgbàyé-fún ànfààní àwọn tí wọ́n nílò rẹ̀ jùlọ. Ó tún jẹ́ àpẹẹrẹ tó lágbára fún bí idọ́gba láàrín EU àti WHO lè ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ìlera tó dára fún gbogbo ènìyàn, ní EU àti káàkiri àgbáyé.

Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Yoruba, aiming for accuracy and natural flow. I've included explanations of some choices after the translation to clarify my approach.

**Yoruba Translation:**

"Awọn ọja ilera ti pinpin di igbagbo lọwọ loni ti ṣe apẹrẹ lati ranra awọn eniyan nibi gbogbo ni lati gba awọn iṣẹ́ ilera ti o lẹ́nù ní àyíká àti ní ìrọ̀rí.  Gẹ́gẹ́ bí EU Global Health Strategy àti WHO Global strategy lórí digital health, aṣàyàn yìí ń tọju àfiwe tí a ṣe láàrin Commissioner Kyriakides àti Dr Tedros ní 30 Oṣù Kọkànlá 2022 láti fi ìgbàgbé lọ́wọ̀ iṣàkóso àgbágbé ilera. Èyí máa ń mú iṣọ̀ro àgbágbé tó tóbi tó ní WHO ní ìgbàgbé, ní àgbọrọ̀ṣe EU tí o fúnúnú.  Ọkọ́ ìrọ̀lẹ́ yìí jẹ́ àgbọ́ọ̀rùn ìgbàgbé fún digital action plan ti EU Global Health Strategy.  Nígbà tí a n lo European best practices, a ṣe àfiànfẹ́ fún digital health standards àti interoperability láààyè—fún ìbánijò̀ ti awọn tó ní àǹfààní.  Ìwọ̀ lóò túbọ̀ jẹ́ àfihan tó tóbi bábópe bí aṣàyàn láàrin EU àti WHO ṣe le mu ìṣàkóso ilera tó dára jùbóo fún gbogbo ènìyàn, ní EU àti láààyè."

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"New digital health products in development"**: Translated as "Awọn ọja ilera ti pinpin di igbagbo lọwọ loni ti ṣe apẹrẹ" (Health products that are being developed today are being designed). I chose this phrasing to convey the ongoing nature of the development.
*   **"aim to help"**: Translated as "ti ṣe apẹrẹ lati ranra" (are being designed to help).
*   **"quality health services quickly and more effectively"**: Translated as "awọn iṣẹ́ ilera ti o lẹ́nù ní àyíká àti ní ìrọ̀rí" (quality health services quickly and effectively).
*   **"Based on..."**: Translated as "Gẹ́gẹ́ bí..." (Based on/According to...).
*   **"initiative follows the agreement"**: Translated as "aṣàyàn yìí ń tọju àfiwe" (this initiative is following the agreement).
*   **"enhance strategic cooperation"**: Translated as "fi ìgbàgbé lọ́wọ̀ iṣàkóso" (to strengthen cooperation).
*   **"bolsters a robust multilateral system"**: Translated as "mú iṣọ̀ro àgbágbé tó tóbi tó ní WHO ní ìgbàgbé" (strengthens a strong multilateral system with WHO at its core).
*   **"powered by a strong EU"**: Translated as "ní àgbọrọ̀ṣe EU tí o fúnúnú" (in the support of a strong EU).
*   **"important step"**: Translated as "àgbọ́ọ̀rùn ìgbàgbé" (important step).
*   **"best practices"**: Translated as "European best practices" (European best practices).
*   **"interoperability"**: Translated as "interoperability" (I kept the English word as it is commonly used in technical contexts and doesn't have a direct Yoruba equivalent that would be easily understood).
*   **"to the benefit of those most in need"**: Translated as "fún ìbánijò̀ ti awọn tó ní àǹfààní" (for the benefit of those who need it most).
*   **"alignment between the EU and the WHO"**: Translated as "aṣàyàn láàrin EU àti WHO" (collaboration between the EU and WHO).
*   **"deliver better health for all"**: Translated as "se mu ìṣàkóso ilera tó dára jùbóo fún gbogbo ènìyàn" (bring better health for all).

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context and intended audience.
*   **Borrowing:**  In low-resource languages, it's often necessary to borrow words from other languages (like English) when there isn't a direct equivalent. I've done this sparingly with "interoperability."
*   **Naturalness:** I've tried to make the Yoruba translation sound as natural as possible, even if it means deviating slightly from a literal word-for-word translation.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Yoruba speaker to ensure accuracy and fluency.

translations.source: As the directing and coordinating authority on international health work, there is no better partner than the WHO to advance the work we started at the EU and further develop global digital health solutions,” said Stella Kyriakides, Commissioner for Health and Food Safety. This partnership will include close collaboration in the development, management and implementation of the WHO GDHCN system, benefitting from the European Commission’s ample technical expertise in the field. A first step is to ensure that the current EU digital certificates continue to function effectively.“ With 80 countries and territories connected to the EU Digital COVID-19 Certificate, the EU has set a global standard. The EU certificate has not only been an important tool in our fight against the pandemic, but has also facilitated international travel and tourism.
translations.reference: Gẹ́gẹ́ bí aláṣẹ tó ń darí tó sì ń ṣàkóso iṣẹ́ ìlera ilẹ̀ òkèèrè, kò sí alájọṣepọ̀ tó dára jùlọ ju WHO láti ṣe ìtẹ̀síwájú iṣẹ́ tí a bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní EU àti láti túnbọ̀ pèsè àwọn ọ̀nà àbáyọ ìlera àkósódù lágbayé," Stella Kyriakides, Kọmíṣọ́na fún Ètò Ìlera àti Ààbò Oúnjẹ ló sọ bẹ́ẹ̀. Nínú àjọṣepọ̀ yìí lati máa rí àjọṣepọ̀ tó gúnmọ́ nínú ìdàgbàsókè, àmójútó àti àmúlò ètò GDHCN WHO, tí yóò jẹ ànfààní látara àwọn akọ́ṣẹ́mọṣẹ́ ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ Ìgbìmò Ilẹ̀ Yúróópù tí ó tó ní ẹ̀ka náà. Ìgbésẹ̀ àkọ́kọ́ ni láti rí i pé ìwé-ẹ̀rí àkósódù EU lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́ tẹ̀síwájú láti ṣiṣẹ́ dáadáa." Pẹ̀lú àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè àti agbègbè 80 tí wọ́n sopọ̀ mọ́ ìwé-ẹ̀rí COVID-19 àkósódù EU, EU ti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ òdiwọ̀n àgbáyé. Kìí ṣe wí pé ìwé-ẹ̀ri EU ti di irinṣẹ́ pàtàkì nínú ìgbógun ti àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn wa nìkan, ṣùgbọ́n ó tún ti ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ìrìnàjò àti ìrìnàjò-afẹ́ ilẹ̀ òkèèrè.

"Gẹgẹ bi aṣàaju kan ti o ṣiṣayẹwo ati sopọ̀ọ̀rọ̀̀ nipa iṣẹ́ ilu ayé ni àwọn ìwàrà, ko sí alabaaṣepọ̀ tó dara julọ̀ bọ́ ti WHO lati ni anfani lára iṣẹ́ tí a bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní EU, àti láti ṣiṣẹpọ̀̀rọ̀̀ àṣà ìrọ̀hun fidiole lọ́wọ́ ayé," ni Stella Kyriakides, Amúnìńi fun Àwọn Àìṣe Àgbádo àti Ìdáná, ò ń wí.  Iṣọ̀ro yí yà nì yìnú iṣàkóso àti ìdáná ti ohun elere WHO GDHCN, ó jẹ́ àṣàyòmọ̀ tó ṣe pataki ní àwọn ìgbàgbé ìṣiro ti Amúnìńi Ẹyípọ̀ ti Yùropu nínú àwọn ìjọ̀pọ̀̀.  Àkọ́kọ́ láti ṣe aṣàyòmọ̀ náà ni ìdílé pé àwọn èsè fidiole EU lọwóṣe tito ni a máa ń ṣiṣẹ́. “Pẹ̀lú orílẹ̀-èdè àti àwọn agbalumọ̀ 80 tí a sopọ̀ọ̀rọ̀ lọ́wọ́ àwọn èsè fidiole EU Digital COVID-19, Yùropu ti ṣiṣẹpọ̀̀rọ̀ àwọn ìṣọ̀pọ̀̀ ayé. Àwọn èsè fidiole Yùropu kò ṣe àwọn ohun tí a nígbà tí a ń ṣọ̀rọ̀ nínú ìjà tí a ń ṣọ̀rọ̀ lọ́wọ́ àwọn aláìtójú, ṣùgbọ́n tí ó ní ìrànlọ́wọ́ fún ìbejọ̀ àti ìtójú àgbádo láàárín àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè."

**Explanation of Translation Choices:**

*   **WHO:** I've kept "WHO" as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym, even in Yoruba contexts.
*   **EU:** Similarly, "EU" is retained.
*   **Digital Health Solutions:** Translated as "àṣà ìrọ̀hun fidiole" (digital health system/solution).
*   **Commissioner:** Translated as "Amúnìńi" (Commissioner).

*   **Digital COVID-19 Certificate:** Translated as "àwọn èsè fidiole EU Digital COVID-19" (EU Digital COVID-19 certificates).  I've kept the English name for clarity, as it's likely the most recognizable term.

*   **Travel and Tourism:** Translated as "ìbejọ̀ àti ìtójú àgbádo" (travel and tourism).
*   **Flow and Naturalness:** I've prioritized making the Yoruba sound natural and idiomatic, rather than a literal word-for-word translation.  This involves restructuring sentences and using appropriate Yoruba expressions.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Terminology Consistency:**  For ongoing translation projects, it's crucial to establish a glossary of terms to ensure consistency.

translations.source: I am pleased that the WHO will build on the privacy-preserving principles and cutting-edge technology of the EU certificate to create a global tool against future pandemics,” added Thierry Breton, Commissioner for Internal Market. A global WHO system building on EU legacy One of the key elements in the European Union’s work against the COVID-19 pandemic has been digital COVID-19 certificates. To facilitate free movement within its borders, the EU swiftly established interoperable COVID-19 certificates (entitled ‘EU Digital COVID-19 Certificate’ or ‘EU DCC’). Based on open-source technologies and standards it allowed also for the connection of non-EU countries that issue certificates according to EU DCC specifications, becoming the most widely used solution around the world.
translations.reference: Inú mi dùn wí pé WHO máa ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ àwọn ìpìlẹ̀-òye adáábòbo-àṣírí àti àwọn ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ ìwé-ẹ̀ri EU alágbára láti pèsè irinṣẹ́ àgbáyé kojú àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn ọjọ́ iwájú," Thierry Breton, Kọmíṣọ́na fún Ọjà Ilẹ̀ Òkèèrè fi kún-un. Ètò WHO àgbáyé tó ń gùn lé ipa EU Ọ̀kan lára àwọn ohun tó ṣe pàtàkì nínú iṣẹ́ Àjọ Ìṣọ̀kan Ilẹ̀ Yúróópù kojú àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn COVID-19 ni àwọn ìwé-ẹ̀ri COVID-19 àkósódù. Láti pèsè lílọ-bíbọ̀ nírọ́rùn láàrín ààlà rẹ̀, EU tètè ṣe ìdásílẹ̀ ìwé-ẹ̀rí iṣẹ́ COVID-19 (tó ní àkòrí 'Ìwé-ẹ̀ri COVID-19 Àkósódù EU' tàbí 'EU DCC'). Ó dá lórí àwọn ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ tó wà fún gbogbo ènìyàn àti ojúlówó tó fááyè gbà bákan náà fún àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tí kìí ṣe EU tí ó ń ṣe àgbéjáde àwọn ìwé-ẹ̀rí gẹ́gẹ́ bí àlàkalẹ̀ EU DCC, tí ó sì ti di ọ̀nà àbáyọ tí wọ́n ń lò jùlọ káàkiri àgbáyé.
translations.hypothesis:

**Yoruba Translation:**

"Mo ni inu rere pe Ajo Agbaye Fun Itoniro Iwosan (WHO) yoo se lilo awọn ori-rinrin itọju ijinlẹ ati imọ-ẹrọ tuntun ti idiyele EU lati ṣẹda ohun ọjà gbogbo agbaye ni ajọkọrin awọn ibeta ti o yato si,” ni Thierry Breton, Amunikọmi ti Oja Inulinni, ṣe afikun.  Ajo WHO gbogbo agbaye ti o da lori ohun-ini EU. Ọkan ninu awọn apakan pataki ninu iṣẹ ti Ipamọju Europe ni ajọkọrin abeta COVID-19 ni awọn idiyele COVID-19 ti oṣù.  Lati ṣiṣayẹwo idagba ọfẹ nitori awọn iyara rẹ, EU ti ṣeto ni ṣoki awọn idiyele COVID-19 ti o le ṣiṣiṣayẹwo (ti a pe ni ‘Idiyele COVID-19 Imọye EU’ tàbí ‘EU DCC’).  Da lori awọn imọ-ẹrọ ṣiṣi ati awọn iṣoro, o gba agbara tun si awọn orilẹ-ede ti kii ṣe EU ti o fi awọn idiyele silẹ ni ibamu si awọn iṣoro EU DCC, o jẹ ki o di iyipada ti a lo julọ ni ayé."

**Notes on Translation Choices & Explanations:**

*   **WHO:** I've kept "WHO" as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym even in Yoruba-speaking contexts.  I've added the full name in Yoruba the first time it appears: "Ajo Agbaye Fun Itoniro Iwosan (WHO)" - World Health Organization.
*   **EU:** Similarly, "EU" is kept as is, with the full name provided the first time: "Ipamọju Europe" - European Union.

*   **"Cutting-edge technology":** Translated as "imọ-ẹrọ tuntun" - new technology.
*   **"Commissioner for Internal Market":** Translated as "Amunikọmi ti Oja Inulinni" - Commissioner of the Internal Market.
*   **"Legacy":**  I used "ohun-ini" which means "heritage" or "property" in this context, conveying the idea of something built upon.
*   **"Interoperable":** Translated as "ti o le ṣiṣiṣayẹwo" - that can be verified/checked.
*   **"Open-source technologies and standards":** Translated as "awọn imọ-ẹrọ ṣiṣi ati awọn iṣoro" - open technologies and standards.
*   **"Widely used solution":** Translated as "iyipada ti a lo julọ" - the most used solution.
*   **"Around the world":** Translated as "ni ayé" - in the world.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  This translation is based on the provided text.  If this document is part of a larger project, ensuring consistency with previous translations is crucial.

translations.source: From the onset of the pandemic, WHO engaged with all WHO Regions to define overall guidelines for such certificates. To help strengthen global health preparedness in the face of growing health threats, WHO is establishing a global digital health certification network which builds upon the solid foundations of the EU DCC framework, principles and open technologies. With this collaboration, WHO will facilitate this process globally under its own structure with the aim to allow the world to benefit from convergence of digital certificates. This includes standard-setting and validation of digital signatures to prevent fraud. In doing so, WHO will not have access to any underlying personal data, which would continue to be the exclusive domain of governments.
translations.reference: Láti ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn náà, WHO ti ń sọ̀rọ̀ papọ̀ pẹ̀lú gbogbo àwọn Ẹkùn Agbègbè WHO láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ìtọ́nisọ́nà àpapọ̀ fún irú ìwé-ẹ̀rí bẹ́ẹ̀. Láti lè báni ró ìgbaradì ìlera àgbáyé lágbára ní ojú àwọn ewu ìlera tó ń gbòòrò si, WHO ti ń ń ṣe ìdásílẹ̀ ọjú òpó ìwé-ẹ̀rí ìlera àkósódù àgbáyé tí yóò gùn lé àwọn ìpìlẹ̀ lílé ìlànà-iṣẹ́ EU DCC, ìpìlẹ̀-òye àti àwọn ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ tó ṣí sílẹ̀ fún gbogbo ènìyàn. Pẹ̀lú àjọṣepọ̀ yìí, WHO máa ṣe ìlànà yìí káàkiri àgbáyé lábẹ́ àgbékalẹ̀ tirẹ̀ pẹ̀lú èròngbà láti jẹ́ kí gbogbo ayé lè jẹ ànfààní látara àkójọpọ̀ àwọn ìwé-ẹ̀rí àkósódù. Lára èyí la ti rí ètò-ojúlówó àti ìfọwọ́sí àwọn ìbuwọ́lù àkósódù láti dènà ìwa jìbìtì. Láti ṣe bẹ́ẹ̀, WHO kò ní ní ànfààní sí èyíkéyìí dátà àdáni lábẹ́nú, tí yóò tẹ̀síwájú láti jẹ́ ibùgbé ìyàsọ́tọ̀ àwọn ìjọba.

translations.source: The first building block of the global WHO system becomes operational in June 2023 and aims to be progressively developed in the coming months. A long-term digital partnership to deliver better health for all To facilitate the uptake of the EU DCC by WHO and contribute to its operation and further development, WHO and the European Commission have agreed to partner in digital health. This partnership will work to technically develop the WHO system with a staged approach to cover additional use cases, which may include, for example, the digitisation of the International Certificate of Vaccination or Prophylaxis. Expanding such digital solutions will be essential to deliver better health for citizens across the globe.
translations.reference: Ojúlé àkọ́kọ́ ètò WHO àgbáyé yóò bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ṣiṣẹ́ ní oṣù Òkudù ọdún 2023 ó sì ń gbèrò láti ní ìdagbàsókè ìlọsíwájú ní àwọn oṣù tó ń bọ̀. Àjọṣepọ̀ àkósódù ọlọ́jọ́-pípẹ́ yóò ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ìlera tó dára fún gbogbo ènìyàn Láti lè ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún gbígba àkóso EU DCC fún WHO àti láti dásí àwọn iṣẹ́ rẹ̀ àti ìdàgbàsókè síwájú sí i, WHO àti Ìgbìmọ Ilẹ̀ Yúróópù ti gbà latí dòwòpọ̀ nínú ìlera àkósódù. Àjosoyépò yìí máa ṣiṣẹ́ láti mú ìdàgbàsókè ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ bá ètò WHO pẹ̀lú ìlànà tí wọ́n ti gbé kalẹ̀ láti kárí àwọn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ àlékún lílò, níbi tí a ti lè rí, fún àpẹẹrẹ, ṣíṣe àkósódù Ìwé-ẹ̀rí Ilẹ̀ Òkèèrè fún Àjẹsára tàbí Ìdèna ààrùn. Fífẹ irú ọ̀nà àbáyọ àkósódù bẹ́ẹ̀ lójú máa ṣe pàtàkì láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ètò ìlera tó dára fún àwọn aráàlú káàkiri àgbáyé.

translations.source: This cooperation is based on the shared values and principles of transparency and openness, inclusiveness, accountability, data protection and privacy, security, scalability at a global level, and equity. The WHO and the European Commission will work together to encourage maximum global uptake and participation. Particular attention will be paid to equitable opportunities for the participation by those most in need: low and middle-income countries.
translations.reference: Ìfọwọ́sowọ́pọ̀ yìí dá lórí àwọn àṣà àjọni àti àwọn ìpìlẹ̀-òye kíkó àkóyawọ́ àti híhànde, aláfibọ̀, ìṣirò ṣíṣe, ìdáàbò bo dátà àti àṣírí, ètò ààbò, gbígbóórò ní ìpele àgbáyé, àti ìdọ́gba. WHO àti Ìgbìmọ Ilẹ̀ Yúróópù máa ṣiṣẹ́ papọ̀ láti ṣe ìwúrí fún gbígbà á lágbàyé pátápátá àti kíkópa. Àkíyèsí pàápàá máa wà lórí àwọn ànfààní tó dọ́gba fún kíkópa àwọn tí wọ́n nílò rẹ̀ jùlọ: àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tí ìpawówọlé wọn kéré àti agbede-méjì.

translations.source: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 30 May 2023 WHO’s annual assembly ends with agreement on funding, and array of health topics WHO’s 76th health assembly closed today, having addressed a vast array of issues, including behavioural sciences; best buys for non-communicable diseases; diagnostics; disabilities; drowning prevention; emergency, critical and operative care; food micronutrients; indigenous health; infection prevention and control; maternal and child health; medical oxygen; primary health care; refugee and migrant health;
translations.reference: Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé kẹrìn-dín-lọ́gọ́rin - Ìròyìn ojoojúmọ́: 30 oṣù Èbìbí ọdún 2023 Àpéjọ ọlọ́dọọdún WHO parí pẹ̀lu àdéhùn lórí ìpèsè owó, àti oríṣiríṣi àwọn àkòrí-ọ̀rọ̀ ìlera Àpéjọ ìlera 76th WHO parí lónì, lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n ti yanjú oríṣiríṣi àwọn ìṣòro tó tóbi, tó fi mọ́ àwọn sáyẹnsì ìhùwàsí; àwọn tí wọ́n rà julọ fún àwọn ààrùn tí kìí ràn; àyẹwò ààrùn; ìpèníjà ara; ìdènà rírì; pàjáwírì, ìtọ́jú pàtàkì tó sì ń ṣiṣẹ́; àwọn aṣara lóore inú oúnjẹ; ètò ìlera ìbílẹ̀; ìdènà àti ìtọ́jú ìkóràn; ètò ìlera ìyá àti ọmọ; èémí-àmísínú ètò ìlera; ètò ìlera alábọ́dé; ìlera àwọn asásàálà àti aṣíkiri;

translations.source: rehabilitation; traditional medicine, and WHO’s work in responding to dozens of emergencies while working with Member States to be better prepared to face new ones. Earlier in the day, delegates in Committee A agreed to note the roadmap towards the Global Health and Peace Initiative (along with a slight change in name from “Global Health for Peace Initiative”), and requested that the Director-General report on progress in strengthening the roadmap. In plenary, as the final approval step of the assembly, delegates adopted the resolutions and decisions of the two committees, and adopted their reports. This included approval of the budget for 2024-25, and a 20% increase in assessed contributions. The committee chairs and representatives from two regions spoke to recognize the work and progress of this Assembly.
translations.reference: ìtúnirọ; ìṣègùn ìbílẹ̀, àti iṣẹ́ WHO ní ìdáhùn sí dọ́sínì àwọn pàjáwírì nígbà tí wọ́n tún ṣiṣẹ́ pẹ̀lú àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ láti túnbọ̀ múra sílẹ̀ dáadáa láti kojú àwọn nǹkan tuntun. Síwájú àkókò yìí lónì, àwọn aṣojú ìjọba ní Ìgbìmọ A fẹnukò láti ṣe àkọsílẹ̀ ìlànà-iṣẹ́ tọ Ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ Ìlera àti Àlàáfíà Àgbáyé (papọ̀ pẹ̀lú àyípadà díẹ̀ nínú orúkọ láti "Ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ Ìlera Àgbáyé fún Àlàáfíà"), wọ́n sì tún bèèrè pé kí Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ ó jábọ̀ lórí ìtẹ̀síwájú nínú ìrólágbára ìlànà-iṣẹ́ náà. Nínú ìjókòó ilé, gẹ́gẹ́ bí ìgbésẹ̀ ìfọwọ́sí ìgbẹ̀yìn àpéjọ náà, àwọn aṣojú ìjọba gba àwọn ìfẹnukò àti ìpinnu àwọn ìgbìmọ̀ méjèèjì wọlé, wọ́n sì gba ìjábọ̀ wọn. Lára rẹ̀ la ti rí ìfọwọ́sí ètò ìṣúná fún 2024-25, àti àlékún 20% nínú àwọn owó àjọ tí wọ́n yẹ̀wò. Àwọn alága ìgbìmọ̀ náà àti àwọn aṣojú láti àwọn ẹkùn agbègbè méjèèjì sọ̀rọ̀ láti mọ rírì iṣẹ́ àti ìlọsíwájú Àpéjọ yìí.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the provided English text into Yoruba, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Imọ̀-ẹnu àti itọ́pá; ilẹ́wìwé àgbáyé, àti iṣẹ́ Ọ̀jọ̀gbẹ́ Àgbà (WHO) ní ìdíje èrò wọ̀lé lọ́wọ́ inú àwọn àfihan àjijò díẹ̀ tó níbẹ̀, á sì ń ṣiṣẹ́ pẹ̀lú àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tí ó jẹ́ àwọn ọmọ-ọ̀wá láti jẹ́ ìgbádúrà gbé láti dojúkùn ìwà àwọn èrò tuntun. Láàárín ọjọ́ náà, àwọn àwọn aṣoju ní Komítí A gbéé ṣe ìròóṣù sí ilé-ẹ̀wé tí ó tún ṣe ìgbádúrà fún Ìlànì Àgbáyé ti Àgbàdọ̀gbẹ́ Àgbà Àgbàláwọ́ Àgbà (Global Health and Peace Initiative) (pẹ̀lú ìṣòro ní orúkọ kan láti “Global Health for Peace Initiative”), àti fún láti ní àkọ́kò-àkọ́kò ròpẹ́ nípa ìṣọ̀σό fún ìdágbádò ilé-ẹ̀wé náà. Ní àkọ́kó gbogbo, bí ó ṣe patọ̀, àwọn aṣoju gbéé ṣe àwọn azaaju àti àwọn ìwífúnrún ti àwọn komítí méjì, àti gbéé ṣe àwọn ìwétútù wọn. Èyí pẹ̀lú ìfọwọ́ṣẹ̀ẹ́ tí àwọn ìgbàgbé-ọ̀gá 2024-25, àti ìdílé 20% ní àwọn ìgbàgbé-ọ̀gá tí ó ti ṣe àfiànfẹ́. Àwọn alàárìn komítí àti àwọn aṣoju láti àwọn agbègbẹ́ mejeéńpẹ̀ẹ́ sọ́rọ̀ láti mo ìdíje àti ìṣọ̀σό ti ìgbàgbègbé yìí.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"rehabilitation"**: Translated as "Imọ̀-ẹnu àti itọ́pá" (Healing and Restoration). This captures the dual aspect of physical and mental recovery.
*   **"WHO’s work"**: Translated as "Iṣẹ́ Ọ̀jọ̀gbẹ́ Àgbà (WHO)" - Keeping the acronym WHO in parentheses for clarity.
*   **"Member States"**: Translated as "àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tí ó jẹ́ àwọn ọmọ-ọ̀wá" (countries that are members).
*   **"roadmap"**: Translated as "ilé-ẹ̀wé" (plan/blueprint).
*   **"Global Health and Peace Initiative"**:  The name is kept as close as possible to the original, with a note about the slight name change.
*   **"resolutions and decisions"**: Translated as "àwọn azaaju àti àwọn ìwífúnrún" (agreements and decisions).
*   **"assessed contributions"**: Translated as "àwọn ìgbàgbé-ọ̀gá tí ó ti ṣe àfiànfẹ́" (contributions that have been confirmed/assessed).
*   **"committee chairs"**: Translated as "Àwọn alàárìn komítí" (Heads of the committees).
*   **"two regions"**: Translated as "àwọn agbègbẹ́ mejeéńpẹ̀ẹ́" (two regions).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  This translation is based on the provided text.  A deeper understanding of the specific context (e.g., the type of assembly, the audience) could lead to further refinements.
*   **Terminology Consistency:**  For ongoing translation projects, it's crucial to establish a glossary of terms to ensure consistency.
*   **Review by Native Speakers:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Yoruba speaker with expertise in the subject matter to ensure accuracy and naturalness.
*   **Dialectal Variations:** Yoruba has different dialects. This translation aims for a generally understandable form, but awareness of the target audience's dialect is important.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: In his closing remarks, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO’s Director-General, noted that “the increase in assessed contributions and the investment round are historic and a huge milestone.” He spoke about the year ahead, with high-level meetings on universal health coverage, tuberculosis and pandemic preparedness and response at this year’s UN General Assembly. He pointed to the continuing negotiations on the pandemic accord and amendments to the International Health Regulations as unprecedented—“generational”—opportunities to learn from the mistakes of the COVID-19 pandemic and ensure they are not repeated. Delegates also had the chance to hear from former WHO staff Gwen Carnelley, who turned 100 this year, who began working with WHO in 1949, just a year after WHO was founded 75 years ago. Video of Closing of the 76th World Health Assembly.
translations.reference: Nínú ọ̀rọ̀ ìparí rẹ̀, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ WHO, sọ wí pé "àlékún nínú owó àjọ tí wọ́n yẹ̀wò àti owó ìdókówò jẹ́ mánigbàgbé àti àṣeyọrí tó tóbi." Ó sọ̀rọ̀ nípa ọdún tó ń bọ̀ níwájú, pẹ̀lú àwọn ìpàdé onípele-gíga lórí kíkárí ìlera lágbàyé, ikọ́-ife àti ìmúrasílẹ̀ àti ìdáhùn sí àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn níbi Àpéjọ Àpapọ̀ UN fún ti ọdún yìí. Ó tọ́ka sí ìtẹ̀síwájú ìdúnàdúrà lórí ìṣọ̀kan àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn àti àtúnṣe sí Àwọn Òfin Ìlera Ilẹ̀ Òkèèrè gẹ́gẹ́ bí nǹkan tí wọn ò lérò - "ìran-díran"-àwọn ànfààní láti kọ́ ẹ̀kọ́ látara àwọn àṣìṣe àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn COVID-19 àti láti rí i dájú pé wọn ò tún wáyé mọ́. Àwọn aṣojú ìjọba tún ní ànfààní láti gbọ́ láti ọ̀dọ òṣìṣẹ́ WHO tẹ́lẹ̀ rí Gwen Carnelley, tí ó pé 100 ní ọdún yìí, tí ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ṣiṣẹ́ pẹ̀lú WHO ní ọdún 1949, ọdún kan péré lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n dá WHO sílẹ̀ ní ọdún 75 sẹ́yìn. Fónrán amóhùnmáwòrán fún Ìparí Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé 76.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document, aiming for accuracy and natural flow. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Ní ìgbà tó fi bẹrẹ opin ìpilẹ̀rọ̀ rẹ̀, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Alákòkò Àgbàgbẹ́ Ọ̀jọ̀gbẹ́ Dídòjú Àgbáyé (WHO), sọ pé “ìdíje tí ó ní ìfọwọ́sọ̀wọ́ tí ó pọ̀, àti ìgbàgbé ayé ìdahun ìdahun jẹ́ àwọn iṣẹ́ àgbágbẹ́ àti àfiwéwé tó tóbi.” Ó ní ìroyè nínú ọdún tó wà lẹ́yìn, pẹ̀lú awọn igbimọ̀ àgbágbẹ́ nípa àwọn ìforúkọsọ̀ àgbáyé, àwọn àmùpatá àti ìṣàgbán àgbáyé, àti ìdahun èyìn àgbáyé níbi Ajumọṣẹ́ Gégéẹ́ UN tí ó máa nì yí. Ó tọka sí àwọn ìgbàwọn ìdahun tí ó ń tànkálẹ́ nípa àwọn ajọ̀wọ́n ìlú àgbágbẹ́ àti àwọn atúnṣe si Àwọn Ofin Àgbáyé Ọ̀jọ̀gbẹ́ bí àwọn anfani àti àwọn anfani “ìlú àgbágbẹ́”—láti mọ́ lòóbìnrin àwọn ìpamọ̀ ti àìṣe COVID-19 àti de bèe wọn kò yá máa ń rírọ̀. Àwọn aṣaju máa ń ní àyàwọ́ láti gbọ́ àwọn ohun tó nṣe láti ọ̀jọ̀gbẹ́ WHO tí ó ti jẹ́ ọmọ ọdún 100 ni ọdún yìí, tí ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí iṣẹ́ pẹ̀lú WHO ní 1949, kan ọdún lẹ́yìn WHO tí ó ti kópon ní ọdún 75.  Fidio ti opin ti Ìgbimọ̀ Ọ̀jọ̀gbẹ́ Dídòjú Àgbáyé 76kọ̀ọ̀sù.

translations.source: Gwen Carnelley is introduced at 54:37 and speaks shortly afterwards. Starting tomorrow, the 153rd meeting of Executive Board begins, where the outcomes of the Assembly will be discussed, among other things.
translations.reference: Wọ́n ṣàfihàn Gwen Carnelley ní 54:37 ó sì sọ̀rọ̀ ní àkókò díẹ̀ lẹ́yin rẹ̀. Bẹ̀rẹ̀ láti ọ̀la, ìpàdé 153rd Ìgbìmọ̀ Alàkóso máa bẹ̀rẹ̀, níbi tí wọn ó ti jíròrò nípa àwọn àbájáde Àpéjọ náà, pẹ̀lú àwọn nǹkan mìíràn.

translations.source: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 29 May 2023 Landmark resolution for strengthening Indigenous Peoples’ health globally The World Health Assembly today approved an unprecedented resolution on the health of Indigenous Peoples, which requests the Director-General to develop a global action plan for the health of indigenous peoples and to present it to the Seventy-ninth World Health Assembly in 2026. Indigenous Peoples, although representing diverse population groups and communities, in general have considerably lower life expectancy than non-indigenous populations. They also have a higher prevalence of many diseases and adverse health conditions, including diabetes, maternal and infant mortality and malnutrition.
translations.reference: Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé kẹrìn-dín-lọ́gọ́rin - Ìròyìn ojoojúmọ́: 29 oṣù Èbìbí ọdún 2023 Ìpinnu tó lápẹrẹ fún ìrólágbára ìlera àwọn ọmọ ìlú káàkiri àgbayé Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé lónì ti buwọ́lù ìpinnu àìròtẹ́lẹ̀ lórí ìlera àwọn ọmọ ìlú, tí ó bèèrè fún kí Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ ó ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ètò amúṣẹ́ṣe àgbáyé fún ìlera àwọn ọmọ ìlú kí ó sì ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ rẹ̀ níbi Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé kọkàn-dín-lọ́gọ́rin ní ọdún 2026. Àwọn Ọmọ Ìlú, bó tilẹ̀ jẹ́ wí pé wọ́n jẹ́ oríṣiríṣi ìran ènìyàn àti àwùjọ, ní àpapọ̀ wọ́n ní ìṣẹ̀mí tó kéré nínú èrò ju àwọn ènìyàn tí kìí ṣe ọmọ ìlú lọ. Wọ́n tún ní ọ̀nkà àpapọ ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ àìsan tó pọ̀ fún ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ààrùn àti ipò ìlera tí ò dára, tó fi mọ́ àtọ̀gbẹ, ikú ìyá àti ọmọ àti àìtó èròjà purotéénì.

Lẹ́gbẹ́ Àgbà Àgbà Ọ̀run Láàárín Àgbáyé tí ó fòwọ́ máa ba àmúùyẹ́wò ìlànìtàn tí ó jẹ́ ìpẹ́rẹ́rẹ́ tó ní ìbánisòrò fún ilera àwọn ènìyàn tí ó jẹ́ abíléédé, tí ó ní àfiànníbínú láti múlẹ̀ ètò iṣẹ́ àgbélébù fún ilera àwọn ènìyàn tí ó jẹ́ abíléédé, àti pe yóò fi ifọwọ́sán sẹ́ láti fi ifàranṣẹ́ ètò náà fún Lẹ́gbẹ́ Àgbà Àgbà Ọ̀run Láàárín Àgbáyé Kẹ́ẹ̀sán-ìn-ọ̀sún ní ọdún 2026. Àwọn ènìyàn tí ó jẹ́ abíléédé, botí ó ṣe àwọn ẹgbẹ́ àwọn ènìyàn àti àwọn obìnrin tí ó yatọ̀, nípari ní àgbágbé àwọn ọ̀run tí ó kéré jùbọ̀ lọ́wọ́ àwọn ènìyàn tí kò sí abíléédé. Wọ́n tun ní ìfaradà tó pípẹ́ jùbọ̀ fún àwọn àwọn ìwọn ọrọ̀ àti àwọn ipò ilera tí ó burú, pẹlú àǹgánjú, ìgbà àgbàyèwò àti ọmọdé, àti àṣù àwọn ènìyàn.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

translations.source: The Assembly requested the action plan be developed in consultation with Indigenous Peoples; that WHO provide support to Member States, upon request, for improving indigenous health; and that the improvement of Indigenous Peoples’ health be included in the development of the Fourteenth WHO General Programme of Work. In the same resolution, the Health Assembly urged Member States to, among other tasks, develop knowledge about the health situation of Indigenous Peoples, with their free, prior and informed consent; develop, fund and implement national health plans, strategies or other measures for Indigenous Peoples;
translations.reference: Àpéjọ náà pè fún wí pé kí ètò amúṣẹ́ṣe náà jẹ́ ṣíṣe ní ìfọ̀rọ̀wérọ̀ pẹ̀lú àwọn Ọmọ Ìlú; pé kí WHO ó pèsè àtìlẹ́yìn fún Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́, tí wọ́n bá bèèrè fún-un, fún àyípadà ìlera ìbílẹ̀; àti pé kí àyípadà ìlera àwọn Ọmọ Ìlú di àfikún sínú àgbékalẹ̀ Ètò Iṣẹ́ Àpapọ WHO Kẹrìnlá. Nínú ìpinnu kan náà, Àpéjọ Ìlera náà rọ Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ láti, pẹ̀lú àwọn iṣẹ́ yòókù, ní òye nípa ipò ìlera Àwọn Ọmọ Ìlú, pẹ̀lú ìfọwọ́sí ọ̀fẹ́, ṣíwájú àti ìfitónilétí wọn; ṣe àgbékalẹ̀, owó kí wọ́n sì ṣàmúlò àwọn ètò ìlera orílẹ̀-èdè, àwọn ìlànà-iṣẹ́ tàbí àwọn ètò mìíràn fún Àwọn Ọmọ Ìlú;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided text, aiming for accuracy and natural flow. I've included explanations of some choices afterward to clarify the reasoning.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Irukọ igbeke naa ni a ti gbero pe a ṣe ayẹwo ni ibamu si awọn eniyan ilẹ-iwosan; pe WHO pese atilẹyin si Awọn Ipinle Ojo, lẹhin atilẹyin, fun ni idagba ni ilera awọn eniyan ilẹ-iwosan; ati pe idagba ni ilera awọn eniyan ilẹ-iwosan ni a gbe si ninu itumọ iṣiro Igbesi Ayé WHO ni ọrọkọ Keje. Ninu aṣọtakun kanna, Irukọ igbeke ti gbero pe Awọn Ipinle Ojo yẹ ki, laarin awọn iṣẹ́ miiran, fi oju wo iṣiro ilera ti awọn eniyan ilẹ-iwosan, pẹlu itọsọna, iṣaro, ati idahun tiworo wọn; ṣiṣẹda, idahun, ati iduroṣinú awọn alarinrin ilera fun awọn eniyan ilẹ-iwosan;

**Explanation of Choices & Key Terms:**

*   **Assembly:** *Irukọ igbeke* - This translates to "the name of the assembly" or "the assembly's designation." It's a common way to refer to a formal body like the Health Assembly.
*   **Action Plan:** *Ayẹwo* - This means "plan" or "assessment." In this context, it refers to the plan of action.
*   **Indigenous Peoples:** *Awọn eniyan ilẹ-iwosan* - This is the standard Yoruba translation for "Indigenous Peoples." *Ilẹ-iwosan* literally means "land of healing" or "land of medicine," conveying the connection to traditional knowledge and practices.
*   **WHO:**  WHO is kept as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym.
*   **Member States:** *Awọn Ipinle Ojo* - "Ojo" means "member" or "state."
*   **Upon request:** *Lẹhin atilẹyin* - "Lẹhin" means "after" and "atilẹyin" means "approval" or "request."
*   **Improving:** *Idagba* - This means "improvement" or "enhancement."
*   **Health:** *Ilera* - This is the standard Yoruba word for "health."
*   **Resolution:** *Aṣọtakun* - This means "resolution" or "decision."
*   **Urged:** *Ti gbero* - This means "has decided" or "has urged."
*   **Develop knowledge:** *Fi oju wo iṣiro* - This translates to "look at the assessment" or "examine the knowledge."
*   **Free, prior and informed consent:** *Itọsọna, iṣaro, ati idahun tiworo* - This is a crucial phrase.
    *   *Itọsọna* - Free
    *   *Iṣaro* - Prior
    *   *Idahun tiworo* - Informed consent (literally, "answer with wisdom/knowledge")
*   **National health plans:** *Alarinrin ilera* - This means "health plans" or "health strategies."
*   **Fourteenth WHO General Programme of Work:** *Igbesi Ayé WHO ni ọrọkọ Keje* - "Igbesi Ayé" means "programme of work" and "ọrọkọ Keje" means "Fourteenth."

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If this document is part of a larger series, consistency in terminology is vital.
*   **Community Review:** Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by native Yoruba speakers, especially those familiar with health and Indigenous Peoples' issues, to ensure accuracy and cultural appropriateness.

translations.source: encourage the attraction, training, recruitment and retention of Indigenous Peoples as health workers taking into account the traditional knowledge and practices. Related document: A76/A/CONF./1 - Health of Indigenous Peoples First-ever resolution to accelerate action on drowning preventionThe delegates at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly agreed a resolution to accelerate action on global drowning prevention. The resolution requests Member States to assess their national drowning situation and to develop and implement multisectoral drowning prevention programmes. Drowning causes 236 000 deaths every year.
translations.reference: ṣe ìwúrí fún ìfanimọ́ra, ìdánilẹ́kọ̀ọ́, ìgbani síṣẹ́ àti gbígba àwọn Ọmọ Ìlú gẹ́gẹ́ bí òṣìṣẹ́ ìlera pẹ̀lú ìbojú wo ìmọ̀ ìbílẹ̀ àti àsà. Àwọn ìwé to fara jọ ọ́: A76/A/CONF./1 - Ìlera Àwọn Ọmọ Ìlú Ìpinnu àkọ́kọ́-irú-rẹ̀ láti mú iṣẹ́ yá lórí ìdènà rírì Àwọn aṣojú ìjọba tí wọ́n wà níbi Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé Kẹrìn-dín-lọ́gọ́rin fẹnukò lórí ìpinnu láti mú iṣẹ́ yá lórí ìdènà rírì. Ìpinnu náà pè fún kí Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ ó ṣe àyẹ̀wò ipò rírì ní orílẹ̀-èdè wọn kí wọ́n sì ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ àti àmúlò àwọn ètò ìdènà rírì ọlọ́pọ̀-ìsọ̀rí. Rírì ń fa ikú 236 000 ní ọdọọdún.

translations.source: It is a leading global cause of injury-related child deaths. Over the past decade, 2.5 million people died from drowning, and over 90% of those occurred in low- and middle-income countries. At the invitation of the United Nations (UN) General Assembly, WHO will coordinate actions within the UN system on drowning prevention and facilitate the observance of World Drowning Prevention Day on 25 July each year. WHO will also set up a Global Alliance for Drowning Prevention with organizations of the UN system, international development partners and nongovernmental organizations. To better understand the true burden and impact of drowning, the resolution further requests WHO to prepare a global status report on drowning prevention.
translations.reference: Ó jẹ́ okùnfà ikú àwọn ọmọdé ajẹmọ́-ìfarapa tó ń léwájú lágbayé. Láti bí ọdún mẹ́wàá sẹ́yìn, 2.5 mílíọ̀nù àwọn ènìyàn ló kú látara rírì, bẹ́ẹ̀ sì ni 90% lé nínú rẹ̀ ló ṣẹlẹ̀ ní àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tí ìpawówọlé wọn kéré àti agbede-méjì. Níbi ìfìwé peni Àpéjọ Àpapọ̀ Àjọ Ìṣọ̀kan Àgbáyé (UN), WHO máa darí àwọn iṣẹ́ láàrín ètò UN lórí ìdènà rírì yóò sì ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ṣíṣe Àyájọ́ Ọjọ́ Ìdènà Rírì Lágbàyé ní ọjọ́ 25 oṣù Agẹmọ ní ọdún kọ̀ọ̀kan. WHO máa tún ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ Ìdènà Rírì fún Ìsọ̀kan Àgbáyé pẹ̀lú àwọn ilé-iṣẹ́ ètò UN, àwọn alájọṣepọ̀ ìdagbàsókè ilẹ̀ òkèèrè àti àwọn ilé-iṣẹ́ tí kìí ṣe ti ìjọba. Láti túnbọ̀ ní òye ẹrù tòótọ́ àti ipa rírì, ìpinnu náà tún tẹ̀síwájú láti pè fún WHO láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ìjábọ̀ ipò àgbáyé lórí ìdènà rírì.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Yoruba translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I'll also include some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Jẹ́ òun kan lára àwọn idi idi ara ilẹ̀ tí o nìtójú lágbàágbè àgbáyé. Lórí àwọn ọdún mẹ́fà tí o kọjá, ènìyàn tó níbùrò lẹ́yìn tó ní àmúgbàtà jẹ́ ọkọ́kòkò ní 2.5 míliónì, àti pé jùbójú 90% ǹfún àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tí ó ní ìdánimọ̀ kekere àti àpótí. Pẹ̀lú iṣàgbéédé ti Àdàkọ́ Àgbàgbè ti Apapo Orílẹ̀-èdè (UN), WHO (Ọrọ̀-ọ̀gójú Àgbàgbè Ọrọ̀-ọ̀gójú) máa ń ṣíṣú àwọn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ láárín àwọn ọ̀tá ti Apapo Orílẹ̀-èdè nípa ìṣàgbéédé ìdánimọ̀ àmúgbàtà, àti fún ìdahun ìtọsọ̀tàn ọjọ́ 25 Ọgbónìgbàgbè, ní gbogbo ọdún. WHO tun máa ń ṣíṣe ìdàgbádògbè Àgbàgbè fún ìṣàgbéédé ìdánimọ̀ àmúgbàtà pẹ̀lú àwọn àwujọ ti Apapo Orílẹ̀-èdè, àwọn alábàwé àgbágbè àjàdé àti àwọn àwujọ tí kò ní ìjọba. Fún láti mọ̀ níbẹ̀ẹ̀ tóótó ohun tí ìdánimọ̀ àmúgbàtà ń ṣíṣẹ́ àti àfíkàn rẹ̀, ìpilẹ̀ yìí tun fẹ́ràn WHO láti ṣíṣẹ́ ìwè àpọ̀nná àgbàgbè nípa ìṣàgbéédé ìdánimọ̀ àmúgbàtà.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **Formal vs. Informal:** I've aimed for a relatively formal tone, suitable for a document like this.  More informal phrasing is possible, but less appropriate here.
*   **"WHO":**  I've kept "WHO" as is, as it's commonly used and recognized. I also included the full Yoruba translation of "World Health Organization" in parentheses for clarity.
*   **Percentages:** Yoruba uses a repeating syllable to represent percentages (e.g., 90% is "O-mo-mo-mo-mo").
*   **"Low- and Middle-Income Countries":**  This was translated as "àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tí ó ní ìdánimọ̀ kekere àti àpótí," which literally means "countries that have small income and poverty." This is a common and understandable way to express this concept in Yoruba.
*   **Word Order:** Yoruba word order can be flexible, but I've tried to maintain a natural flow while staying true to the original meaning.
*   **Cultural Context:**  I've considered the cultural context and tried to use language that would be easily understood by a Yoruba-speaking audience.

**Important Considerations:**

*   **Dialectal Variation:** Yoruba has several dialects. This translation aims for a generally understandable form, but some phrasing might be more common in certain regions than others.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Yoruba speaker with experience in technical or formal writing to ensure accuracy and appropriateness.

translations.source: Related document: EB152/CONF./2 - Accelerating action on global drowning prevention Read more about the resolution World Drowning Prevention Day 2023 Member States mobilize behind resolution to tackle chemicals, waste and pollution Member States welcomed the resolution addressing environmental determinants, including management of chemicals and waste. The efforts can help prevent up to one fifth of all suicide-related deaths from highly hazardous pesticides.
translations.reference: Àwọn ìwé tó fara jọ ọ́: EB152/CONF./2 - Mímú iṣẹ́ yá lórí ìdènà rírì lágbàyé Kà sí i nípa ìpinnu náà Àyájọ́ Ọjọ́ Ìdènà Rírì Lágbàyé 2023 Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ tò sẹ́yìn ìpinnu láti kojú kẹ́míkà, ìdọ̀tí àti bíba afẹ́fẹ́ jẹ́ Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ gba ìpinnu tó ń yanjú àwọn aṣokùnfà àwùjọ wọlé, tó fi mọ́ àmójútó àwọn kẹ́míkà àti ìdọ̀tí. Àwọn ìgbìyànjú náà lè ṣe ìrànwọ́ láti dènà tó ìdá kan nínú ìdá márùn-ún àwọn ikú ajẹmọ-ìgbẹ̀mí-ara-ẹni látara àwọn oògùn apa kòkòro tó léwu gidi gan-an.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document:

**Afiwewe Dokumenti ti a ni ibatan: EB152/CONF./2 - Ilọsiwaju iṣẹ́lẹ lori iwọn àìtó ti mimú Ra arinrinye diẹ sii nipa ijawọto World Drowning Prevention Day 2023 Awọn orilẹ-ede oniṣe ngbe ijinlẹ lẹ́nu ní ibi ijawọto lati ṣàfiẹ́ràn awọn kemikali, àpònnú àti ilù. Awọn orilẹ-ede oniṣe gbé ìwádàrà fún ìlànìpọ̀nmọ̀ẹ́jì nípa àwọn ìkọ̀ọ́ṣe àgbáyé, pẹ̀lú ìdarí àwọn kemikali àti àpònnú. Ìfọwọ́ṣe yí lè ràn án lọ́wọ́ láti ṣàfiẹ́ràn tí ó tó òun kan lójú àwon àmúgbàjá àwon ìgbàjá tó ní ibatan si ìtà bị́ ó ṣe yẹ kí a ṣe.**

**Explanation of some choices and considerations:**

*   **"Accelerating action"**: Translated as "Ilọsiwaju iṣẹ́lẹ" (Progress of action/effort).
*   **"Drowning Prevention"**: Translated as "iwọn àìtó ti mimú" (Prevention of drowning).
*   **"Member States mobilize"**: Translated as "Awọn orilẹ-ede oniṣe ngbe ijinlẹ lẹ́nu" (Member states take serious action/mobilize).
*   **"Tackle"**: Translated as "ṣàfiẹ́ràn" (to address/solve).
*   **"Environmental determinants"**: Translated as "àwọn ìkọ̀ọ́ṣe àgbáyé" (environmental factors).
*   **"Highly hazardous pesticides"**: Translated as "àwon ìgbàjá tó ní ibatan si ìtà" (pesticides that cause death).
*   **"Suicide-related deaths"**: Translated as "àwon ìgbàjá tó ní ibatan si ìtà" (deaths related to suicide).



I have aimed for a translation that is both accurate and reads naturally in Yoruba.  Let me know if you'd like any adjustments or have other documents you'd like me to translate!

translations.source: Member States were urged to bolster the implementation of existing WHO strategies including implementation of the WHO Chemicals Roadmap which outlines key roles of the health sector in implementing the Strategic Approach to International Chemicals Management. The resolution also encourages the Ministries of Health to engage in efforts to prepare proposals for an intergovernmental science policy panel and negotiations for a treaty to end plastic pollution. The resolution called upon the Director-General to undertake several actions, including: publishing a report on the health implications of chemicals, waste and pollution from a "One Health" perspective; updating the 2012 document on the State of the Science of Endocrine Disrupting Chemicals jointly with the United Nations Environment Programme; and
translations.reference: Wọ́n rọ Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ láti ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn ìṣàmúlò àwọn ìlànà-iṣẹ́ WHO tó ti wà tẹ́lẹ̀ tó fi mọ́ ìṣàmúlò ìlànà-iṣẹ́ kẹ́míkà WHO tí ó ṣe àlàkalẹ̀ àwọn ojúṣe pàtàkì fún ẹ̀ka ètò ìlera nínú ìṣàmúlò Ìlànà Ọlọ́gbọ́n fún Àmójútó Àwọn Kẹ́míkà Ilẹ̀ Òkèèrè. Ìpinnu náà tún ṣe ìwúrí fún àwọn Ilé-iṣẹ́ Ìjọba tó ń rí sí Ètò Ìlera láti máa ṣe àwọn ìgbìyànjú láti pèsè àdéhùn fún ìgbìmọ̀ adájọ́ lórí òfin sáyẹ́nsì ìjọba-sí-ìjọba àti ìdúnàdúrà fún àdéhùn láti fi òpin sí bíba afẹ́fẹ́ jẹ́ ike. Ìpinnu náà ké sí Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ náà láti gbé oríṣiríṣi ìgbésẹ̀, tó fi mọ́: ṣíṣe àtẹ̀jáde ìjábọ̀ lórí àwọn àkóbá kẹ́míkà fún ìlera, ìdọ̀tí àti bíba afẹ́fẹ́ jẹ́ láti ojú ìwòye "One Health"; ìsọdọ̀tun ìwé 2012 lórí Ipò Sáyẹnsì fún Àwọn Kẹ́míkà Tó ń ṣe ìdíwọ́ fún ẹsẹ́ ní àjọṣepọ̀ pẹ̀lú Ètò Agbègbè Àjọ Ìṣọ̀kan Àgbáyé; àti

translations.source: supporting countries in developing national or regional human biomonitoring programmes for chemicals of concern. Related document: A76/A/CONF./2 - The impact of chemicals, waste and pollution on human health WHO’s work on chemical safety and health Achieving well-being: a global frameworkMember States agreed to adopt the "Global framework for integrating well-being into public health utilizing a health promotion approach", which strives to enable all people to flourish and achieve their full physical and mental health potential throughout their lives and across generations. The Global framework recommends six key strategic directions that focus on: universal health coverage, equitable economies, protecting the planet, social protection systems, digital systems to enable health, and measuring and monitoring well-being.
translations.reference: ṣíṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè nínú àgbékalẹ̀ àwọn ètò aṣòfiwọ̀n-kẹ́míkà nínú ọmọnìyàn ní orílẹ̀-èdè tàbí ẹkùn agbègbè fún àwọn kẹ́míkà tí wọ́n jẹ́ ẹ̀dun ọkàn. Àwọn ìwé tó fara jọ ọ́: A76/A/CONF./2 - Ipa àwọn kẹ́míkà, ìdọ̀tí àti bíba afẹ́fẹ́ jẹ́ lórí ìlera ènìyàn Iṣẹ́ WHO lórí ààbò kẹ́míkà àti ìlera Ṣíṣe Àṣeyọrí ìgbáyégbádùn: ìlànà-iṣẹ́ àgbáyé Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ fẹnukò láti gba "ìlànà-iṣẹ́ àgbáyé fún ṣíṣe àfikún ìgbáyé-gbádùn sínú ìlera àwùjọ pẹ̀lú lílo ìlànà ṣíṣe ìpolongo ìlera", tó ń ṣe ìgbìyànjú láti jẹ́ kí gbogbo ènìyàn gbérí àti láti ṣe àṣeyọrí agbára ìlera ara àti ọpọlọ wọn ní kíkún ní gbogbo ọjọ́ ayé wọn àti láti ìran-díran. ìlànà-iṣẹ́ Àgbáyé náà dábàá àwọn ojú ọ̀nà ìlànà-iṣẹ́ pàtàkì mẹ́fà tó dá lórí: kíkárí ìlera lágbàyé, ọrọ̀-ajétó dọ́gba, dídáábò bo ayé, àwọn ètò ìdáàbò bo àwùjọ, àwọn ètò àkósódù láti fún ìlera láàyè, bákan náà ni ìgbéléwọ̀n àti àmójútó ìgbáyégbádùn.

translations.source: The framework proposes close collaboration with sectors outside the health sector to promote and protect health. It serves as a guide for all stakeholders to engage in a coherent and coordinated manner around a common purpose: promoting the health of people and planet in a sustainable and equitable manner. promoting the health of people and planet in a sustainable and equitable manner. The Assembly requests the Director-General to report on implementation of the global framework in 2024, 2026 and 2031. Related document: A76/7 Add.2 - Well-being and health promotion
translations.reference: ìlànà-iṣẹ́ náà dábàá àjọṣepọ̀ tó gúnmọ́ pẹ̀lú àwọn ẹ̀ka ní ìta ẹ̀ka ètò ìlera láti ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn àti láti dáàbò bo ètò ìlera. Ó ń ṣiṣẹ́ gẹ́gẹ́ bíi ìtọ́nisọ́nà fún gbogbo àwọn alẹ́nulọ́rọ̀ láti kópa ní ọ̀nà tó ní ìṣọ̀kan àti ètò yíká èròngbà àjọni: ṣíṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ìlera àwọn ènìyàn àti àgbáyé ní ọ̀nà alálòpẹ́ àti ìdọ́gba. ṣíṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ìlera àwọn ènìyàn àti àgbáyé ní ọ̀nà alálòpẹ́ àti ìdọ́gba. Àpéjọ náà bèèrè pé kí Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ náà jábọ̀ lórí ìṣàmúlò ìlànà-iṣẹ́ àgbáyé ní ọdún 2024, 2026 àti 2031. Àwọn ìwé tó fara jọ ọ́: A76/7 Add.2 - àtìlẹ́yìn ìgbáyé-gbádùn àti ìlera
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Eto-ẹri yí ṣí iṣọkan alabaṣepọ̀ pẹ̀lú àwọn ìtajà tí ó wà lẹ́nu àárọ̀gbẹ́ àjọ̀ṣepọ̀ àgbáyé tí ó máa ṣe ìtọ́pá àti àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ àgbáyé. Ìfi sí i pé èmi ìràforí kan fún gbogbo àwọn olùṣákùlérọ̀ láti ṣe ìṣọ̀ro ní ọ̀nà tí ó ṣókàn àti àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ nílé ohun tó jẹ́ àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ àgbáyé, ní àwọn ọ̀nà tí ó ṣànyanwà àti àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀. Ìfi sí i pé èmi ìràforí kan fún gbogbo àwọn olùṣákùlérọ̀ láti ṣe ìṣọ̀ro ní ọ̀nà tí ó ṣókàn àti àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ nílé ohun tó jẹ́ àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ àgbáyé, ní àwọn ọ̀nà tí ó ṣànyanwà àti àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀.  Ìjọ̀ Àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ ń bẹ́ àṣẹ láti fi àkọ́nìjí láti Ààlùkò-Génàràl nípa ìdàgbádò ti eto-ẹri gbogbo àgbáyé ní 2024, 2026, àti 2031. Àkọ́kọ̀ ìwé tó ní àpẹ́: A76/7 Ilé-ẹ̀kọ̀.2 - Àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ àti Àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ Àgbáyé.

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **"Framework"**: Translated as "Eto-ẹri" (meaning "framework" or "plan"). This is a common and appropriate term in Yoruba for a structured plan or system.
*   **"Close collaboration"**: Translated as "iṣọkan alabaṣepọ̀" (meaning "close cooperation" or "close partnership").
*   **"Outside the health sector"**: Translated as "lẹ́nu àárọ̀gbẹ́ àjọ̀ṣepọ̀ àgbáyé" (meaning "outside the circle of global health").
*   **"Promote and protect health"**: Translated as "sẹ̀ ìtọ́pá àti àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ àgbáyé" (meaning "to promote and protect global health").
*   **"Serves as a guide"**: Translated as "Ìfi sí i pé èmi ìràforí kan" (meaning "It serves as a guide").
*   **"Stakeholders"**: Translated as "àwọn olùṣákùlérọ̀" (meaning "those who have a stake" or "participants").
*   **"Coherent and coordinated manner"**: Translated as "ní ọ̀nà tí ó ṣókàn àti àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀" (meaning "in a clear and coordinated way").
*   **"Common purpose"**: Translated as "ohun tó jẹ́ àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀" (meaning "what is beneficial").
*   **"Sustainable and equitable manner"**: Translated as "ní àwọn ọ̀nà tí ó ṣànyanwà àti àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀" (meaning "in ways that are effective and beneficial").
*   **"The Assembly requests"**: Translated as "Ìjọ̀ Àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ ń bẹ́ àṣẹ" (meaning "The Assembly is requesting").
*   **"Director-General"**: Translated as "Ààlùkò-Génàràl" (a direct transliteration, as this is a common title).
*   **"Report on implementation"**: Translated as "nípa ìdàgbádò" (meaning "about the implementation").
*   **"Related document"**: Translated as "Àkọ́kọ̀ ìwé tó ní àpẹ́" (meaning "a document with a name").
*   **"Well-being and health promotion"**: Translated as "Àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ àti Àṣàwọ̀nírọ̀ Àgbáyé" (meaning "Well-being and global health promotion").

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If you have more information about how this document will be used, the translation can be further refined.
*   **Terminology Consistency:**  For ongoing projects, it's crucial to establish a glossary of terms to ensure consistency in translation.
*   **Review by Native Speakers:**  Ideally, a native Yoruba speaker should review the translation for accuracy, fluency, and cultural appropriateness.
*   **Transliteration vs. Translation:**  For some terms (like "Director-General"), transliteration (using Yoruba characters to represent the English word) is often the best approach, especially if there isn't a direct equivalent in Yoruba.

translations.source: New resolution to accelerate efforts on food micronutrient fortification The delegates approved a resolution on accelerating efforts to prevent micronutrient deficiencies through safe and effective food fortification. Deficiencies in vitamin and mineral status, particularly of folate, iron, vitamin A, and zinc, affect 50% of all preschool aged children and 67% of all women of reproductive age worldwide. Micronutrient deficiencies can have serious consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects.Large scale food fortification is part of the solution. By adding essential vitamins and minerals to staple foods and condiments, such as wheat and maize flours, rice, cooking oil and salt in accordance with national consumption patterns and deficiencies, countries can correct and further prevent a demonstrated micronutrient deficiency.
translations.reference: Àwọn ìpinnu tuntun láti mú kí ìgbìyànjú lórí ìrólágbára àwọn aṣara lóore inú oúnjẹ yá Àwọn aṣojú ìjọba náà fọwọ́sí ìpinnu kan lórí mímú kí àwọn ìgbìyànjú láti dènà àwọn àìtó aṣara lóore nípasẹ̀ ìrólágbára oúnjẹ tí ò léwu tó sì ń ṣiṣẹ́ ó yá. Àìtó iye fítáámì àti aṣara lóore, pàápàá jùlọ ti fólétì, áyọ́ọ́nù, Fítáámì A, àti zínkì, ń kóbá 50% gbogbo àwọn ọmọ tí ọjọ́ orí wọn wà lábẹ́ ilé-ẹ̀kọ́ àṣẹ̀ṣẹ̀ bẹ̀rẹ̀ àti 67% gbogbo àwọn obìnrin tí ọjọ́ orí wọn wà ní ipò ìbímọ kárí ayé. Àìtó èròjà aṣara lóore lè ní ìpadàbọ̀ tó lágbára, tó fi mọ́ wíwọ́ ọ̀pá ẹ̀yìn àti àwọn àlébù túúbù ìmọ̀lára mìíràn. Ìrólágbára oúnjẹ lórí òṣùwọ̀n-ńlá jẹ́ ọ̀kan lára ọ̀nà àbáyọ náà. Pẹ̀lú ṣíṣe àfikún àwọn fítáámì àti aṣara lóore tó ṣe pàtàkì sí àwọn oúnjẹ ojoojúmọ́ àti àwọn ìsebẹ̀, bíi àlìkámà àti àgbàdo ìyẹ̀fun, ìrẹsì, òróró ìsebẹ̀ àti iyọ̀ ní ìbámu pẹ̀lú bátánì àti lílò orílẹ̀-èdè àti àìtó, àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè lè ṣe àtúnṣe kí wọ́n sì tún tẹ̀síwájú ìdènà àìtó èròjà aṣara lóore tí wọ́n ṣàpèjúwe.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Yoruba translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I'll also include some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Iṣẹẹ́ tuntun fun apọju awọn ireti lori idagba awọn ohun didun ninu ounjẹ. Awọn aṣoju ti ṣàkoso iṣẹẹ́ kan lori idagba awọn ireti ti o ni itọju awọn àìníyere ohun didun nipa fifọ awọn ohun ti o mọnamọ ni ọjà pọ̀ pọ̀. Àìníyere àwọn wītámínù àti miníràlù, pataki àwọn tó ní àkóso pẹ̀lú folate, írọn, wītámínù A, àti zinc, ń ṣíṣe nípa 50% àwọn ọmọdé tí ó ń tànkálẹ́ àti 67% àwọn obìnrin tí ó ń ṣiṣe àwọn ọ̀run. Àìníyere ohun didun le ní àwọn ìlànì tí ó pọ̀, pẹ̀lú spina bifida àti àwọn àìníyere ohùn ọ̀nà.  Ìdágbà ounjẹ tó tóbi jù jùun ni apakan ti àfikúnrẹ́.  Nígbà tí a fa àwọn wītámínù àti miníràlù pàtàkù sí àwọn àkànsọ̀sọ̀ ounjẹ àti àwọn iyipada, bẹ́ẹ̀ sí iṣẹpọ̀ àti ìdáná, gẹ́gẹ́ bí àwọn àkàsákọ̀sọ̀ àti àwọn àkànsọ̀sọ̀ tí ó ní ìlù nígbà tí a nímọ̀, orílẹ̀-èdè le ṣàkóso àti ṣe ìṣọ̀ọṣọ̀ nígbá ti àìníyere ohun didun tí a ti fi àmùù.

translations.source: The resolution urges Member States to develop policies on food fortification with micronutrients and/or supplementation, and to consider ways of strengthening financing and monitoring mechanisms. The resolution was agreed under the umbrella of the United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition (2016-2025) report. Related document: EB152/CONF./5 Accelerating efforts for preventing micronutrient deficiencies and their consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects, through safe and effective food fortification Related links
translations.reference: Ìpinnu náà rọ Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ àwọn òfin lórí ìrólágbára oúnjẹ pẹ̀lú àwọn èròjà aṣara lóore àti/tàbí àfikún, láti láti gbèrò àwọn ọ̀nà láti ró ìpèsè owó lágbára àti àwọn ètò ìṣàmójútó. Wọ́n fẹnukò lórí ìpinnu náà lábẹ́ àbùradà ìjábọ̀ Ọdún Mẹ́wàá Iṣẹ́ lórí Èròjà Aṣara lóore Àjọ Ìṣọ̀kan Àgbáyé (2016-2025). Ìwé tó fara jọ ọ́: EB152/CONF./5 Mímú kí àwọn ìgbìyànjú fún ìdènà àìtó èròjà aṣara lóore àti ìpadàbọ̀ wọn ó yá, tó fi mọ́ wíwọ́ ọ̀pá ẹ̀yìn àti àwọn àlébù túúbù ìmọ̀lára mìíràn, nípasẹ̀ ìrólágbára oúnjẹ tí ò léwu tó sì ṣe é lò Àwọn ojú-òpótó fara jọ ọ́

Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy within the constraints of the language. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Irukọ akọni yí ò pé oríṣẹ̀ pataki gbọ́dọ̀ ṣíṣẹ́ pọ́ǹdìrì nípa ìgbòru àwọn àkànṣe àti/bàbá àṣànjú, àti pe gbọ́dọ̀ ń yàwú ọ́wọ́̀ bí ó ṣe le ṣe àgbẹ́rùngba àfiwéẹ́ àti ìtanipa. Irukọ akọni yìí fẹ́ jọ̀wọ́ sí ìbàjẹ́ Àwọn Ìgbà Ọ̀sùn ti Apapo Agbọ́gbọ́ ti Apapo Orílẹ̀-èdè (2016-2025). Àkọ́kò ilé-ìwé tí a ní ìfọ̀rọ̀wẹ́ sí: EB152/CONF./5 – Gbona ìjà sí ìfọwọ́ṣẹ̀ ìgbòru àwọn àkànṣe àti àwọn ìlànì wọn, pẹ̀lú ìwọn ìdán balú àti àwọn àìṣe tí ó ṣọ́fún ìgbà àti àwọn àìṣe mìíràn. Ẹ̀rọ̀ àdàgbódò tó ní àwọn àlátàsílẹ̀.

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **"Resolution"**: I translated this as "Irukọ akọni" which means "Name of a resolution" or "Title of a resolution." It's the most common and appropriate term in this context.
*   **"Member States"**: Translated as "oríṣẹ̀ pataki" which means "important countries" or "key states." This is a standard way to refer to member states in Yoruba.
*   **"Food fortification with micronutrients and/or supplementation"**: This was a tricky phrase. I opted for "ìgbòru àwọn àkànṣe àti/bàbá àṣànjú."  "Ìgbòru" means fortification. "Àwọn àkànṣe" means micronutrients. "Àbàbá àṣànjú" means supplementation. The "/bàbá" is used to indicate "and/or" as in the original.
*   **"Strengthening financing and monitoring mechanisms"**: Translated as "ń yàwú ọ́wọ̀ bí ó ṣe le ṣe àgbẹ́rùngba àfiwéẹ́ àti ìtanipa." This conveys the idea of improving or bolstering financial and monitoring systems.
*   **"United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition (2016-2025)"**: Translated as "Ìbàjẹ́ Àwọn Ìgbà Ọ̀sùn ti Apapo Agbọ́gbọ́ ti Apapo Orílẹ̀-èdè (2016-2025)." This is a direct translation, keeping the structure similar to the English.
*   **"Accelerating efforts"**: Translated as "Gbona ìjà" which means "intense struggle" or "accelerated effort."
*   **"Preventing micronutrient deficiencies and their consequences"**: Translated as "ìfọwọ́ṣẹ̀ ìgbòru àwọn àkànṣe àti àwọn ìlànì wọn." This means "preventing micronutrient deficiencies and their effects."
*   **"Spina bifida and other neural tube defects"**: Translated as "ìwọn ìdán balú àti àwọn àìṣe tí ó ṣọ́fún ìgbà àti àwọn àìṣe mìíràn." This is a descriptive translation, explaining the conditions.
*   **"Related links"**: Translated as "Ẹ̀rọ̀ àdàgbódò tó ní àwọn àlátàsílẹ̀." This means "Related documents that have links."

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

translations.source: Read more about the resolution WHO's work on food fortification Member States approve actions to counter substandard and falsified medicines On 27 May, the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly approved a resolution to conduct an independent review of the Member State Mechanism (MSM) for incidents, substandard and falsified medicines, reinforcing the Director-General’s January 2023 call for increased vigilance and action in this important area. The review will be initiated at the next MSM steering committee meeting later in 2023.
translations.reference: Kà sí i nípa ìpinnu náà Iṣẹ́ WHO lórí ìrólágbára oúnjẹ Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ fọwọ́sí ìgbésẹ̀ láti kojú àwọn oògùn tí kìí ṣe ojúlówó àti ayédèrú Ní 27 oṣù Èbìbí, Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé Kẹrìn-dín-lọ́gọ́rin fọwọ́sí ìpinnu kan láti ṣe àtúnyẹwò olómìnira Ètò Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ (MSM) fún àwọn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀, àwọn oògùn tí kìí ṣe ojúlówó àti ayédèrú, tí ó ń ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn ìpè oṣù Ṣẹẹrẹ ọdún 2023 Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ fún àlékún ìṣọ́ àti iṣẹ́ ní ààyè pàtàkì yìí. Àtúnyẹwò náà ni wọ́n máa ṣe níbi ìpàdé ìgbìmọ̀ àwọn adarí MSM tó ń bọ̀ ní àkókò kan ní ọdún 2023.

Ni ọjọ 27 Òwójú, Ile-Asọfin Ilú-ilẹ Gbogbo (World Health Assembly) ti o gbàlá ńlá ti ṣe igbeoye lati ṣe iṣayẹwo ti o jẹ́ ìwàpọ̀ ti Awọn Giṣẹ́ ti o jẹ́ Giṣẹ́ (Member State Mechanism - MSM) fun awọn iṣẹ́lẹ, awọn ohun ilọri ti o buru, ati awọn ohun ilọri ti o ṣàkóso. translations.source: The Assembly established the MSM in 2012 to tackle substandard and falsified medical products from a public health perspective and in a transparent and inclusive way. The goal of the MSM is to protect public health and promote access to affordable, safe, efficacious and quality medical products. Noting recent events of contaminated medicines found on various markets and related preventable deaths, Member States emphasized the support needed to improve their capacity to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products through access to appropriate technologies, including laboratory testing facilities, along with improved oversight on informal markets and advertising, and the online sale of medical products. Member States called for embracing and implementing track and trace technologies for market control and surveillance, as well as the need for formal collaboration by relevant local and global partners such as law enforcement and customs administrations. Member States were encouraged to align with conventions aimed at improvement of legislation and imposition of prohibitive sanctions for wrongful dealing in substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.reference: Àpéjọ náà ṣe ìdásílẹ̀ MSM ní ọdún 2012 láti kojú àwọn ohun èlò oògùn tí kìí ṣe ojúlówó àti ayédèrú ní ojú ìwòye ìlera àwùjọ àti ní ọ̀nà àkóyawọ́ àti aláfibọ̀. Èròngbà MSM ni láti dáàbò bo ìlera àwùjọ àti láti ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ànfààní sí àwọn ohun èlò oògùn tó rọjú, aláìléwu, tó múnádóko tó sì jẹ́ ojúlówó. Ìtọ́kasí ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ àwọn oògùn tó ti bàjẹ́ tí wọ́n rí ní oríṣiríṣi ọjà láìpẹ́ yìí àti àwọn tó fara jọ ikú tó ṣe é dènà, Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ tẹnu mọ́ àtìlẹ́yìn tí wọ́n nílò láti mú àyípadà bá ìkápá wọn láti dènà, ṣàwarí àti láti dáhùn sí àwọn ohun èlò oògùn tí kìí ṣe ojúlówó àti ayédèrú nípasẹ̀ ànfààní sí àwọn ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ tó yẹ, tó fi mọ́ àwọn ohun èlò àyẹ̀wò ààyè fún ìmọ̀ ìjìnlẹ̀, pẹ̀lú àtúnṣe ìfojúrénà lórí àwọn ọjà àti ìpolówó gbẹ̀fẹ́, àti títa àwọn ohun èlò oògùn lórí ẹ̀rọ ayélujára. Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ pè fún gbígbà àti ṣíṣe àmúlò àwọn ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ tọpa kí o sì wá káàkiri fún ìdarí àti ìtọpinpin ọjà bákan náà ni ìnílò àjọṣepọ̀ aláìgbẹ̀fẹ́ láti ọ̀dọ̀ oríṣiríṣi àwọn alájọṣepọ̀ ìbílẹ̀ àti lágbàyé bíi ìṣàkóso àwọn agbófinró àti àwọn oníbodè. Wọ́n rọ Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ láti wà ní ìdọ́gba pẹ̀lú àwọn àpéjọpọ̀ tí wọ́n gbèrò fún àyípadà òfin àti ṣíṣe àwọn kàn-án-pá ìṣelééwọ̀ fún àwọn ìṣòwò tí ò tọ́ nínú àwọn ohun èlò oògùn tí kìí ṣe ojúlówó àti ayédèrú.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document, aiming for accuracy and clarity while considering the nuances of the language. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Iṣọ̀ro Àṣàjú (Assembly) ni a ṣọ́ọ́ MSM ni ọdún 2012 lára fun ìdánilọ́ọ́pọ̀ àti àfiifẹ́ àwọn ọ́gbẹ́ èṣi àgbáṣe lọ́òkùnrin, lára ni òójú-ìwà àgbáṣe àti ìṣọ̀ọ́ṣọ̀. Àṣọ̀pọ̀ MSM ni a ṣe fún ìṣàlẹ́ èṣi àgbáṣe àti gbé ìrọ̀òmù sí iṣàpàá ìforúkọsọ̀, àìṣíṣí, àfiifẹ́, àti àṣà ìwàdé àgbáṣe.  Nígbà tí a fi mọ̀ọ́ àwọn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ tí a rí ní àwọn ọjà nígbà yìí nípa àwọn ọ́gbẹ́ èṣi tí a fi ìwàpààmọ̀, àti àwọn èkùn tó ṣe dé tèmí, Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè tí wọ́n jẹ́ ọmọ-ilé ní a ní ìtọsọ̀ọ́ fún ìṣànyan àti ìdahun tó ní láti ṣíṣàyẹ́, fìyàn, àti nípa àwọn ọ́gbẹ́ èṣi tí a fi ìwàpààmọ̀, lára ni ìdahun sí àwọn ohun elere tó tọ, pẹ̀lú àwọn àwọn ìtajà àwọn ìdáni, àti ìṣọ̀ọ́ṣọ̀ nípa àwọn ọjà ìlọòsọ̀ àti àwọn ìforúkọsọ̀ lórí ayé àpamọ̀. Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè ní a ní ìgbàgbé láti gbé ìlọ̀ọ́sọ̀ọ́ àti àṣilẹ́ àwọn ohun elere fún ìṣàkóso ọjà àti ìyanu, à bá àfi fún àwọn ohun tó ní láti ṣíṣàdúrà nípa ìṣọ̀ọ́ṣọ̀ pẹ̀lú àwọn alátàjò àti àwọn ìjọ̀sọ̀ọ́ tí wọ́n ní àfiifẹ́. Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè ní a gbàgbé láti ṣíṣíṣí pẹ̀lú àwọn ìfidírá tí a ṣe fún ìdágbádò àṣà ìjọ̀sọ̀ọ́ àti ìṣíṣí àwọn ìkọ̀ọ́ṣe tó ní láti ṣíṣàyẹ́ fún àwọn èṣi tí a fi ìwàpààmọ̀ àti àwọn èṣi tí a fi ìwàpààmọ̀.

**Explanation of Choices & Key Terms:**

*   **Iṣọ̀ro Àṣàjú (Assembly):** Direct translation of "Assembly."
*   **MSM:** I've kept "MSM" as is, assuming it's an established acronym within the context.  If a Yoruba equivalent exists and is commonly used, it could be substituted.
*   **ìdánilọ́ọ́pọ̀ àti àfiifẹ́ (substandard and falsified):**  This is a standard way to express "substandard and falsified" in Yoruba, combining "ìdánilọ́ọ́pọ̀" (substandard/inferior) and "àfiifẹ́" (counterfeit/fake).
*   **òójú-ìwà àgbáṣe (public health perspective):**  "òójú-ìwà" means perspective or viewpoint, and "àgbáṣe" refers to health.
*   **ìṣọ̀ọ́ṣọ̀ (transparent and inclusive):** "ìṣọ̀ọ́ṣọ̀" encompasses both transparency and inclusiveness.
*   **Àṣọ̀pọ̀ (goal):**  A common word for "goal" or "objective."
*   **gbé ìrọ̀òmù sí (promote access):**  This phrase conveys the idea of making something accessible.
*   **àìṣíṣí (efficacious):**  Means effective or efficacious.
*   **àṣà ìwàdé (quality):**  Refers to the quality of production or manufacture.
*   **àwọn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ tí a rí ní àwọn ọjà nígbà yìí (recent events of contaminated medicines found on various markets):** A more descriptive phrasing to ensure clarity.
*   **àwọn èkùn tó ṣe dé tèmí (related preventable deaths):**  "èkùn" means death, and the phrase conveys the idea of preventable deaths.
*   **Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè tí wọ́n jẹ́ ọmọ-ilé (Member States):** "Orílẹ̀-èdè" means state or country, and "ọmọ-ilé" means member or citizen.
*   **ìṣànyan àti ìdahun (prevent, detect and respond):**  Standard Yoruba terms for these actions.
*   **ohun elere (technologies):**  A general term for "technologies" or "tools."
*   **àwọn ìtajà àwọn ìdáni (laboratory testing facilities):**  "ìtajà" means facility or center, and "ìdáni" refers to laboratory.
*   **ìlọ̀ọ́sọ̀ọ́ àti àṣilẹ́ (embracing and implementing):** "ìlọ̀ọ́sọ̀ọ́" means to accept or embrace, and "àṣilẹ́" means to implement.
*   **ìṣàkóso ọjà àti ìyanu (market control and surveillance):** Standard terms for these concepts.
*   **alátàjò àti àwọn ìjọ̀sọ̀ọ́ (law enforcement and customs administrations):** Direct translations.
*   **ìfidírá (conventions):**  Means agreements or conventions.
*   **ìdágbádò àṣà ìjọ̀sọ̀ọ́ (improvement of legislation):**  "ídágbádò" means improvement, and "àṣà ìjọ̀sọ̀ọ́" refers to legislation.
*   **ìṣíṣí àwọn ìkọ̀ọ́ṣe (imposition of prohibitive sanctions):** "ìṣíṣí" means imposition, and "ìkọ̀ọ́ṣe" means sanctions.

**Important Considerations:**

*   **Context is Key:** This translation is based on the provided text.  The best translation always depends on the specific context and intended audience.
*   **Regional Variations:** Yoruba has regional dialects. This translation aims for a generally understandable form.
*   **Terminology:**  For highly technical documents, it's crucial to confirm that the chosen Yoruba terms are the standard and accepted ones within the relevant field.

translations.source: First ever resolution on behavioural sciences for better health is adopted Today Member States adopted the behavioural sciences for better health resolution with Member States showing broad consensus towards the need for integrating systematically behavioural science theory, methods and approaches across health topics and public health functions. The resolution urges Member States to acknowledge the role of behavioural science in achieving better health outcomes, to identify opportunities for increased use and, to establish functions and units for generating and translating evidence to inform policies and programmes. It also requests the Director-General to mainstream the use of behavioural science within the organization and to provide support to Member States through the development of guidance and the provision of technical assistance. Member States recognized the achievement of the Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative led by the secretariat, congratulated Malaysia, sponsor of the resolution, as well as the other 19 countries that joined as co-sponsors, and thanked the Director-General for the report.
translations.reference: Ìpinnu àkọ́kọ́-irú-rẹ̀ lórí sáyẹnsì ìhùwàsí fún ìlera tó dára ti di gbígbà wọlé Lónì Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ gba ìpinnu sáyẹ́nsì ìhùwàsí fún ìlera tó dára wọlé pẹ̀lú bí Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ ṣe ń ṣe àfihàn ìṣọ̀kan tó gbòòrò fún ìdí fún àfikún tíọ́rì sáyẹnsì ìhùwàsí pẹ̀lú ọgbọ́n, àwọn ìlànà àti ìgbésẹ̀ káàkiri àwọn àkòrí-ọ̀rọ̀ ìlera àti àwọn ojúṣe ìlera àwùjọ. Ìpinnu náà rọ Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ láti mọ rírì sáyẹ́nsì ìhùwàsí láti ṣe àṣeyọrí àbájáde ìlera tó dára, láti ṣàwárí àwọn ànfààní fún àlékún ìlò àti, láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ àwọn ojúṣe àti ẹ̀ka fún ìṣẹ̀dá àti ìtumọ̀ ẹ̀rí láti ṣe àwọn òfin àti ètò. Ó tún bèèrè pé kí Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ láti sọ lílo sáyẹ́nsì ìhùwàsí láàrín ilé-iṣẹ́ náà di nǹkan àti láti pèsè àtìlẹ́yìn fún Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ nípasẹ̀ àgbékalẹ̀ ìtọ́nisọ́nà àti ìpèsè ìrànlọ́wọ́ omímọ̀ ẹ̀rọ. Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ mọ rírì àṣeyọrí Ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ Sáyẹ́nsì Ìhùwàsí fún Ìlera Tó Dára tí Ilé-iṣẹ́ ìjọba léwájú rẹ̀, kí Malaysia kú orí-ire, onígbọ̀wọ́ ìpinnu náà, bákan náà ni àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè 19 tí ó kù tí wọ́n darapọ̀ gẹ́gẹ́ bíi onígbọ̀wọ́-àjọṣe, wọ́n sì dúpẹ́ lọ́wọ́ Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ fún ìjábọ̀ náà.

Loni, awọn orilẹ-ede oniṣe ti gba idahun lori awọn imọ-ọgbẹ awọhin fun ilera, pẹ̀lú awọn orilẹ-ede ti fihan adunirin ti o tobi nipa ohun ti o yẹ kí a ṣùgbọn awọn imọ-ọgbẹ awọhin, awọn ireti, ati awọn irọ̀rin lọ́wọ́ láàrin àwọn ìwọn ilera àti àwọn iṣẹ́ ilera gbogbo. Idahun yí jọmọ́ orilẹ-ede oniṣe láti mọ́ ìdàgbàsókè ti imọ-ọgbẹ awọhin nínú àwọn ìforúkọsílẹ̀ ilera tó dára, láti ràn án lọ́wọ́ àwọn aye fún ìdàgbàsókè àti lilo, àti láti ṣíṣe àwọn iṣẹ́ àti àwọn iyàwó fún ìdahun ìṣọ̀rọ̀ àti itumọ̀ fún àwọn ilana àti awọn eto-ẹkọ. O tun ròwé láti Ààrọ̀gbàjọ́-Génììrí láti ṣùgbọn ìdàgbàsókè ti imọ-ọgbẹ awọhin láàrin àwọn àjọ̀yédé àti láti ní àkọ́nìyàn fún àwọn orilẹ-ede oniṣe láàrin àwọn itọsọ̀sọ̀ àti àwọn ìrànṣọ̀ ọ̀tède. translations.source: They stressed the importance of building capacity in this area, particularly in regional offices, and of creating a repository of evidence and synergies between sectors, including with academia and the private sector. Related documents: EB152(23) - Behavioural sciences for better health EB152/25 - Behavioural sciences for better health, Report by the Director-General Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative The above items were discussed as part of the document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report by the Director-General.
translations.reference: Wọ́n tẹnu mọ́ pàtàkì ṣíṣe ìdásílẹ̀ ilé-iṣẹ́ ní agbègbè yìí, pàápàá jùlọ ní àwọn ọ́fíísì ẹkùn agbègbè, àti ìṣẹ̀dá àkójọpọ̀ ẹ̀rí àti àjọṣepọ̀ láàrín àwọn ẹ̀ka, tó fi mọ́ pẹ̀lú àwọn ilé-ẹ̀kọ́ àti ẹ̀ka aládàni. Ìwé tó fara jọ ọ́: EB152(23) -Sáyẹnsì ìhùwàsí fún ìlera tó dára EB152/25 - Sáyẹnsì Ìhùwàsí fún ìlera tó dára, Ìjábọ̀ láti ọwọ́ Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ Sáyẹnsì Ìhùwàsí fún Ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ Ìlera Tó Dára Àwọn ohun tó wà lókè yìí ni wọ́n jíròrò lé lórí gẹ́gẹ́ bí ọ̀kan lára àwọn ìwé A76/7 Rev.1 - Ìjábọ̀ ìṣọ̀kan láti ọwọ́ Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀.
