Okay, here's the Yoruba translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and natural flow. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Wọ́n títí ìdáná ti pàṣẹ́ àwọn àṣàyàn yìí, pàtá pupọ̀ níbi àwọn iṣọ̀ro agbègbè, àti níbi ìfọwọ́ṣà ìwé àfiànfẹ́ àti àṣà ìdahun láàrin àwọn apakan, pẹ̀lú àwọn ilẹ̀-ẹ̀kọ́ àti àwọn iṣọ̀ro ilẹ́. Àwọn ìwé tó ní àpẹẹrẹ́: EB152(23) - Àwọn imọ̀ ìdáná àti àṣà fún ilera tó dára EB152/25 - Àwọn imọ̀ ìdáná àti àṣà fún ilera tó dára, ìtójúmọ̀ láti Alàárìn-Ẹkun Àṣà Initiative fún Ilera tó dára. Àwọn ìtemí yìí fún ìròyìn díẹ̀ nínú àwọn àkọ́ṣẹ́ A76/7 Rev.1 - ìròyìn tí Alàárìn-Ẹkun ṣíṣe nípa ìpọ̀nnú.

**Notes on Translation Choices & Explanations:**

*   **"They stressed the importance..."**:  "Wọ́n títí ìdáná ti pàṣẹ́ àwọn àṣàyàn yìí" - This translates to "They emphasized/highlighted these points."  "Títí" means to emphasize or highlight. "Pàṣẹ́" means to order or instruct, but in this context, it conveys the idea of stressing something.
*   **"building capacity"**: "pàṣẹ́ àwọn àṣàyàn" -  This is a common way to express building capacity in Yoruba.
*   **"regional offices"**: "àwọn iṣọ̀ro agbègbè" -  Direct translation, "regional offices."
*   **"repository of evidence"**: "ìfọwọ́ṣà ìwé àfiànfẹ́" - This is a more formal way to say "repository of evidence." "Ìfọwọ́ṣà" means a collection or store. "Ìwé àfiànfẹ́" means evidence or proof.
*   **"synergies between sectors"**: "àṣà ìdahun láàrin àwọn apakan" - "Àṣà ìdahun" means synergy. "Láàrin àwọn apakan" means "between sectors."
*   **"academia"**: "àwọn ilẹ̀-ẹ̀kọ́" - This refers to the world of education or academia.
*   **"private sector"**: "àwọn iṣọ̀ro ilẹ́" - Direct translation, "private businesses/sector."
*   **"Related documents"**: "Àwọn ìwé tó ní àpẹẹrẹ́" - "Related documents" or "documents of example."
*   **"Behavioural sciences for better health"**: "Àwọn imọ̀ ìdáná àti àṣà fún ilera tó dára" -  "Imọ̀ ìdáná" means behavioral science. "Àṣà" means habits or customs. "Ilera tó dára" means better health.
*   **"Report by the Director-General"**: "ìtójúmọ̀ láti Alàárìn-Ẹkun" - "Report from the Director-General." "Alàárìn-Ẹkun" is the Yoruba term for Director-General.
*   **"Consolidated report"**: "ìròyìn tí Alàárìn-Ẹkun ṣíṣe nípa ìpọ̀nnú" - "Report that the Director-General made about the decision."

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Yoruba, like many low-resource languages, can have multiple ways to express the same idea. The best translation depends heavily on the specific context.
*   **Formal vs. Informal:** I've aimed for a relatively formal tone, suitable for a document.  A more informal translation would use different vocabulary and sentence structures.
*   **Terminology:**  Technical terms (like "behavioral sciences") can be challenging. I've used the most common and understandable equivalents, but further refinement might be needed based on the specific audience.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  It's *always* best to have a native Yoruba speaker review the translation for accuracy and naturalness.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate or if you'd like me to refine this one further.

translations.source: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 27 May 2023 First global strategy on infection prevention and controlThe World Health Assembly agreed today on the first-ever global strategy on infection prevention and control (IPC), which builds on almost two decades of efforts led by WHO and partners. The strategy provides Member States with strategic directions to substantially reduce the ongoing risk of health care-associated infections (HAIs), including those that exhibit antimicrobial resistance. HAIs are among the most frequent adverse events occurring in the context of health service delivery. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent major disease outbreaks such as Ebola virus disease, the Middle East respiratory syndrome and the Sudan virus disease have clearly exposed the existing gaps in IPC programmes in all countries.
translations.reference: Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé kẹrìn-dín-lọ́gọ́rin - Ìròyìn ojoojúmọ́: 27 oṣù Èbìbí ọdún 2023 ìlànà-iṣẹ́ àgbáyé àkọ́kọ́ lórí ìdíwọ́ àti ìdènà àkóràn Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé ti fẹnukò lónì lórí ìlànà-iṣẹ́ àgbéṣe lórí ìdíwọ́ àti ìdènà àkóràn (IPC) tó jẹ́ àkọ́kọ́-irú-rẹ̀, tí ó gùn lé àwọn ìgbìyànjú bíi ògún ọdún tí WHO àti àwọn alájọṣepọ̀ rẹ̀ léwájú rẹ̀. ìlànà-iṣẹ́ náà pèsè àwọn ọ̀nà ọlọ́gbọ́n fún Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ láti mú àdínkù alálòpẹ́ bá ewu àwọn àkóràn ajẹmọ́-ìlera (HAIs) tó ń lọ lọ́wọ́, tó fi mọ́ àwọn tí wọ́n ń ṣàfihàn ẹ̀yàwuuru jáàgùn. àwọn HAI wà lára àwọn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ àìfẹ́ gbogbo ìgbà tó pọ̀ jùlọ tó ń wáyé ní ọ̀gangan ipò ìpèsè ètò ìlera. Àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn COVID-19 àti àwọn àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn ńlá láìpẹ́ yìí bíi ààrùn kòkòrò Ẹ̀bọ́là, àìsàn èémí Àárín Ìlà-oòrùn àti kòkòrò ààrùn Sudan ti tú àṣírí àlàfo tó wà nínú ètò IPC ní gbogbo àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè kedere.

Okay, here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I'll also include some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Ìtọsọ̀tàn:**

Ọjọ́ Kẹ́ẹ̀dógún ti Àṣàgbọ̀ngbon Ilẹ̀-Àgbègbè Ọ̀run (World Health Assembly) – Imudojú Ọjọ́ọ́: 27 Òṣù Ẹrù, 2023. Àkọ́ṣẹ́ Ìlú-nyà fún ìdágbádògbèdé àti ìṣàgbánrọ̀pọ̀.

Àṣàgbọ̀ngbon Ilẹ̀-Àgbègbè Ọ̀run baje idagba loni lori àkọ́ṣẹ́ Ìlú-nyà fún ìdágbádọ̀gbèdé àti ìṣàgbánrọ̀pọ̀ (IPC) àkọkọ́ nígbà gbogbo, eyí ti o da lori ìgbà kan tó bẹ́ẹ̀ tó ọdún mẹ́fà tí WHO àti awọn alaba mo ṣe. Àkọ́ṣẹ́ náà nífẹ̀ẹ́ awọn orilẹ̀-èdè tí o jẹ́ àwọn ọmọ-ilé-ìwé pẹ̀lú àwọn itọsọ̀tàn àjọdé tí ó le pọ̀ níbẹ̀ẹ̀ sí ìdágbádògbèdé tó tó ọpọ̀lọ̀pọ̀ ti awọn ìwọn ọ̀rọ̀ àgbágbé tí ó ní ìṣàgbánrọ̀pọ̀. Awọn ìwọn ọ̀rọ̀ àgbágbé wọ̀nyí ni àwọn iṣẹ́ àìsán tí ó jù wọ́n tó ní àgbágbé àwọn ìwà àgbẹ̀wògbé.

Àṣàgbọ̀ngbon COVID-19 àti àwọn ìpòjúde àgbágbé tó rọ̀un tóótó bí Ebola virus disease, Middle East respiratory syndrome, àti Sudan virus disease ti fihan lára àwọn ìkú-àìsán tí ó wà nínú àwọn ẹ̀sẹ́ ìwà àgbẹ̀wògbé ní gbogbo awọn orilẹ̀-èdè.

---

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **IPC (Infection Prevention and Control):** I've kept the acronym "IPC" as it's increasingly used internationally, even in Yoruba contexts. I've also provided the full translation in parentheses the first time it appears.
*   **"World Health Assembly":**  Translated as "Àṣàgbọ̀ngbon Ilẹ̀-Àgbègbè Ọ̀run" which is the standard Yoruba term.
*   **"Health care-associated infections (HAIs)":** Translated as "awọn ìwọn ọ̀rọ̀ àgbágbé tí ó ní ìṣàgbánrọ̀pọ̀." This is a more descriptive and accurate translation than a literal one.
*   **"Strategic directions":** Translated as "àwọn itọsọ̀tàn àjọdé" - meaning guiding principles or strategic pathways.
*   **"Exposed the existing gaps":** Translated as "ti fihan lára àwọn ìkú-àìsán" - meaning revealed the weaknesses or shortcomings.
*   **Word Choice:** I've tried to use vocabulary that is relatively common and understandable in contemporary Yoruba.
*   **Sentence Structure:** I've adjusted sentence structure where necessary to ensure the translation flows naturally in Yoruba.  Direct, word-for-word translations often sound awkward.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  This translation is based on the provided text.  If this document is part of a larger series or has a specific target audience, further adjustments might be needed.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Yoruba speaker, especially someone familiar with medical or public health terminology, to ensure accuracy and cultural appropriateness.
*   **Terminology Consistency:**  For ongoing translation projects, it's crucial to develop a glossary of terms to ensure consistency.

translations.source: The strategy sets a clear vision: by 2030, everyone accessing or providing health care is safe from associated infections. Its three key objectives are: to prevent infection in health care; act to ensure IPC programmes are in place and implemented; and coordinate IPC activities with other areas and sectors. The strategy is focused on any setting where health care is delivered, across the health system; it is based on the principle of clean and safe care as a fundamental component of the right to health, which is equity driven, and which should ensure accountability and sustainability. The global IPC strategy will be complemented by, and used in conjunction with, an associated global action plan and monitoring framework, that will be developed in 2023–2024. Related document EB152/9 - Draft global strategy on infection prevention and control, Executive summary Report by the Director-General
translations.reference: ìlànà-iṣẹ́ náà ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ àfojúsùn tó hàn: ní ọdún 2030, gbogbo ènìyàn tó ń lo tàbí pèsè ètò ìlera rí ìdáàbòbò kúrò níbi àwọn àkóràn tó so mọ́ ọ. Àwọn kókó èròngbà rẹ̀ mẹ́tẹ̀ẹ̀ta ni: láti dènà àkóràn nínú ètò ìlera; ṣiṣẹ́ láti rí i dájú pé àwọn ètò IPC wà ní ìkàlẹ̀ wọ́n sì ń ṣàmúlò rẹ̀; kí ó sì darí àwọn iṣẹ́ IPC pẹ̀lú àwọn agbègbè àti ẹ̀ka mìíràn. ìlànà-iṣẹ́ náà ni wọ́n fi sun èyíkéyìí ààyè tí wọ́n ti ń pèsè ètò ìlera, káàkiri ètò ìlera; ó dá lórí ìpìlẹ̀-òye ìtọ́jú tó mọ́ tí kò sì léwu gẹ́gẹ́ bí òpó ìpìlẹ̀ fún ẹ̀tọ́ sí ìlera, tí ìdọ́gba ń darí, tí ó sì gbọdọ̀ ṣe àrídájú ìṣirò àti alálòpẹ́. ìlànà-iṣẹ́ IPC lágbayé ni wọ́n máa ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún pẹ̀lú, tí wọn ó sì lò ó papọ̀ pẹ̀lú, ètò amúṣẹ́ṣe àgbáyé àti ìlànà-iṣẹ́ àmójútó tó ní nǹkan ṣe pẹ̀lú ẹ̀, tí wọn yóò ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ rẹ̀ ní ọdún 2023-2024. Ìwé tó fara jọ ọ́ EB152/9 - Àkọsílẹ ìlànà-iṣẹ́ àgbáyé lórí ìdíwọ́ àti ìdènà àkóràn, Ìjábọ̀ ìsọníṣókí Aláṣẹ láti ọwọ́ Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀
translations.hypothesis:

translations.source: WHO's work on infection prevention and control Historic resolution paves the way for strengthening rehabilitation in health systemsToday the World Health Assembly agreed a landmark resolution on strengthening rehabilitation in health systems. Rehabilitation services play a key role in ensuring the enjoyment of human rights including the highest attainable standard of physical and mental health. It also promotes sexual and reproductive health, and recognizes the right to work and the right to education. This landmark resolution aims to address the challenges in rehabilitation such as the need to: increase awareness of rehabilitation when setting health priorities and research agendas, allocating resources, promoting cooperation and enabling technology transfer;
translations.reference: Iṣẹ́ WHO lórí ìdíwọ́ àti ìdènà àkóràn Ìpinnu mánigbàgbé ṣínà fún ìrólágbára ìtúnirọ nínú ètò ìlera Lónì Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé fẹnukò lórí ìpinnu tó lápẹrẹ lórí ìrólágbára ìtúnirọ nínú ètò ìlera. Ìpèsè iṣẹ́ ìtúnirọ kó ipa pàtàkì láti rí i dájú pé ìgbádùn ẹ̀tọ́ ọmọnìyàn tó fi mọ́ ojúlówó ìlera ara àti ọpọlọ tó ga jùlọ tí ènìyàn lè ní. Ó tún ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ìlera ìbálòpọ̀ àti ìbímọ, ó sì mọ rírì ẹ̀tọ́ sí iṣẹ́ àti ẹ̀tọ́ sí ètò ẹ̀kọ́. Ìpinnu tó lápẹrẹ yìí gbèrò láti yanjú àwọn ìpèníjà nínú ìtúnirọ gẹ́gẹ́ bíi ìdí láti: mú àlékún bá ìpolongo ìtúnirọ nígbà tí wọ́n bá ń ṣe ètò àwọn kàn-án-pá ìlera àti èròngbà ìwádìí, pínpín ohun èlò, ṣíṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ìfọwọ́sowọ́pọ̀ àti gbígba ìfiránṣẹ́ ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ;

translations.source: ensure countries are better equipped to respond to the sudden increase in rehabilitation needs including assistive technology due to health emergencies; ensure persons in marginalized and vulnerable situations have access to affordable, quality and appropriate rehabilitation services including assistive technology; avoid high out-of-pocket costs for people to access rehabilitation services and assistive technology that can cause financial hardships; and address the current insufficient level of rehabilitation workforce to serve the needs of the population. The resolution lists a range of actions to be taken by the WHO Secretariat such as:
translations.reference: rí i dájú pé àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè ní ohun èlò tó dára láti dáhùn sí àlékún òjijì nínú ìnílò ìtúnirọ tó fi mọ́ ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ aṣèrànwọ́ nítorí àwọn pàjáwírì ìlera; rí i dájú pé àwọn ènìyàn tí wọ́n wà nípò ìdẹ́yẹsí tí wọ́n sì kángun séwu ní ànfààní sí ìpèsè iṣẹ́ ìtúnirọ tó rọjú, tó jẹ́ ojúlówó tó sì péye tó fi mọ́ àwọn ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ aṣèrànwọ́; sọ́ra fún ìnáwó àpò-ara-ẹni fún àwọn ènìyàn láti ní ànfààní sí ìpèsè iṣẹ́ ìtúnirọ àti ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ aṣèrànwọ́ tí ó lè fa ìnira owó; àti yanjú ìpele àìtó àwọn òṣìṣẹ́ ìtúnirọ lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́ láti ṣíṣe fún ìnílò àwọn ará ìlú. Ìpinnu yìí ṣe àkọsílẹ̀ ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ìgbésẹ̀ tí Ilé-iṣẹ́ Ìjọba WHO ní láti gbé bíi:

Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Riran, awọn orile-ede ni a ṣe pataki pe wọn ni ohun ti o jẹ apẹrẹ to lati ni idahun si idagba ti awọn iye idurosilẹ ti o nṣẹ, pẹlu awọn ohun elo ti o ran re, nitori awọn iṣoro ilu ti o ṣiṣe niyanju; riiran, awọn eniyan ti o wa ni ipo ailopinṣin ati ailẹnu ni a ṣe pataki pe wọn ni irọrun si awọn iye idurosilẹ ti o ni ogo, iwulo, ati eyiti o tọ, pẹlu awọn ohun elo ti o ran re; gbanigba awọn idiyele ti o ga julọ ti awọn eniyan ni aṣaaju ti o le mu awọn iṣoro owo ti o le pa apa ni awọn eniyan ti o n gbiyanju lati gba awọn iye idurosilẹ ati awọn ohun elo ti o ran re; ati ṣe iranlọwọ iye ti awọn alailẹni ti o nṣe idurosilẹ ti o kere lọ ti o nilo lati fi ranre awọn iye ti awọn eniyan. Aṣayàn yí fihan ìwọn ìgbàgbé àwọn ìforukọsitẹ ti a yà lẹnu lábẹ àwọn àkóso ti WHO, pẹlú:

**Explanation of Choices & Key Terms:**

*   **"Ensure"**: Translated as "Riran" (meaning "certainly," "assuredly," or "it is ensured"). This conveys the sense of making something happen.
*   **"Countries are better equipped"**: "Awọn orile-ede ni a ṣe pataki pe wọn ni ohun ti o jẹ apẹrẹ to" - This translates to "Countries are made important that they have what is a good example." It's a slightly more verbose way to express "better equipped" in Yoruba, emphasizing the need for them to *have* the necessary resources.
*   **"Respond to the sudden increase in rehabilitation needs"**: "Ni idahun si idagba ti awọn iye idurosilẹ ti o nṣẹ" -  "Idahun si" means "response to," "idagba" means "increase," and "awọn iye idurosilẹ" means "rehabilitation needs." "Ti o nṣẹ" means "sudden" or "emerging."
*   **"Assistive technology"**: "Awọn ohun elo ti o ran re" - This is a direct translation, "ohun elo" means "technology" and "ti o ran re" means "that helps."
*   **"Marginalized and vulnerable situations"**: "Ipo ailopinṣin ati ailẹnu" - "Ailopinṣin" means "marginalized" (lacking support or opportunity), and "ailẹnu" means "vulnerable."
*   **"Affordable, quality and appropriate"**: "Ti o ni ogo, iwulo, ati eyiti o tọ" - "Ogo" means "quality," "iwulo" means "affordable," and "eyiti o tọ" means "appropriate."
*   **"Out-of-pocket costs"**: "Awọn idiyele ti o ga julọ ti awọn eniyan ni aṣaaju" - This translates to "the highest costs that people pay in advance." It's a descriptive way to convey the meaning.
*   **"Financial hardships"**: "Awọn iṣoro owo ti o le pa apa ni awọn eniyan" - "Iṣoro owo" means "financial problems," and "ti o le pa apa ni awọn eniyan" means "that can cause harm to people."
*   **"Insufficient level of rehabilitation workforce"**: "Iye ti awọn alailẹni ti o nṣe idurosilẹ ti o kere lọ" - "Iye" means "level," "awọn alailẹni" means "professionals/workers," "ti o nṣe idurosilẹ" means "doing rehabilitation," and "ti o kere lọ" means "insufficient."
*   **"The resolution lists a range of actions"**: "Aṣayàn yí fihan ìwọn ìgbàgbé àwọn ìforukọsitẹ ti a yà lẹnu" - This is a more formal way of saying "The resolution shows a range of actions."
*   **"WHO Secretariat"**: "Àwọn àkóso ti WHO" - "Àwọn àkóso" means "secretariat" or "administration."

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  This translation is based on the provided text.  The best translation always depends on the specific context of use.

translations.source: publishing a baseline report by the end of 2026 with information on the capacity of Member States to respond to rehabilitation needs; developing targets and indicators for effective coverage of rehabilitation services by 2030; ensuring appropriate resources are allocated at WHO to support Member States in implementing technical guidance and resources; and supporting Member States to integrate rehabilitation and assistive technology in their emergency preparedness and response plan. The WHO Secretariat will report on progress in the implementation of this resolution to the Health Assembly in 2026, 2028 and 2030.
translations.reference: ṣíṣe àtẹ̀jáde ìjábọ̀ ní ìparí ọdún 2026 pẹ̀lú àlàyé lórí ìkápá Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ láti dáhùn sí àwọn ohun tí ìtúnirọ nílò; ṣíṣe àgbékalẹ̀ àwọn àfojúsùn àti atọ́ka fún kíkárí ìpèsè iṣẹ́ ìtúnirọ tó ṣe é lò ní ọdún 2023; ṣíṣe àrídájú pé wọ́n pèsè àwọn ohun èlò tó yẹ fún WHO láti ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ nínú ìṣàmúlò ìtọ́nisọ́nà ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ àti ohun èlò; àti ṣíṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ láti ṣe àfikún ìtúnirọ àti ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ aṣèrànwọ́ nínú ètò ìgbaradì àti ìdáhùn pàjáwírì wọn. Ilé-iṣẹ́ ìjọba WHO máa jábọ̀ lórí ìtẹ̀síwájú nínú ìṣàmúlò ìpinnu yìí fún Àpéjọ Ìlera ní ọdún 2026, 2028 àti 2030.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy within the constraints of the language. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Ni pataki, a gba pe a ṣe atilẹyin igilọ ti awọn idiyele ni opin odun 2026 pẹlu alaye nipa agbara awọn orilẹ-ede ti orilẹ-ede lati dapọ awọn iwulo atilẹba; ṣiṣe awọn idiiran ati awọn ọwọn fun iṣafihan to ṣoṣii ti awọn iṣẹ akanṣe nipasabẹ 2030; riiran pe a fi owo to ṣe pataki silẹ ni WHO lati ṣe atilẹyin fun awọn orilẹ-ede ni ṣiṣi awọn itọsọna to ṣe pataki ati awọn ohun elo; ati ni itọsọna ti awọn orilẹ-ede lati ṣapamosi atilẹba ati imọ-ẹrọ alamsẹ ni awọn alarinrin wọn ti atilẹba ati awọn ipo idahun. Ajo WHO ti o ṣe aṣoju yoo royi lori itẹwọ pupọ ni awọn ipilẹṣẹ ti aṣoju yii si Igbimọ Agbaye Ẹru ni 2026, 2028, ati 2030.

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **"publishing a baseline report"**: Translated as "ṣe atilẹyin igilọ ti awọn idiyele" (to create/establish a foundation of costs/figures).  "Baseline report" is a specific technical term.  I opted for a phrasing that conveys the idea of establishing a benchmark.
*   **"Member States"**: Translated as "awọn orilẹ-ede ti orilẹ-ede" (countries of the country). This is a standard way to refer to member states in Yoruba.
*   **"respond to rehabilitation needs"**: Translated as "dapo awọn iwulo atilẹba" (to address/handle initial needs).
*   **"developing targets and indicators"**: Translated as "ṣiṣe awọn idiiran ati awọn ọwọn" (creating targets and indicators).
*   **"effective coverage"**: Translated as "iṣafihan to ṣoṣii" (complete/thorough coverage).
*   **"ensuring appropriate resources are allocated"**: Translated as "riiran pe a fi owo to ṣe pataki silẹ" (ensuring that specific funds are allocated).
*   **"technical guidance and resources"**: Translated as "awọn itọsọna to ṣe pataki ati awọn ohun elo" (specific guidance and tools/resources).
*   **"emergency preparedness and response plan"**: Translated as "awọn alarinrin wọn ti atilẹba ati awọn ipo idahun" (their plans of initial response and answers).
*   **"WHO Secretariat"**: Translated as "Ajo WHO ti o ṣe aṣoju" (WHO Secretariat that is in charge).
*   **"Health Assembly"**: Translated as "Igbimọ Agbaye Ẹru" (World Health Assembly).
*   **"report on progress"**: Translated as "royi lori itẹwọ pupọ" (report on overall progress).
*   **"implementation of this resolution"**: Translated as "awọn ipilẹṣẹ ti aṣoju yii" (the actions of this resolution).

**Important Notes:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If this is part of a larger document, there might be preferred terminology already established.
*   **Yoruba is Dynamic:**  Yoruba, like any language, has regional variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable form.
*   **Technical Terminology:**  Some technical terms might not have direct equivalents in Yoruba. I've tried to use the most appropriate phrasing to convey the meaning.

*   **EB152/8, A76/7 Rev.1:** These codes are generally left as is, as they are standard identifiers.
*   **"Strengthening rehabilitation"**:  "Ilọpo si àgbesikä rehabilitasi" - This conveys the idea of improving or boosting rehabilitation. "Ilọpo" means improvement or strengthening. "Àgbesikä" means rehabilitation.
*   **"Health systems"**: "Eto ilu ilu" - This is a standard translation for health systems.
*   **"Report by the Director-General"**: "Iroyin ti Alakosi-Gbekele" -  "Alakosi-Gbekele" is the standard Yoruba term for Director-General.
*   **"Related link"**: "Apakan asọye" - This translates to "related aspect" or "related part."
*   **"WHO's work on rehabilitation"**: "Ọ̀naṣọ̀ WHO nipa rehabilitasi" - "Ọ̀naṣọ̀" means work or activities.
*   **"The items above were discussed as part of..."**: "Awọn ibi ti o tọka yii ti gbé jade ni agbaye ti iroyin..." - This is a more formal way of saying "were discussed within the context of the report."  "Gbé jade" means to bring out or discuss.
*   **"Consolidated report"**: "Iroyin ti o sopọ̀pọ̀" - "Sopọ̀pọ̀" means consolidated or combined.
*   **"Resolution on strengthening diagnostics capacity"**: "Ipamọye lori idagba agbara ifiwégbàdé àgbáyé" - "Ipamọye" means resolution. "Idagba agbara" means strengthening capacity. "Ifiwégbàdé àgbáyé" means diagnostics capacity.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context.  If you have more information about how this document will be used, the translation could be further refined.
*   **Terminology Consistency:**  For ongoing projects, it's crucial to establish a glossary of terms to ensure consistency across all translated documents.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, a native Yoruba speaker with experience in health or international organizations should review the translation for accuracy and naturalness.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate or if you'd like me to refine this one further.

translations.source: On 26 May Member States endorsed a resolution to strengthen diagnostics capacity in countres and to improve access to diagnostic services. The broad ranging resolution recognizes that diagnostic services are vital for the prevention, surveillance, diagnosis, case management, monitoring and treatment of communicable, noncommunicable, neglected tropical and rare diseases, injuries, and disabilities. Diagnostics allow for the precise identification of diseases, and therefore the timely initiation of the correct treatments for better health outcomes. The resolution considers the full spectrum of “diagnostics”, thus including both “in vitro” laboratory tests e.g. rapid diagnostic tests and polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and “non in vitro” diagnostics e.g. imaging or blood pressure measurement devices. It covers actions for research and development, manufacturing (including local production and technology transfer), regulation, selection and procurement, awareness, advocacy and addressing access barriers in general.
translations.reference: Ní 26 oṣù Èbìbí Àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ buwọ́lù ìpinnu kan láti ró ìkápá ìdámọ̀ àisàn ní àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè lágbára àti láti mú àyípadà bá ànfààní sí ìpèsè iṣẹ́ ìdámọ̀ àìsàn. Ìpinnu oríṣiríṣi tó gbòòrò náà mọ̀ wí pé ìpèsè iṣẹ́ ìdámọ̀ àìsàn ṣe pàtàkì fún ìdènà, ìtọpinpin , ìdámọ̀ àìsàn, àmójútó ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀, ṣíṣe àmójútó àti ìtọ́jú ààrùn tí kìí ràn, àwọn ààrùn ìgbóná àti èyí tó ṣọ̀nwọ́n tí wọ́n ti patì, ìfarapa, àti ìpèníjà ara. Ìdámọ̀ àìsàn fi ààyè gba ìdánimọ̀ àìsàn ní pàtó, fún ìdí èyí ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ àwọn ìtọ́jú tó péye fún àbájáde ìlera tó dára lásìkò. Ìpinnu náà ro gbogbo ìpìlẹ "ìdámọ̀ àisàn", torí náà a rí àpapọ̀ àwọn àyẹ̀wo ààyè fún ìmọ̀ ìjìnlẹ̀ "nínú ìgò" b.a àyẹ̀wò ìdámọ̀ àìsàn ojú ẹsẹ̀ àti pínpín DNA sí ìṣọ̀rí (PVR) àti ìdánimọ̀ àìsàn "tí kìí ṣe nínú ìgò" b.a. àwọn ẹ̀rọ ayàwòrán tàbí wíwọn ìfúnpá. Ó kárí àwọn igbéṣẹ̀ fún ìwádìí àti ìdàgbàsókè, ìpèsè nǹkan (tó fi mọ́ ìpèsè lábẹ́lé àti ìfiránṣẹ́ ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ), òfin, yíyàn àti rírà, ìpolongo, ìgbẹnusọ àti kíkojú àwọn ìdíwọ́ ànfààní lápapọ̀.

translations.source: The implementation of the resolution will build and expand on previous and current work at three levels of WHO to help countries improve access to diagnostic services. The Secretariat is requested to report on implementation progress in 2025. Related document EB152/6 Strengthening diagnostics capacity
translations.reference: Ìṣàmúlò ìpinnu náà máa gùn lé yóò sì tún fẹ àwọn iṣẹ́ àtẹ̀yìnwá àti lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́ ní àwọn ìpele mẹ́tẹ̀ẹ̀ta WHO lójú láti ran àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè lọ́wọ́ láti mú àyípadà bá ànfàní sí ìpèsè iṣẹ́ ìdámọ̀ àìsàn. Wọ́n ní kí Ilé-iṣẹ́ ìjọba náà jábọ̀ lórí ìtẹ̀síwájú àmúlò ní ọdún 2025. Ìwé tó fara jọ ọ́ EB152/6 ríro ìkápá ìdámọ̀ àisàn lágbára
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy within the context of a WHO resolution:

**Ìpilẹ̀ àkóso yìí máa ń daabọ́ àti láti ilana tí ó ti wà àti tí ó ń ṣẹlẹ lọwọ́ ní àkọ́kọ́ mẹ́ta WHO, báyìí máa ń ranlọ́wọ́ orílẹ̀-èdè láti ilọpọ̀ọ́nìyẹ́ láti gbé ìwààlẹ́ àgbáṣiṣẹ́. A máa ń beere lọ́wọ́ Ibi-Ìgbàjá (Secretariat) láti royi ìrìnàjẹ́ àkóso ní 2025. Àkọ́kọ̀ọ́ àwọn ìwé tó ní ibi tí ó ní àfiifẹ́ EB152/6 Gíga àgbíyesi ìfarada àgbáṣiṣẹ́.**

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Ìpilẹ̀ àkóso yìí:** "The implementation of the resolution" -  "Ìpilẹ̀" means implementation, "àkóso" means resolution/management, and "yìí" refers to "this."
*   **máa ń daabọ́ àti láti ilana tí ó ti wà àti tí ó ń ṣẹlẹ lọwọ́:** "will build and expand on previous and current work" - This is a more idiomatic way to express building upon existing work. "Daabọ́" means to build, "ati lati" means and from, "ilana" means process/work, "ti ó ti wà" means that which existed, and "ti ó ń ṣẹlẹ lọwọ́" means that which is ongoing.
*   **ní àkọ́kọ́ mẹ́ta WHO:** "at three levels of WHO" - "ní" means at/in, "àkọ́kọ́ mẹ́ta" means three levels, and "WHO" is kept as is (as it's an acronym).
*   **báyìí máa ń ranlọ́wọ́ orílẹ̀-èdè láti ilọpọ̀ọ́nìyẹ́ láti gbé ìwààlẹ́ àgbáṣiṣẹ́:** "to help countries improve access to diagnostic services" - "báyìí máa ń ranlọ́wọ́" means will help, "orílẹ̀-èdè" means countries, "láti ilọpọ̀ọ́nìyẹ́" means from access, "láti gbé" means to improve, and "ìwààlẹ́ àgbáṣiṣẹ́" means diagnostic services.
*   **A máa ń beere lọ́wọ́ Ibi-Ìgbàjá (Secretariat):** "The Secretariat is requested" - "A máa ń beere lọ́wọ́" means it is requested, "Ibi-Ìgbàjá" is a translation of Secretariat, and "(Secretariat)" is added in parentheses for clarity.
*   **láti royi ìrìnàjẹ́ àkóso ní 2025:** "to report on implementation progress in 2025" - "láti royi" means to report, "ìrìnàjẹ́ àkóso" means implementation progress, and "ní 2025" means in 2025.
*   **Àkọ́kọ̀ọ́ àwọn ìwé tó ní ibi tí ó ní àfiifẹ́ EB152/6:** "Related document EB152/6" - "Àkọ́kọ̀ọ́ àwọn ìwé" means related document, "tó ní ibi tí ó ní àfiifẹ́" means that has a reference, and "EB152/6" is kept as is.
*   **Gíga àgbíyesi ìfarada àgbáṣiṣẹ́:** "Strengthening diagnostics capacity" - "Gíga àgbíyesi" means strengthening, "ìfarada àgbáṣiṣẹ́" means diagnostics capacity.

translations.source: Awards for outstanding contributions to public health presented during the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly During a moving ceremony at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly in Geneva, awards were presented to persons and institutions from around the world for their outstanding contributions to public health. “The 2023 public health prizes and awards celebrate people who have made a significant contribution to global health, and whose work has extended far beyond the call of normal duty,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “At a time when the world faces many challenges, each is an inspiration and a reminder of the progress that can be made to improve health and wellbeing for all.” The call for nominations of candidates for each prize is sent out each year after closure of the World Health Assembly.
translations.reference: Àmì-ẹ̀yẹ fún ìgbìyànjú aláìlẹ́gbẹ́ sí ìlera àwùjọ tí wọ́n gbékalẹ̀ lásìkò Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé Kẹrìn-dín-lọ́gọ́rin Lásìkò tí ayẹyẹ ń lọ lọ́wọ́ níbi Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé Kẹrìn-dín-lọ́gọ́rin ní Geneva, wọ́n pín àmì-ẹ̀yẹ fún àwọn ènìyàn àti àwọn ilé-iṣẹ́ káàkiri àgbáyé fún ìgbìyànjú aláìlẹ́gbẹ́ wọn sí ìlera àwùjọ. "Ẹ̀bùn àti àmì-ẹ̀yẹ ìlera àwùjọ ọdún 2023 náà gbóríyìn fún àwọn ènìyàn tí wọ́n kópa ribiribi tí wọ́n kó nínú ìlera àwùjọ, àti àwọn tí iṣẹ́ wọn ti ré kọjá ìpè fún iṣẹ́ lásán," Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ WHO ló sọ bẹ́ẹ̀. *Ní àkókò tí gbogbo ayé ń kojú ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ìpèníjà, ìkọ̀ọ̀kan wọn jẹ́ ìwúrí àti ìtanilólobó nípa ìtẹ̀síwájú tí ó ṣe é ṣe láti mú àyípadà bá ìlera àti ìgbáyégbádùn fún gbogbo ènìyàn." Ìpè fún yíyan àwọn akópa fún ẹ̀bùn kọ̀ọ̀kan ni wọ́n máa ń fi ránṣẹ́ síta ní ọdún kọ̀ọ̀kan lẹ́yìn ìparí Àpéjọ Ìlera Àgbáyé náà.

**Aṣayán fún àwọn àfiifẹ́ ìṣàlẹ́ ọ̀tún̄bọ̀ lọ́wọ́ àgbóye ọ̀rọ̀ ilera tí a ṣàfiẹ́ nígbà Àṣàgbóye Ilera Lẹ́gbẹ́ Ìlú (World Health Assembly) ńlá tí ó jẹ́ ńlá tí ńlá.**

Lẹ́nu àṣàgbóye tí ó wà nínú ìgbàgbé àṣàgbóye ilera Lẹ́gbẹ́ Ìlú tí ó jẹ́ ńlá tí ńlá ni Geneva, a ṣàfiẹ́ àwọn aṣayán fún àwọn ènìyàn àti àwọn ìjọ̀pọ̀ ti ó wà láàyànjú àgbègbè fún àwọn àfiifẹ́ wọn lọ́wọ́ àgbóye ọ̀rọ̀ ilera. “Àwọn aṣayán àgbóye ọ̀rọ̀ ilera tí a ṣe ni 2023 jẹ́ fún àwọn ènìyàn tí ó ní ìṣàlẹ́ pàtàkù fún ilera láàyànjú àgbègbè, àti pé ìwọ̀ wọn tún jẹ́ ìfaradà lọ́wọ́ àwọn ìforukọ̀sọ̀ àṣà.” Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Alákòọ̀ Ilera Lẹ́gbẹ́ Ìlú, nìgba náà wó nì. “Lábìnnínbìn nígbà tó ní àwọn ìdánbalétí púpọ̀ ni àgbègbè, gbogbo wọn ni ìpọ̀npọ̀ àti ohun tó ń fi ìrànlọ́wọ́ fún ìdàgbádò ilera àti àṣà àgbébégbé fún gbogbo ènìyàn.” Àkọ́kò fún àwọn ìboríṣírẹ́ fún àwọn aṣayán wọ̀ jọ̀ ni gbogbo ọdún, lẹ́yìn tí àṣàgbóye ilera Lẹ́gbẹ́ Ìlú tí ó jẹ́ ńlá tí ńlá ti parí.

**Explanation of Choices & Key Terms:**

*   **Aṣayán:** Award(s)
*   **Àfiifẹ́ ìṣàlẹ́ ọ̀tún̄bọ̀ lọ́wọ́ àgbóye ọ̀rọ̀ ilera:** Outstanding contributions to public health (This is a more formal and precise phrasing)
*   **Àṣàgbóye Ilera Lẹ́gbẹ́ Ìlú (World Health Assembly):**  I've kept "World Health Assembly" in English in parentheses for clarity, as it's a specific international organization.  "Lẹ́gbẹ́ Ìlú" means "City Assembly" or "International Assembly" in this context.

*   **Alákòọ̀ Ilera Lẹ́gbẹ́ Ìlú:** WHO Director-General. "Alákòọ̀" means Director or Leader.

translations.source: Nominations can be made by national health administrations of a WHO Member State and by any former recipient of the prizes. At its 152nd session in January 2023, the Executive Board designated the 2023 winners of the prizes, on the basis of proposals made by the dedicated selection panels for each prize. The seven laureates have been selected to celebrate their unique role and contribution for public health in their countries and globally. They come from four WHO Regions – for the Americas, the Eastern-Mediterranean, South-East Asia and the Western Pacific. Five winners are individuals, and three among them are women.
translations.reference: Yíyàn lè jẹ́ ṣíṣe láti ọ̀dọ àwọn alákòso ìlera orílẹ̀-èdè fún Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ WHO àti láti ọ̀dọ ẹni tí ó ti gba ẹ̀bùn náà tẹ́lẹ̀. Níbi ìjókòó 152nd rẹ̀ ní oṣù Ṣẹẹrẹ ọdún 2023, Ìgbìmọ̀ Alàkóso yan àwọn tí wọ́n gba ẹ̀bùn náà ní ọdún 2023, tó dá lórí àwọn àbá tí ìgbìmọ̀ àwọn adájọ́ aṣẹ àṣàyàn tí wọ́n ní àfọkànsìn náà ṣe fún ẹ̀bùn kọ̀ọ̀kan. Wọ́n ti yan àwọn ẹni iyì méjèèje náà láti ṣe àjọyọ̀ ipa aláìlẹ́gbẹ́ wọn àti àfikún wọn sí ìlera àwùjọ ní àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè wọn àti káàkiri àgbáyé. Wọ́n wá láti Àwọn Ẹkùn agbègbè WHO mẹ́rin - fún àwọn Amẹ́ríkà, Eastern-Mediterranean, Gúúsù Ìlà-oòrùn Asia àti Western Pacific. Àwọn olóríire márùn-ún nínú wọn jẹ́ ẹni kọ̀ọ̀kan, mẹ́ta nínú wọn sì jẹ́ obìnrin.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document:

Awọn orukọ aṣaju le ṣe pataki nipasẹ̀ awọn iṣakoso ilera wa lagunwa ti orile-ede ti WHO, ati nipasẹ̀ eyekeje ti awọn oniwun prizes niwaju. Ni ibamu si igbekele 152 rẹ ni Oṣù Kẹsán 2023, Igbimọ ni Agba ti aṣoju ti ṣeto awọn olutọju ti awọn prizes ni 2023, lori ipilẹṣẹ awọn iroyipo ti a ṣaṣeyọri nipasẹ̀ awọn aṣoju iroyipo ti a ṣeto fun ọkọọkan ti awọn prizes. Awọn olutọju mẹfà ti a yan lati gbe ireti ati ifiyesi fun ipinlẹ ilera ni awọn orilẹ-ede wọn ati ni kariaye. Wọn wa lati awọn agbo-ile mẹrin ti WHO – fun Amẹrika, Ila-oorun Iwọ-papo, Aziya Iwọ-Oorun ati Iwọ-Ogun Ila-papo. Eniyan marun ni awọn olutọju, ati pe mẹta ninu wọn ni obinrin.

translations.source: In addition, the two institutions that have received a 2023 award are led by women. Winners of the 2023 prizes Sasakawa Health PrizeThe 2023 laureates: The Nick Simons Institute from Nepal, and Professor Vichai Tienthavorn from Thailand The Nick Simons Institute is a nongovernmental organization aiming to improve health care in the rural areas of Nepal. Working closely with the Ministry of Health and Population of Nepal, the Institute has trained 7000 medical professionals (such as midwives and anaesthetic assistants) in rural areas, who now provide most-needed services to local communities in the absence of a sufficient pool of general practitioners and specialists. The Institute has supplied essential equipment and provided additional health personnel to alleviate the lack of medical professionals in 44 hospitals, and supported the design of an action plan for better quality services in more than 100 rural hospitals.
translations.reference: Ní àfikún, àwọn ilé-iṣẹ́ méjì tí wọ́n ti gba àmì-ẹ̀yẹ ọdún 2023 ló jẹ́ wí pé obìnrin ló léwájú wọn. Àwọn tí wọ́n gba ẹ̀bùn ọdún 2023 Ẹ̀bùn Ìlera Sasakawa Àwọn ẹni iyì ọdún 2023: Nick Simons Institute láti Nepal, àti Ọ̀jọ̀gbọ́n Vichai Tienthavorn láti Thailand Nick Simons Institute jẹ́ ilé-iṣẹ́ tí kìí ṣe ti ìjọba tó ń gbèrò láti mú àyípadà bá ètò ìlera ní àwọn agbègbè ìgbèríko Nepal. Ó ń ṣiṣẹ́ papọ̀ pẹ̀lú Ilé-iṣẹ́ Ìjọba tó ń rí sí Ètò Ìlera àti Ònkà ènìyàn ní Nepal, Ilé-iṣẹ́ náà ti kọ́ 7000 àwọn akọ́ṣẹ́mọṣẹ́ ètò ìlera (bíi àwọn agbẹ̀bí àti àwọn amúgbá lẹ́gbẹ̀ apèsè abẹ́rẹ́ apa-ìrora) ní àwọn agbègbè ìgbèríko, tí wọ́n lè pèsè àwọn ìpèsè iṣẹ́ tí wọ́n nílò jùlọ fún àwọn àwùjọ ìbílẹ̀ níbi tí ò bá ti sí orísun ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn òṣìṣẹ́ ìlera àti akọ́ṣẹ́mọṣẹ́. Ilé-iṣẹ́ náà ti pèsè àwọn irinṣẹ́ pàtàkì ó sì ti pèsè àfikún àwọn akọ́ṣẹ́mọṣẹ́ ìlera láti mú àdínkù bá àìsí àwọn akọ́ṣẹ́mọṣẹ́ ìlera ní ilé-ìwòsàn 44, ó sì ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún àgbékalẹ̀ ètò amúṣẹ́ṣe fún ìpèsè iṣẹ́ ojúlówó tó dára ní àwọn ilé-ìwòsàn ìgbèríko tó lé ní 100.

translations.source: The Institute also conducts research and advocacy activities to shape perceptions and influence policies that affect rural health workers in Nepal. Professor Vichai Tienthavorn is President of the Praboromarajchanok Institute at the Ministry of Public Health of Thailand. One of the nation-wide initiatives he has led is an innovative health promotion policy to help address thalassemia, with his work resulting in the adoption of an effective national policy for thalassemia prevention and control. He also designed an effective communications tool and screening method to help citizens to adapt their behaviours in order to prevent and control diabetes and hypertension. As a result of this work, a national policy was adopted with significant positive impact on the country’s disease burden.
translations.reference: Ilé-iṣẹ́ náà tún ṣe ìwádìí àti ìgbẹnusọ fún àwọn iṣẹ́ láti yàwòrán ojú ìwòye àti láti dásí àwọn òfin tó kan àwọn òṣìṣẹ́ ìlera ìgbèríko ní Nepal. Ọ̀jọ̀gbọ́n Vichai Tienthavorn ni Ààrẹ Praboromarajchanok Institute ní Ilé-iṣẹ́ ìjọba tó ń rí sí Ètò Ìlera àwùjọ ní Thailand. Ọ̀kan lára àwọn ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ jákèjádò-àgbáyé tí ó ti léwájú ni òfin ìpolongo ìlera onímọ̀ ọ̀tun láti báni kojú ìṣòro àìtó hóró ẹ̀jẹ̀ agbẹ̀mí, pẹ̀lú bí iṣẹ́ rẹ̀ ṣe yọrí sí gbígba òfin orílẹ̀-èdè tó ń ṣiṣẹ́ fún ìdíwọ́ àti ìdènà ìṣòro àìtó hóró ẹ̀jẹ̀ agbẹ̀mí wọlé. Ó tún ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ irinṣẹ́ ìbáraẹnisọ̀rọ̀ tó ń ṣiṣẹ́ àti ìlànà àyẹ̀wò láti ran àwọn ará ìlú lọ́wọ́ láti gba ìhùwàsí wọn fún ìdíwọ́ àti ìdènà àtọgbẹ àti ẹ̀jẹ̀ ríru. Nítorí iṣẹ́ yìí, wọ́n gba òfin orílẹ̀-èdè kan wọlé pẹ̀lú ipa rere tó lápẹrẹ lórí ẹrù ààrùn orílẹ̀-èdè.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and natural flow. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Ni afikun, iwe-iwé ti Ile-ẹse naa tun ṣe iwadi ati awọn iṣẹ́ ìgbádúróní lára, boya a le yanju àwọn èdè àti ṣíṣúyíṣú iṣọkan tí ó ní àfiifẹ́ fún àwọn oníṣe ilera tí ó wà ní àwọn agbègbẹ́ òtura ní Nepal. Olùdásílẹ̀ ti Praboromarajchanok Institute tí ó wà ní Ministry of Public Health ti Thailand ni Professor Vichai Tienthavorn. Ọ̀kan nínú àwọn ìgbàgbé lọ́wọ́ tó fowo ara rẹ̀ ṣe ni ẹniti ó ṣíṣẹ́ nípa ìlọsiwaju ilera tí ó ṣókàn lára, lára abájìọpẹ́ẹ̀ tó ní ìrànlọ́wọ́ fún ìdágbádòràn thalassemia, àti pé ìṣíṣí iṣé rẹ̀ fúnní níbẹ̀ pé a gbà dé ìlànà ilẹ̀ tí ó ní àmójú àti ìṣàkóso fún thalassemia. Ó tun ṣíṣẹ́ nípa ohun elo àgbáfè lára àti ìwífúnrere tí ó ní ìrànlọ́wọ́ fún àwọn ènìyàn láti ṣàlàyé àwọn ìdáná wọn nífẹ̀ẹ́ lára lára abájìọpẹ́ẹ̀ tó ní ìrànlọ́wọ́ fún ìdágbádòràn àti ìṣàkóso pẹpẹẹ́ àti ìgbẹ̀dógún. Báyìí, a gbà dé ìlànà ilẹ̀ pẹ̀lú àpilẹ́ẹ́ àpẹ́rẹ́ tó ní àfiifẹ́ fún ìwà ilẹ̀.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Institute"**: I used "Ile-ẹse" which generally means "Institute" or "Research Institute."
*   **"Advocacy activities"**: Translated as "awọn iṣẹ́ ìgbádúróní" (advocacy works/activities).
*   **"Shape perceptions"**:  "yanju àwọn èdè" (to shape/formulate opinions/expressions).
*   **"Influence policies"**: "ṣíṣúyíṣú iṣọkan" (to influence/affect decisions/agreements).
*   **"Nation-wide initiatives"**: "Àwọn ìgbàgbé lọ́wọ́ tó fowo ara rẹ̀ ṣe" (Initiatives he personally carried out).
*   **"Health promotion policy"**: "ìlọsiwaju ilera" (health promotion).
*   **"Thalassemia"**: I kept "thalassemia" as it's a recognized medical term.
*   **"Effective national policy"**: "ìlànà ilẹ̀ tí ó ní àmójú àti ìṣàkóso" (national policy that has effectiveness and control).
*   **"Communications tool"**: "ohun elo àgbáfè" (a widely used tool).
*   **"Screening method"**: "ìwífúnrere" (method of detection/screening).
*   **"Adapt their behaviours"**: "ṣàlàyé àwọn ìdáná wọn" (to change/modify their behaviors).
*   **"Disease burden"**: "ìwà ilẹ̀" (the state of the country/nation - used to convey the impact of disease).
*   **"As a result of this work"**: "Báyìí" (As a result/Consequently).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Without more context about the specific audience and purpose of this translation, some choices are educated guesses.
*   **Terminology:** Medical terminology can be tricky.  I've tried to use common Yoruba terms where possible, but some specialized terms might need further review by a Yoruba-speaking medical professional.
*   **Natural Flow:** I've prioritized making the Yoruba text read naturally, even if it means slightly deviating from a literal word-for-word translation.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: Professor Tienthavorn also played an essential role in addressing the challenge of uneven distribution of the health workforce, especially in border and rural areas of Thailand. He led the establishment of partnerships with village schools to recruit high-school students, train them and help them settle back locally after having accomplished an accelerated medical curriculum. United Arab Emirates Health Foundation Prize2023 award laureate: Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre from the PhilippinesIn addition to being a renowned clinician and researcher, Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre is a passionate advocate for exclusive breastfeeding and the founder of Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina (Health of Mother and Child) – a Philippine non-governmental organization. Dr Silvestre has leveraged her experience as a community practitioner to conduct research and design a protocol to help improve the health of mothers and newborns. The pioneering protocol she created describes a simple set of actions to be undertaken by health workers attending a mother during delivery and her newborn after birth.
translations.reference: Ọ̀jọ̀gbọ́n Tienthavorn tún kó ipa pàtàkì nínú kíkojú ìpèníjà pínpín àwọn òṣìṣẹ́ ìlera lọ́nà tí ò dọ́gba, pàápàá jùlọ ní àwọn agbègbè ibodè àti ìgbèríko Thailand. Ó léwájú ìdásílẹ̀ àjọṣepọ̀ kan pẹ̀lú àwọn ilé-ẹ̀kọ́ ìgbèríko láti gba àwọn akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ ilé-ẹ̀kọ girama, kọ́ wọn ó sì tún ràn wọ́n lọ́wọ́ láti padà sí ìgbèríko lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n ti parí kọ̀ríkúlọ́mù ètò ìlera tó yára. Ẹ̀bùn United Arab Emirates Health Foundation ọdún 2023 fún ẹni iyì: Dr Maria Asuncion Silvester láti Philippines ní àmì-ẹ̀yẹ Ní àfikún sí jíjẹ́ gbajúgbajà òṣìṣẹ́ ìlera àti oníwádìí, Dr Maria Asuncion Silvester jẹ́ agbẹnusọ fún ògédé ìfọ́mọlọ́yàn tọkàn-tọkàn àti olùdásílẹ̀ Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina (Ìlera Ìyá àti Ọmọ) - Ilé-iṣẹ́ tí kìí ṣe ti ìjọba ní orílẹ̀-èdè Philippine. Dr Silvester ti ṣàmúlò ìrírí rẹ̀ gẹ́gẹ́ bíi òṣìṣẹ́ ìlera láwùjọ láti ṣe ìwádìí ó sì ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ìlànà láti mú àyípadà bá ìlera àwọn ìyá àti àwọn ọmọ tuntun. Ìlànà àkọ́kọ́ tí ó pèsè ṣàpèjúwe ètò amúṣẹ́ṣe tó rọrùn tí àwọn òṣìṣẹ́ ètò ìlera tí wọ́n ń dá àwọn ìyá lóhùn lásìkò ìbímọ àti ọmọ rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìbímọ yóò ṣe.

translations.source: This protocol, which is applicable in all resource settings, helps to bridge the health equity gap in mother-and-child care. In partnership with the national government of the Philippines as well as 17 countries in the WHO Western Pacific Region, she has played a key role in scaling up the First Embrace campaign for Early Essential Newborn Care – a set of simple WHO-recommended and cost-effective interventions. His Highness Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Prize for Research in Health Care for the Elderly and in Health Promotion 2023 award laureates: The National Center for Chronic and Noncommunicable Disease Control and Prevention from China; and Dr Abla Mehio Sibai from Lebanon The National Center for Chronic and Noncommunicable Disease Control and Prevention, part of the Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention (China CDC), has hosted a department focusing on the health of older people since 2013.
translations.reference: Ìlànà yìí, tí ó ṣe é lò ní gbogbo ààyè ìtọ́jú, ń pèsè ìrànlọ́wọ́ láti dí àlàfo ìdọ́gba ìlera nínú ìtọ́jú ìyá-àti-ọmọ. Ní àjọṣepọ̀ pẹ̀lú ìjọba orílẹ̀-èdè Philippines bákan náà ni àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè 17 ní Ẹkùn Agbègbè Western Pacific WHO, ó ti kó ipa pàtàkì nínú fífẹ ìpolongo Ìdìmọ́ra Àkọ́kọ́ fún Ìtọ́jú ọmọtuntun Tó Ṣe Pàtàkì ní Ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ lójú - ètò àwọn ìdásí tó rọrùn tí WHO dábàá tí kò ga ju ara lọ. Ẹ̀bùn Ọlọ́lá Jùlọ Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah fún Ìwádìí nínú Ètò Ìlera fún Àwọn Arúgbó àti nínú Ìpolongo Ìlera Àwọn ẹni iyì àmì-ẹ̀yẹ náà fún ọdún 2023: Ààyè Orílẹ̀-èdè fún Ààrùn tó Lágbára tí Kìí ràn láti China; àti Dr Alba Mehio Sibai láti Lebanon Ààyè Orílẹ̀-èdè fún Ìdíwọ́ àti Ìdènà Ààrùn tó Lágbára tí Kìí ràn, apá kan lára Ààyè fún Ìdíwọ́ àti Ìdènà Ààrùn Chinese (China CDC), ti gbàlejò ẹ̀ka kan tó ń gbájúmọ́ ìlera àwọn ènìyàn tí wọ́n ti dàgbà láti ọdún 2013.

translations.source: It delivers a broad range of programmes and activities that advance health promotion for older people, ranging from better understanding the causes of morbidity and mortality through national surveillance for noncommunicable diseases, surveys, death registries and research, to tailored programmes to promote health literacy and evidence-based policies. Initiatives include a dedicated toolkit for older people, capacity‑building of 2000 primary care staff, a health advocacy week dedicated to older people, and an innovative project to improve the mental and cognitive health of more than half a million older persons in both urban and rural areas. Dr Abla Mehio Sibai is Professor of Epidemiology and Dean of the Faculty of Health Sciences at the American University of Beirut in Lebanon. She has made an outstanding contribution to older people’s health in Lebanon and the region, developing a holistic approach to the promotion of healthy ageing. She led the innovative initiative of enrolling close to 600 older people every year at the University for Seniors at the American University of Beirut, in addition to training public health professionals.
translations.reference: Ó ń pèsè àwọn ètò lóriṣiríṣi àti iṣẹ́ tí ó ń mú ìlọsíwájú bá ìpolongo ìlera fún àwọn ènìyàn tí wọ́n ti dàgbà, bẹ̀rẹ̀ láti nínú òye tó dára nípa àwọn aṣokùnfa àìsàn àti ikú nípasẹ ìtọpinpin fún àwọn ààrùn tí kìí ràn, ìwádìí, àwọn àkọsílẹ̀ ikú àti ìwádìí, fún àwọn ètò tí wọ́n ṣe láti ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ìmọ̀ ìlera àti àwọn òfin adálórí-ẹ̀rí. Lára ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ náà ni irinṣẹ́ tí wọ́n fi sọrí àwọn ènìyàn tí wọ́n ti dàgbà, ìdánilẹ́kọ̀ọ́ àwọn òṣìṣẹ́ ètò ìlera alábọ́dé 2000, ọ̀sẹ̀ ìgbẹnusọ fún ìlera tí wọ́n ṣe fún àwọn ènìyàn tí wọ́n ti dàgbà, àti iṣẹ́ àkànṣe tuntun láti mú àyípadà bá ìlera ọpọlọ àti òye fún àwọn ènìyàn tí wọ́n ti dàgbà tí wọ́n lé ní ìdajì mílíọ̀nù ní àwọn ìlú àti ìgbèríko. Dr Abla Mehio Sibai jẹ́ Ọ̀jọ̀gbọ́n nípa Àjàkálẹ̀ Ààrùn àti Díínì Ẹ̀ka-ẹ̀kọ́ Àwọn Sáyẹ́nsì Ìlera ní American University of Beirut ní Lebanon. Ó ti kó ipa aláìlẹ́gbẹ́ sí ìlera àwọn ènìyàn tí wọ́n ti dàgbà ní Lebanon àti ní ẹkùn agbègbè náà, ṣíṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ìgbésẹ̀ àpapọ̀ fún àtìlẹ́yìn ìlera àwọn arúgbó. Ó léwájú ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ ìmọ̀-ọ̀tunláti ṣe ìforúkọsílẹ̀ àwọn ènìyàn tó ti dàgbà tí wọ́n tó 600 ní ọdọọdún ní Yunifásítì fún Àwọn Àgbàlagbà ní American University of Beirut, ní àfikún sí kíkọ́ àwọn akọ́ṣẹ́mọṣẹ́ ìlera àwùjọ.

translations.source: She co-led the landmark WHO Global Burden of Disease Study in Lebanon (2000) and conducted the first national survey on noncommunicable diseases and risk factors, informing advocacy, policy-making and programme planning for health promotion. Building on her numerous achievements, Dr Sibai has developed the “National Strategy for Older Persons” in Lebanon (2020–2030). Dr Sibai also created the Arab Ageing Network and the Centre for Studies on Ageing to create links between research and policy at the national, regional and international levels. Dr LEE Jong-wook Memorial Prize for Public Health 2023 award laureate: Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses from Guatemala
translations.reference: Ó pẹ̀lú jọ léwájú Ìṣòro Àgbáyé WHO fún Ìwádìí Ààrùn tó lápẹẹrẹ ní Lebanon (2000) ó sì ṣe ìwádìí orílẹ̀-èdè àkọ́kọ́ lórí àwọn ààrùn tí kìí ràn àti àwọn aṣokùnfà ewu, ṣíṣe agbẹnusọ, òfin-ṣíṣe àti ṣíṣe ètò fún ìpolongo ìlera. Gígùn lórí ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn àṣeyọrí rẹ̀, Dr Sibai ti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ "ìlànà-iṣẹ́ Orílẹ̀-èdè fún Àwọn tí wọ́n ti dàgbà" ní Lebanon (2020-2030). Dr Sibai tún ti dá ojú-òpóArúgbó Árábù àti Ààyè fún Ìwádìí lórí Arúgbó sílẹ̀ láti ṣe ìdásílẹ̀ àjọṣepọ̀ láàrín ìwádìí àti òfin ní àwọn ìpele orílẹ̀-èdè, ẹkùn agbègbè àti ilẹ̀ òkèèrè. Ẹ̀bùn Ìrántí Dr LEE Jong-wook fún Ìlera àwùjọ Àwọn ẹni iyì àmì-ẹ̀yẹ náà fún ọdún 2023: Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses láti Guatemala

translations.source: Having promoted community participation in hospital care for over 20 years, Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses set up community-based committees focusing on the safety and satisfaction of patients, their families and the community. Given the positive impact of his work in improving the quality of patient care, the model of ethical and patient-centred care that he designed was scaled up at the national level. Nowadays, as the national supervisor for hospitals of Guatemala, he has designed a programme to improve the quality of services for hospital patients and their families. Dr Meneses has recently coordinated an innovative pilot project in the Guatemala South Health Area to build the first national integrated network of health services, with a priority focus on underserved communities. His work led to the adoption of a national strategy of integrated health services networks, the first in Central America.
translations.reference: Lẹ́yìn tí ó ti ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ìkópa àwùjọ nínú ìtọ́jú ilé-ìwòsàn fún bíi 20 ọdún, Dr Francisco Meneses ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ìgbìmọ̀ adálórí-àwùjọ tó gbájúmọ́ ààbò àti ìtẹ́lọ́rùn àwọn aláìsàn, àwọn ẹbí wọn àti àwùjọ. Pẹ̀lú ipa rere iṣẹ́ rẹ̀ láti mú àyípadà bá ojúlówó ìtọ́jú aláìsàn, àwòṣe ìtọ́jú tó dára adálórí-aláìsàn tí ó ṣe ni wọ́n fẹ̀ lójú ní ìpele orílẹ̀-èdè. Ní àsìkò yìí, gẹ́gẹ́ bíi alámòjútó orílẹ̀-èdè fún àwọn ilé-ìwòsàn ti Guatemala, ó ti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ètò kan láti mú àyípadà bá ojúlówó ìpèsè iṣẹ́ fún àwọn aláìsàn ní ilé-ìwòsàn àti àwọn ẹbí wọn. Dr Meneses ti ṣàkóso iṣẹ́ àkànṣe onímọ̀ tuntun kékeré ní Agbègbè Ìlera Gúúsù Guatemala láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ojú-òpóaláfibọ̀ orílẹ̀-èdè àkọ́kọ́ fún ìpèsè iṣẹ́ ìlera, pẹ̀lú ọ̀rayàn láti gbájúmọ́ àwọn àwùjọ tí wọn kò tọ́jú dáadáa. Iṣẹ́ rẹ̀ ti yọrí sí gbígba ìlànà-iṣẹ́ orílẹ̀-èdè lórí àwọn ojú-òpóìpèsè iṣẹ́ ìlera aláfibọ̀ wọlé, àkọ́kọ́ rẹ̀ ní Àárín Gbùngbùn ilẹ̀ Adúláwọ̀.

translations.source: Nelson Mandela Award for Health Promotion 2023 award laureate: Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma from Bahrain In addition to being the Chief Executive Officer of the National Health Regulatory Authority of Bahrain, Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma has held various leadership positions in government and academic institutions in her country. Dr Al Jalahma has played a major role in the development of numerous national and region-wide strategies and plans in the field of primary healthcare, child and adolescent health, chronic diseases and antismoking. In her current role, Dr Al Jalahma has led major successful initiatives, such as the national accreditation system for health institutions, the digital transformation of the National Health Regulatory Authority, and several initiatives leading to increased efficiency and excellence in the health sector.
translations.reference: Àmì-ẹ̀yẹ Nelson Mandela fún Ìpolongo Ìlera Ẹni iyì àmì-ẹ̀yẹ náà fún ọdún 2023: Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma láti Bahrain Ní àfikún sí jíjẹ́ Olórí Aláṣẹ fún Àwọn Aláṣẹ Aṣòfin Ìlera Orílẹ̀-èdè ti Bahrain, Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma ti di oríṣiríṣi ipò asíwájú mú nínú ìjọba àti ní àwọn ilé-ẹ̀kọ́ ní orílẹ̀-èdè rẹ̀. Dr Al Jalahma ti kó ipa pàtàkì nínú ìdàgbàsókè oríṣiríṣi àwọn ìlànà-iṣẹ́ àti ètò orílẹ̀-èdè àti káàkiri ẹkùn agbègbè ní ẹ̀ka ètò ìlera alábọ́dé, ìlera ọmọ àti ọ̀dọ́, àwọn ààrùn tó lágbára àti àìmu-sìgá. Ní ipò rẹ̀ lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́, Dr Al Jalahma ti léwájú àwọn ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ aláṣeyọrí tó ṣe pàtàkì, bíi ètò àyẹ̀wò orílẹ̀-èdè fún àwọn ilé-iṣẹ́ ìlera, àyípadà oní àkósódù fún Àwọn Aláṣẹ Aṣòfin Ìlera Orílẹ̀-èdè, àti oríṣiríṣi ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ tí ó ń yọrí sí àlékún iṣẹ́ àti àṣeyọrí ní ẹ̀ka ètò ìlera.

*   **Dialectal Variations:** Yoruba has several dialects. This translation aims for a generally understandable form, but some words or phrases might be preferred differently in specific regions.

translations.source: She is also a prominent voice for health promotion across various channels throughout all countries of the Gulf Cooperation Council.
translations.reference: Ó tún jẹ́ ohùn tó gbajúgbajà fún ìpolongo ìlera káàkiri oríṣiríṣi ojú-òpójákèjádò gbogbo àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè Ìgbìmọ̀ Ìfowópamọ́ Ọ̀gbun.
translations.hypothesis: Óun ni ó máa ń jẹ́ òṣùgbó̀ kan tó ilù máa ń ilé ìjùbọ̀ àgbáyé fún ìdíró àgbàdọ́gbọ̀ ní àwọn ilé-ìṣọ̀ro àti oríléède gbogbo àwọn orílè-èdè Arabá ti Gulf Cooperation Council.

translations.source: WHO and Republic of Korea sign landmark agreement to boost biomanufacturing capacity WHO and the Republic of Korea have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to establish a global training hub in biomanufacturing. This global training centre will serve all low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) to produce biologicals, such as vaccines, insulin, monoclonal antibodies, and cancer medicines. The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the need to expand the local and regional production of vaccines and other essential health products in LMICs to ensure equitable access. To provide countries with the know-how to rapidly produce mRNA vaccines, WHO, in collaboration with partners, established the mRNA Technology Transfer programme, with the formal inauguration of the Hub facility in South Africa in April 2023.
translations.reference: WHO àti Republic of Korea buwọ́lu àdéhùn tó lápẹrẹ láti mú àlékún bá ìkápá ìpèsè ajẹmọ́-ìbí WHO àti Republic of Korea ti tọwọ́ bọ Ìwé Àdéhùn Àsọyépọ̀ láti ṣe ìdásílẹ̀ ibùdó ìdánilẹ́kọ̀ọ́ àgbáyé fún ìpèsè ajẹmọ́-ìbí. Ààyè ìdánilẹ́kọ̀ọ́ àgbáyé náà máa ṣiṣẹ́ sin gbogbo àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tí ìpawówọlé wọn kéré àti agbede-méjì (LMICs) láti pèsè àwọn ajẹmọ́-ìbí, bíi àjẹsára, insulínì, àkàndá sójà ara àti àwọn oògùn ààrùn jẹjẹrẹ. Àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn COVID-19 ṣàfihàn ìdí láti fẹ ìpèsè àjẹsára lábẹ́lé àti ní ẹkùn agbègbè àti àwọn ohun èlò ìlera mìíràn tó ṣe pàtàkì nínú LMICs láti ṣe àrídájú ànfààní tó dọ́gba lójú. Láti pèsè ìmọ̀ọ́ṣe fún àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè láti lè tètè pèse àjẹsára mRNA, WHO, ní àjọṣepọ̀ pẹ̀lú àwọn alájọṣepọ̀, ṣe ìdásílẹ̀ ètò Ìfiránṣẹ́ Ìmọ̀ Ẹ̀rọ nRNA, pẹ̀lú ìfilọ́lẹ̀ ilé-ìwòsàn ibùdó ní South Africa ní oṣù Igbe ọdún 2023.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the translation of the provided document into Yoruba, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the context of a low-resource language setting. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Ni pataki, WHO ati Orilẹ-ede Korea (South Korea) ti fi ipin kan lẹnu lati fi agbara ilọsiwaju iṣo-biọgbologbon (biomanufacturing) mulẹ. WHO ati Orilẹ-ede Korea ti ṣubu iwe itọsọna (Memorandum of Understanding) lati ṣeto ibi igbimọgbẹ agbaye ni iṣo-biọgbologbon. Ibi igbimọgbẹ agbaye yí àfori gbogbo awọn orilẹ-ede kekere ati iwọn-ona (LMICs) ni anfani ṣiṣepọ awọn biọ-ọrọ, gẹgẹ bi awọn ọrọ-rẹ, insulini, awọn abawọn monoclonal, ati awọn ọrọ-rẹ cancer.  Àṣàbàgbẹẹ COVID-19 fihan ilọsiwaju ti a mọ ni lati fa awọn ohun elo ti a ṣe ni ilẹ-ilẹ ati ni agbaye ni awọn orilẹ-ede kekere ati iwọn-ona (LMICs) lati rii daju pe gbogbo eniyan ni aṣaaju si ohun ti wọn ṣe pataki.  Lati fun awọn orilẹ-ede ni ohun ti wọn mọ bi o ṣe le ṣe awọn ọrọ-rẹ mRNA ni iyara, WHO, ni idahun pẹlu awọn alaba, ti ṣeto atilẹba iṣo-rẹ mRNA, pẹlu iṣirobọṣi ti ibi igbimọgbẹ ni South Africa ni Oṣù Kẹrin 2023.

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **WHO:**  I've kept "WHO" as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym even in Yoruba contexts.
*   **Republic of Korea (South Korea):** I've included both the formal name and the more common "South Korea" in parentheses for clarity.
*   **Biomanufacturing:**  "Iṣo-biọgbologbon" is a direct translation, combining "iṣo" (production/manufacturing) and "biọgbologbon" (biological). This is a relatively new term, so it might require explanation in some contexts.
*   **Memorandum of Understanding:** "Iwe itọsọna" is a standard translation for this.
*   **Global Training Hub:** "Ibi igbimọgbẹ agbaye" - "Ibi" means place/centre, "igbimọgbẹ" means training, and "agbaye" means global.
*   **Low- and Middle-Income Countries (LMICs):** "Awọn orilẹ-ede kekere ati iwọn-ona" - This is a direct translation.  Using the acronym "LMICs" might be confusing for many Yoruba speakers.
*   **Biologicals:** "Awọn biọ-ọrọ" - "Biọ" means biological, and "ọrọ" means products/things.
*   **Vaccines, Insulin, Monoclonal Antibodies, Cancer Medicines:** These are translated directly, using Yoruba equivalents where possible.
*   **mRNA Technology Transfer Programme:** "Atilẹba iṣo-rẹ mRNA" - "Atilẹba" means initiative/programme, "iṣo-rẹ" means production/transfer, and "mRNA" is kept as is.
*   **Formal Inauguration:** "Iṣirobọṣi" - means inauguration/opening.
*   **South Africa:** Kept as is, as it's a well-known place name.
*   **Oṣù Kẹrin:** April (Fourth month in Yoruba calendar)

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:** This translation aims for general accuracy.  The best translation will always depend on the specific audience and purpose.
*   **Terminology:**  New technical terms (like "biomanufacturing") may need to be explained or adapted further. Consider creating a glossary if the document will be used frequently.
*   **Readability:**  I've tried to use relatively simple sentence structures to improve readability.
*   **Review by Native Speakers:**  It's *crucial* to have this translation reviewed by native Yoruba speakers, especially those familiar with the subject matter, to ensure accuracy and naturalness.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: This multilateral effort and other recent local production initiatives call for ambitious workforce development strategies to recruit, upskill and retain the staff working at these facilities, with unique competencies required in bioproduction. To address this gap, WHO and the Republic of Korea have engaged in closed collaboration for over a year and are now formalizing their commitment towards the establishment of a global training hub in biomanufacturing. “We are delighted to sign this Memorandum of Understanding to elevate our partnership to the next level and collaborate in the development of a comprehensive training curriculum. We hope this world-class training hub will help to build a skilled workforce and ensure quality health products can be manufactured around the world,” said Dr Yukiko Nakatani, WHO Assistant Director-General, Access to Medicines and Health Products. The Government of the Republic of Korea is offering a large range of national training facilities and programmes covering the full spectrum of biological manufacturing and is expanding its operations to accommodate more trainees from other countries with the construction of a Global Bio Education Campus to be launched in 2025.
translations.reference: Ìgbésẹ̀ oríṣiríṣi yìí àti àwọn ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ ìpèsè lábẹ́lẹ́ mìíràn láìpẹ́ yìí pè fún ìlànà-iṣẹ́ ìdagbàsókè àwọn òṣìṣẹ́ aláfọkànsìn fún ìgbaniwọlé, ìdánilẹ́kọ̀ọ́ àti láti tẹ̀síwájú pẹ̀lú àwọn òṣìṣẹ́ tó ń ṣiṣẹ́ ní àwọn ilé-ìwòsàn wọ̀nyìí, pẹ̀lú ìmọ̀ọ́ṣe tó yàtọ̀ tí ó ṣe pàtàkì nínú ìpèsè ajẹmọ́-ìbí. Láti wá iyànjú sí àlàfo yìí, WHO àti Republic of Korea ti kópa nínú àjọṣepọ̀ abẹ́lé láti bí ọdún kan wọ́n sì ti ń sọ ìfọkànsìn wọn fún ìdásílẹ̀ ibùdó ìdánilẹ́kọ̀ọ́ àgbáyé fún ajẹmọ́-ìbí di tòótọ́. "Inú wa dùn láti buwọ́lù Ìwé Àdéhùn Àsọyépọ̀ yìí láti gbé àjọṣepọ̀ wa lọ sí ìpele tí ó kàn kí á sì ní àjọṣepọ̀ nínú àgbékalẹ̀ kọ̀ríkúlọ́mù Ìdánilẹ́kọ̀ọ́ kíkún. A lérò wí pé ibùdó ìdánilẹ́kọ̀ọ́ tó dára jùlọ lágbàyé yìí máa ràn wá lọ́wọ́ láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ àwọn òṣìṣẹ́ tí wọ́n nímọ̀ àti láti ríi dájú pé àwọn ohun èlò ìlera tó jẹ́ ojúlówó lè di ṣíṣe káàkiri àgbáyé, Dr Yukiko Nakatani, Igbákejì Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ WHO, Ànfààní sí Àwọn Oògùn àti Àwọn Ohun Èlò Ìlera. Ìjọba orílẹ̀-èdè Republic of Korea ń ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ àwọn ilé-ìwòsàn ìdánilẹ́kọ̀ọ́ orílẹ̀-èdè àti ètò lóriṣiríṣi tó kárí gbogbo ìpele ìpèsè ajẹmọ́-ìbí tó sì ń fẹ iṣẹ́ rẹ̀ lójú láti ṣe àfikún ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ láti àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè mìíràn pẹ̀lú kíkọ́ Ọgbà Ilé-ẹ̀kọ́ Ìbí Àgbáyé tí wọn yóò ṣe ìfilọ́lẹ̀ rẹ̀ ní ọdún 2025.

translations.source: “I hope this Memorandum of Understanding will enhance cooperation between the Republic of Korea and WHO to strengthen biomanufacturing capacities of LMICs,” said Mr Hwang Seunghyun Director General, Global Vaccine Hub Office at the Ministry of Health and Welfare from Republic of Korea. In parallel, WHO is intensifying regulatory system strengthening to ensure the quality and safety of health products. Stronger regulatory agencies will also enhance confidence in locally produced products and counter misinformation and the availability of unsafe substandard or falsified medicines.
translations.reference: Mo lérò pé Àdéhùn Àsọyépọ̀ yìí máa ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ìfọwọ́sowọ́pọ̀ láàrín Republic of Korea àti WHO láti ró ìkápá ìpèsè ajẹmọ́-ìbí fún LMICs lágbára," Mr Hwang Seunghyun Olùdarí Àpapọ̀, Ọ́fíísì Ibùdó Àjẹsára Àgbáyé ní Ilé-iṣẹ́ Ìjọba tó ń rí sí Ètò Ìlera àti Ìgbáyégbádùn láti Republic of Korea. Ní àfiwé, WHO ń ṣe àtúnṣe sí ìrólágbára ètò òfin láti rí i dájú pé ojúlówó àti ààbò àwọn ohun èlò ìlera wà. Àwọn àjọ alámòjútó tó lágbára máa tún mú kí ìgbàgbọ́ wà nínú àwọn ohun èlò tí wọ́n ṣe ní abẹ́lé tí yóò sì gbógun ti ìròyìn ẹlẹ́jẹ̀ àti wiwà àwọn oògùn tí kìí ṣe ojúlówó tàbí ayédèrú tó léwu.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the translation of the provided English text into Yoruba, aiming for accuracy and natural flow. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

"Mo nire ju igba ti Memorandum of Understanding yí ó ní ìgbádákùn àfiànifún àse àti ìdahun láàrin Orílè-èdè Korea àti WHO láti mú ìṣiṣẹpọ̀ àwọn biomanufacturing tí ó wà ní àwọn orílè-èdè tí ó ní àmúṣi (LMICs) di látà."  Mr. Hwang Seunghyun, Alákòòkò Gómìnà, Ìtajà Global Vaccine Hub ní Ẹkẹ́lẹ́gbẹ́ Àgbàyemo Àgbádo àti Àìsálẹ́ ti Orílè-èdè Korea, nìyẹn ó wí pé. Ní àwọn ìgbà yìú, WHO n fẹ́ràn ìdágbádò àfiànifún ìjèsíṣẹ́ àti ìṣàdúróòyìn láti de ọwọ́ láti rí i pé àwọn èrò àgbáyé máa ń jẹ́ àti lágbàdò. Àwọn aṣàájú ìjèsíṣẹ́ tí ó jẹ́ látà ló máa ń fi ìgbádákùn àfiànifún àse àti ìdahun ní àwọn èrò tí ó wà ní inú orílè-èdè àti láti fi ìtan ó jọ̀wọ́ àwọn ìwífúnrọ̀ àti àwọn èrò tí ó kéré, àti àwọn èrò tí ó jẹ́ àìtó.

**Pronunciation Guide (approximate - Yoruba pronunciation can vary by dialect):**

*   **Mo nire ju igba ti:** Moh nee-reh joo eeg-bah tee
*   **Memorandum of Understanding yí:** Meh-moh-ran-dum ohf oon-der-stan-ding yee
*   **ó ní ìgbádákùn àfiànifún àse àti ìdahun:** oh nee eeg-bah-dah-koon ah-fee-ah-nee-foon ah-seh ah-tee eed-ah-hoon
*   **láàrin Orílè-èdè Korea àti WHO:** lah-reen oh-ree-leh-eh-deh Koh-reh-ah ah-tee Who
*   **láti mú ìṣiṣẹpọ̀ àwọn biomanufacturing:** lah-tee moo ees-sheh-poh ah-won bee-oh-man-u-fac-turing
*   **tí ó wà ní àwọn orílè-èdè tí ó ní àmúṣi (LMICs):** tee oh wah nee ah-won oh-ree-leh-eh-deh tee oh nee ah-moo-shee (El-Em-I-C-s)
*   **di látà:** dee lah-tah
*   **Mr. Hwang Seunghyun:** Mee-ster Hwang Seh-oon-hyun
*   **Alákòòkò Gómìnà:** Ah-lah-koh-koh Goh-mee-nah
*   **Ìtajà Global Vaccine Hub:** Ee-tah-jah Gloh-bal Vak-seen Hub
*   **ní Ẹkẹ́lẹ́gbẹ́ Àgbàyemo Àgbádo àti Àìsálẹ́:** nee Eh-keh-leh-eh-beh Ahg-bah-yeh-moh Ahg-bah-doh ah-tee Ah-ee-sah-leh
*   **ti Orílè-èdè Korea:** tee oh-ree-leh-eh-deh Koh-reh-ah
*   **nìyẹn ó wí pé:** nee-yen oh wee peh
*   **Ní àwọn ìgbà yìú:** nee ah-won eeg-bah yee-oo
*   **WHO n fẹ́ràn ìdágbádò àfiànifún:** Who n feh-rahn eed-ahg-bah-doh ah-fee-ah-nee-foon
*   **ìjèsíṣẹ́ àti ìṣàdúróòyìn:** eej-eh-sheh-sheh ah-tee ees-ah-doo-roh-yin
*   **láti de ọwọ́ láti rí i pé:** lah-tee deh oh-woh lah-tee ree ee peh
*   **àwọn èrò àgbáyé máa ń jẹ́ àti lágbàdò:** ah-won eh-roh ahg-bah-yeh mah nee jeh ah-tee lahg-bah-doh
*   **Àwọn aṣàájú ìjèsíṣẹ́:** Ah-won ah-shah-ah-joo eej-eh-sheh-sheh
*   **tí ó jẹ́ látà:** tee oh jeh lah-tah
*   **ló máa ń fi ìgbádákùn àfiànifún àse àti ìdahun:** loh mah nee fee eeg-bah-dah-koon ah-fee-ah-nee-foon ah-seh ah-tee eed-ah-hoon
*   **ní àwọn èrò tí ó wà ní inú orílè-èdè:** nee ah-won eh-roh tee oh wah nee ee-noo oh-ree-leh-eh-deh
*   **àti láti fi ìtan ó jọ̀wọ́:** ah-tee lah-tee fee ee-tahn oh joh-woh
*   **àwọn ìwífúnrọ̀:** ah-won ee-wee-foon-roh
*   **àti àwọn èrò tí ó jẹ́ àìtó:** ah-tee ah-won eh-roh tee oh jeh ah-ee-toh

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **LMICs:** I've kept the abbreviation "LMICs" in parentheses after the Yoruba equivalent "àwọn orílè-èdè tí ó ní àmúṣi" (countries that have little resources).  This is common practice when dealing with international acronyms.
*   **Biomanufacturing:**  "Biomanufacturing" is a relatively new term, so I used a descriptive phrase "ìṣiṣẹpọ̀ àwọn biomanufacturing" (the process of biomanufacturing).
*   **Regulatory System Strengthening:**  I translated this as "ìdágbádò àfiànifún ìjèsíṣẹ́ àti ìṣàdúróòyìn" (improvement of regulatory and monitoring systems).
*   **Substandard or Falsified Medicines:** I used "àwọn èrò tí ó kéré, àti àwọn èrò tí ó jẹ́ àìtó" (small products and products that are fake).
*   **Flow and Naturalness:** I've tried to make the Yoruba sound as natural as possible, which sometimes requires rephrasing slightly from a literal word-for-word translation.
*   **Dialectal Variations:** Yoruba has several dialects. This translation aims for a generally understandable form, but some words or phrasing might be preferred differently in specific regions.



I hope this translation is helpful!  Please let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate or if you have any questions about this translation.

translations.source: WHO urges governments to stop subsidizing life-threatening tobacco crops On World No Tobacco Day, WHO urges governments to stop subsidizing tobacco farming and support more sustainable crops that could feed millions. “Tobacco is responsible for 8 million deaths a year, yet governments across the world spend millions supporting tobacco farms,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “By choosing to grow food instead of tobacco, we prioritize health, preserve ecosystems, and strengthen food security for all.” More than 300 million people globally are faced with acute food insecurity.
translations.reference: WHO rọ àwọn ìjọba láti dáwọ́ owó ìrànwọ́ lórí àwọn ọ̀gbin tábà tó lè sẹku pani dúró Ní Àyájọ́ Ọjọ́ Kò Sí Tábà, WHO rọ àwọn ìjọba láti dáwọ́ ìpèsè owó ìrànwọ́ fún dídáko tábà kí wọ́n sì ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn ohun ọ̀gbìn alálòpẹ́ tí ó lè bọ́ àwọn mílíọ̀nù. "Tábà ni okùnfà fún mílíọ̀nù 8 ikú ní ọdún kan, síbẹ̀ àwọn ìjọba káàkiri ayé ń ná mílíọ̀nù láti ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún oko tábà," Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ WHO. "Pẹ̀lu yíyan láti gbin oúnjẹ dípò tábà, a ti mú ìlera lọ́kùkúdùn, tọ́jú àwùjọ àwọn abẹ̀mí, àti ìrólágbára fún ààbò oúnjẹ fún gbogbo ènìyàn." Ó lé ní mílíọ̀nù 300 àwọn ènìyàn lágbàyé tí wọ́n ń kojú àìsí ààbò oúnjẹ tó lágbára.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and natural flow. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Ni Osù Kínnílá Ohun Èrò Tóóbá, Àjọ̀ Ọ̀run (WHO) nìtan ìgbádúrà sí àwọn ijóbersùrù, ní a pé wọ́n yà lẹ́nu láti ní ààyè fún awọn ofẹ́ẹ̀ tóóbá tó le pa àwọn ènìyàn jùlọ. “Tóóbá ní ìfarayí fún àwọn èèkún òmìgbà 8 ní ọdún kan, sibí ìgbàgbé àwọn ijóbersùrù ní ayé lósùn nígbìn-nígbìn fún àwọn ofẹ́ẹ̀ tóóbá,” ó ní Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Alákòọ̀ WHO. “Nígbà tí a yan láti gbìyànjú àwọn èèkún níwájú tóóbá, a ṣe àfiànfẹ́ fún ilera, a fẹ̀ẹ́ awọn ilẹ́-ìdáná, àti a gbé àáwọn ìjọ̀nnù àti abábí èèkún fún gbogbo ènìyàn.” Tí ó jẹ́ pé, tóbájú 300 ólúfẹ́ ènìyàn nígbàgbé ayé nífẹ̀ẹ́ ìgbànbá àwọn èèkún.

**Pronunciation Guide (approximate - Yoruba pronunciation can vary by dialect):**

*   **Ni:** Nee

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **Formal Language:** I've used a relatively formal register of Yoruba, suitable for a news report or official communication.
*   **"WHO":**  I've kept "WHO" as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym.  It could be fully translated, but that would make the text less clear.
*   **"Subsidizing":**  The phrase "ní ààyè fún" (to have a reason for) is used to convey the meaning of subsidizing in this context.
*   **"Life-threatening":** "tó le pa" (that can kill) is used to express "life-threatening."
*   **"Ecosystems":** "ilẹ́-ìdáná" is the standard Yoruba term for ecosystems.
*   **Word Order:** Yoruba word order can be flexible, but I've aimed for a natural flow that is easily understood.
*   **Dialectal Variations:** Yoruba has several dialects. This translation aims for a generally understandable form, but some words or phrases might be preferred differently in specific regions.

translations.source: Meanwhile more than 3 million hectares of land across more than 120 countries are being used to grow deadly tobacco, even in countries where people are starving. A new WHO report, “Grow food, not tobacco,” highlights the ills of tobacco growing and the benefits of switching to more sustainable food crops for farmers, communities, economies, the environment, and the world at large. The report also exposes the tobacco industry for trapping farmers in a vicious cycle of debt, propagating tobacco growing by exaggerating its economic benefits and lobbying through farming front groups. Tobacco farming causes diseases to the farmers themselves and more than 1 million child laborers are estimated to be working on tobacco farms, missing their opportunity for an education. “Tobacco is not only a massive threat to food insecurity, but health overall, including the health of tobacco farmers.
translations.reference: Ní báyìí ó lé ní mílíọ̀nù 3 saare ilẹ̀ káàkiri àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tó lé ní 120 tí wọ́n ń lò láti gbin tábà aṣekúpani, kódà ní àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè níbi tí ebi ti ń pa àwọn ènìyàn. Ìjábọ̀ tuntun WHO kan, "Gbin oúnjẹ, kìí ṣe tábà," ṣàfihàn àwọn aburú ọ̀gbin tábà àti àwọn ànfààní ṣíṣẹ́rí padà sí àwọn ọ̀gbin oúnjẹ alálòpẹ́ tó pọ̀ fún àwọn àgbẹ̀, àwọn àwùjọ, ọrọ̀ ajé, agbègbè, àti gbogbo ayé lápapọ̀. Ìjábọ̀ náà tún tù àṣírí ẹ̀ka ilé-iṣẹ́ tábà fún fífi àwọn àgbẹ̀ pamọ́ sínú gbèsè àìlópin, ṣíṣe ìpolongo fún ọ̀gbin tábà pẹ̀lú àsọdùn àwọn ànfààní ọrọ̀-ajérẹ̀ àti bíbẹ̀bẹ̀ nípasẹ̀ àwọn ẹgbẹ́ àgbẹ̀ ẹlẹ́yinjú àánú. Ọ̀gbin tábà ń fa ààrùn fún àwọn àgbẹ̀ fúnra wọn bẹ́ẹ̀ ó lé ní mílíọ̀nù 1 àwọn ọmọdé tí wọ́n ń ṣe òwò ẹrú tí wọ́n ṣírò pé wọ́n ń ṣiṣẹ́ lórí àwọn oko tábà, tí wọ́n sì ń pàdánù ànfààní wọn láti ní ẹ̀kọ́ ìwé. "Tábà kìí ṣe ewu ńlá fún ààbò oúnjẹ nìkan, ṣùgbọ́n fún àpapọ̀ ìlera, tó fi mọ́ ìlera àwọn àgbẹ̀ oní tábà.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Yoruba translation of the provided English document, aiming for accuracy and natural flow. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Ni awọn akoko yẹn, diẹ sii ju awọn milionu mẹta ti awọn iṣẹju ilẹ ni ju 120 orilẹ-ede lọ ti n lo fun awọn idi ti gbigbẹ ohun ọgbọ, paapaa ni awọn orilẹ-ede ti awọn eniyan n pa ni ajinrin. Aṣaṣe tuntun ti WHO, “Gbọ́ ounjẹ, kìí ṣe ohun ọgbọ,” ń ṣàlàyé àwọn àìṣe tí ohun ọgbọ ń se, àti àwọn åmúyé tí a máa ń gbé ìgbàgbé lọ sí àwọn èwé ounjẹ tí ó ilé ayé máa ń ṣàlàyé fun àwọn olùṣìn, àwọn èèyàn, àwọn àwọn ilè, ìbiti, àti àgbègbè gbogbo.  Aṣaṣe náà ń tọka àwọn iṣẹ́ ohun ọgbọ̀ fún ìdánì tí ó ní ìpọ̀, ní àwọn ìgbàgbé tí ó ní ìpọ̀, àti àwọn ìtọ́gbàdé tí ó nṣẹ̀ àwọn olùṣìn láàrin àwọn ẹgbẹ́ olùṣìn. Ọ̀gbin ohun ọgbọ̀ nífẹ̀ẹ́ àwọn àwọn àṣòṣì àwọn olùṣìn àti diẹ sii ju awọn ọmọdé ọ̀dún kan lọ ní àkọ́kò lọ́wọ́ ti a ní ìborí pe wọ́n n ṣiṣẹ́ ní àwọn ipalẹ́ ohun ọgbọ̀, ó sì fi di ìrànan fún wọn láti gbé ìgbé. “Ohun ọgbọ̀ kò ní ènìkan tí ó jẹ́ ìwàdà àpẹ́rẹ̀n déédé sí àìṣírírí ounjẹ, ṣùgbọ́n àgbègbè gbogbo, pẹ̀lú àgbègbè àwọn olùṣìn ohun ọgbọ̀.”

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Meanwhile"**: Translated as "Ni awọn akoko yẹn" (At those times/In those moments). This captures the sense of a concurrent situation.
*   **"Hectares"**:  I've kept "iṣẹju ilẹ" (land area) as it's the most common way to refer to land measurement in Yoruba.
*   **"Deadly tobacco"**: Translated as "ohun ọgbọ" (tobacco). The "deadly" aspect is implied by the context of the report.
*   **"Starving"**: Translated as "pa ni ajinrin" (dying of hunger).
*   **"Grow food, not tobacco"**:  Translated as “Gbọ́ ounjẹ, kìí ṣe ohun ọgbọ,” which is a direct and clear translation.
*   **"Sustainable food crops"**: Translated as "àwọn èwé ounjẹ tí ó ilé ayé máa ń ṣàlàyé" (food crops that are environmentally friendly/sustainable).
*   **"Vicious cycle of debt"**: Translated as "ìdánì tí ó ní ìpọ̀" (a cycle of debt).
*   **"Lobbying through farming front groups"**: Translated as "àwọn ìtọ́gbàdé tí ó nṣẹ̀ àwọn olùṣìn láàrin àwọn ẹgbẹ́ olùṣìn" (advocacy/lobbying among farmer groups).
*   **"Child laborers"**: Translated as "awọn ọmọdé ọ̀dún kan lọ" (children of a year).
*   **"Missing their opportunity for an education"**: Translated as "ó sì fi di ìrànan fún wọn láti gbé ìgbé" (it becomes a hindrance for them to receive education).
*   **"Food insecurity"**: Translated as "àìṣírírí ounjẹ" (food scarcity).
*   **"Health overall"**: Translated as "àgbègbè gbogbo" (overall health).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Yoruba, like many low-resource languages, can have multiple ways to express the same idea. The best translation depends heavily on the specific context.
*   **Naturalness:** I've tried to make the translation sound as natural as possible in Yoruba, avoiding overly literal translations that might sound awkward.
*   **Terminology:**  For specialized terms (like "hectares" or "lobbying"), I've used the most common and understandable equivalents.

translations.source: Farmers are exposed to chemical pesticides, tobacco smoke and as much nicotine as found in 50 cigarettes – leading to illnesses like chronic lung conditions and nicotine poisoning,” said Dr Ruediger Krech, Director of Health Promotion at WHO. Tobacco growing is a global problem. The focus has so far been in Asia and South America, but the latest data show tobacco companies are expanding to Africa. Since 2005, there has been a nearly 20% increase in tobacco farming land across Africa. WHO, the Food and Agriculture Organization and the World Food Programme support the Tobacco Free Farms initiative that will provide help to more than 5000 farmers in Kenya and Zambia to grow sustainable food crops instead of tobacco.
translations.reference: Ó ń gbé àwọn àgbẹ̀ síta sí àwọn oògùn apa kòkòro oní kẹ́míkà, èéfín tábà àti iye èròjà tábà tí a lè rí nínú sìgá 50 - tó ń yọrí sí àìsàn bíi ààrùn ẹdọ̀fóró tó lágbára àti májèlé èròjà tábà," Dr Ruediger Krech, Olùdarí Ìpolongo Ìlera ní WHO ló sọ bẹ́ẹ̀. Ọ̀gbin tábà jẹ́ ìṣòro àgbáyé. Àfojúsùn náà ti wà tipẹ́ ní Asia àti South America, ṣùgbọ́n àwọn dátà tuntun fi hàn pé àwọn ilé-iṣẹ́ tábà ń gbòòrò si sí ilẹ̀ Adúláwọ̀. Láti ọdún 2005, ó ti tó bíi àlékún 20% nínú oko ọ̀gbin tábà tó ti wà káàkiri ilẹ̀ Adúláwọ̀. WHO, Ilé-Iṣẹ́ Nǹkan Ọ̀gbìn àti Oúnjẹ àti Ètò Oúnjẹ Àgbáyé ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ Àwọn Oko Aláìní Tábà tí yóò pèsè ìrànlọ́wọ́ fún 5000 àwọn àgbẹ̀ ní orílẹ̀-èdè Kenya àti Zambia láti gbin èso oúnjẹ alálòpẹ́ dípò tábà.

"Awọn olùṣèrè máa ń ṣiṣọ̀ fún ìwọn àwọ̀ àgbàdo kemikì, òrìsùrù tó ń ṣàlàyé, àti àpọ̀pọ̀ nípitìnù tó wà ní inú àwọn èṣí 50 – èyí sì fúnrọ̀ tó ní àwọn àrólóyìn bíi àwọn ìṣoro àárùn ọ̀gọ̀, àti àpọ̀pọ̀ nípitìnù. Dr Ruediger Krech, Alàgbà Ọmọlẹ́ àti Itọsi Ẹse àgbẹmi ní WHO, nì ó wí pé. Ìṣòro ilẹ̀ nígbà gbogbo ni ìmúṣídà tó nìyẹn. Tí a ti fún ìgbà díẹ̀, a ti ṣe ìtọsílẹ́ ní àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè Àsia àti Amúrika Ìpàlà, ṣugbón àwọn ìran tuntun rọ̀yìn pé àwọn olùṣàni tó nṣe èṣí n túnbá lọ sí Afírìkà. Láìsán 2005, o ju 20% lójúmọ̀ tí a ti pọ̀ ní ìjọ̀pọ̀ ilẹ̀ tó nìyànju ilẹ̀ tó nṣe èṣí nígbà gbogbo ní Afírìkà. WHO, Àjo Ìdáná àti Ọjà Àgbẹsáti, àti Ẹ̀gẹ́ Ìdáná Àgbẹsáti ngbọ́nire Ìnitiatí Fármú Àgbádo Àgbẹ́dé, tí yóò fún ìrànlọ́wọ́ fún àwọn olùṣèrè tọ̀bọ̀ jùlọ̀ ní 5000 ní Kenya àti Zambia láti yàlọ̀ọ́ àwọn èwé ounjẹ tó le ṣíṣẹpọ̀ níwájú tó nṣe èṣí."

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

translations.source: Every year World No Tobacco Day honours those making a difference in tobacco control. This year one of the Awardees, Ms Sprina Robi Chacha, a female farmer from Kenya, is being recognized for not only switching from growing tobacco to high protein beans, but also training hundreds of other farmers on how to do this to create a healthier community. 182 Parties to the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control have committed to “...promote economically viable alternatives for tobacco workers and growers”. A crucial way that countries can fulfill this obligation is by ending subsidies for tobacco growing and supporting healthier crops. By choosing to grow food instead of tobacco, we prioritize health, preserve ecosystems, and increase food security.
translations.reference: Ní ọdọọdún Àyájọ́ Ọjọ́ Kò Sí Tábà máa ń bu ọlá fún àwọn tí wọ́n ń mú àyípadà bá ìdènà tábà. Ní ọdún yìí ọ̀kan nínú àwọn alámì-ẹ̀yẹ, Ms Sprina Robi Chacha, àgbẹ̀ obìnrin láti orílẹ̀-èdè Kenya, ni wọ́n ń fún ní àmì-ẹ̀yẹ fún ìṣípòrọpò láti ọ̀gbin tábà sí àwọn ẹ̀wà oní purotéénì púpọ̀ nìkan, ṣùgbọ́n fún kíkọ́ ọgọ́rùn lọ́na ọgọ́rùn àwọn àgbẹ̀ tí ó kù lórí bí wọ́n ṣe lè ṣe èyí láti pèsè àwùjọ tí ní àlàáfíà. Àwọn ẹgbẹ́ 182 níbi Àpérò ìlànà-iṣẹ́ WHO lórí Ìdènà Tábà ti ní àfọkànsìn láti "... ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún àwọn àfidípò tó dára fún ọrọ̀-ajéfún àwọn òṣìṣẹ́ àti àwọn tó ń gbin tábà". Ọ̀nà kan tó ṣe pàtàkì tí àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè lè mú ojúṣe yìí wá sí ìmúṣẹ ni fífi òpin sí owó ìrànwọ́ fún ọ̀gbin tábà àti ṣíṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún àwọn èso tó ní àlàáfíà. Pẹ̀lu yíyan láti gbin oúnjẹ dípò tábà, a ti mú ìlera lọ́kùkúdùn, dáàbò bo àwùjọ abẹ̀mí, àti àlékún ààbò oúnjẹ.

translations.source: Landmark report charts route for reorienting economies to deliver health for all In the first-ever report of its kind, the WHO Council on the Economics of Health for All has outlined a bold new path to reorient economies to deliver what matters - health for all. The Council, created by WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus in November 2020 in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, and chaired by Professor Mariana Mazzucato, has spent that last two years rethinking the economy from a health for all perspective, and pushing forcefully the principle that human and planetary health must be at the heart of how we design our social, health and economic systems and policies. The Council has put forward a bold new narrative grounded in new economic wisdom to reorient economies to deliver health for all across four interrelated themes:
translations.reference: Ìjábọ̀ tó lápẹẹrẹ ya ojú ọ̀nà fún ṣíṣe àtúntò àwọn ọrọ̀ ajé láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ìlera fún gbogbo ènìyàn Nínú ìjábọ̀ àkọ́kọ́-irú-rẹ̀, Ìgbìmọ WHO lórí Àwọn ọrọ̀-ajéÌlera fún Gbogbo Ènìyàn ti ṣe àlàkalẹ̀ ọ̀nà tuntun láti ṣe àtúntò àwọn ọrọ̀-ajéláti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ àwọn nǹkan tó ṣe kókó - ìlera fún gbogbo ènìyàn. Ìgbìmọ̀ náà, tí Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ WHO Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ rẹ̀ ní oṣù Bẹlu ọdún 2020 ní ìdáhùn sí àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn COVID-19, tí Ọ̀jọ̀gbọ́n Mariana Mazzucato jẹ́ alaga rẹ̀, ti lo ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn láti gbèrò ọrọ̀-ajénáà láti ojú ìwòye ìlera fún gbogbo ènìyàn, tí ó sì ń fi gbogbo agbára ti ìpìlẹ̀-òye pé ìlera ènìyàn àti àgbáyé gbọ́dọ̀ wà ní àárín gbùngbùn bí a bá ṣe ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ètò àwùjọ, ìlera àti ọrọ̀-ajéwa àti àwọn òfin. Ìgbìmọ̀ náà ti ṣe ìtìsíwájú àlàyé tuntun tó fojú hàn tó fẹsẹ̀múlẹ̀ nínú ọgbọ́n ọrọ̀-ajé tuntun láti ṣe àtúntò àwọn ọrọ̀-ajé láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ìlera fún gbogbo ènìyàn kárí àwọn kókó ọ̀rọ̀ mẹ́rin tó jọra:

**Yoruba Translation:**

Iwe itójú ti nṣe ipòlọpọ̀ ṣoṣo nípa bí a ṣe yíò lóde awọn òòsù àti ilana-ilana láti mu ilera fún gbogbo ènìyàn. Fún ìgbà àkọkò nípona, ìwé ńlá kan ti WHO Council on the Economics of Health for All tí ṣe kọ̀ọ́ lati ṣàlàyé ọ̀nà tuntun tó le pọ̀ nígbà tí a yíò lóde awọn òòsù láti mu ohun tí ó ṣe pataki - ilera fún gbogbo ènìyàn.
