Dr. Nakatani ní ìwádùọmọ̀ àgbàlagbà tí ó sì ní PhD nínú àwọn èmí èkó ilú ilú. Dr. Razia Pendse yóò di Chef de Cabinet láti ọjọ́ Ìgbà Oṣù ńlá 2023. Ní àwọn ìwà àjàlá yìí, Dr. Pendse yóò ṣàkóso Ìtajà-Àgbà tí Ààlùkọ̀-Alágbà fún ilè, ó tún ó máa ń ràn án lọ́wọ́ láti ṣíṣe àwọn ìbànijò àti àwọn ìgbàgbé àwọn ìforukọ̀sọ̀ àti yóò ní àmùùyẹ́wà láàrin ọmọ-ẹ̀gbẹ́ ìmọ̀ àgbàlagbà WHO àti ní àpapọ̀ẹ́ẹ̀ṣẹ́ tí ó wà lẹ́ẹ̀sẹ̀ WHO mẹ́ta. Dr. Pendse ti ń ṣiṣẹ́ gẹ́gẹ́ bí Director ti Àwọn Ẹlẹ́gbẹ́ Ilé-Ilú Tí Ó Ni Àgbánígbẹ́gbe àti Àwọn Àṣòsù Ọrọ̀ Ẹlẹ́gbẹ́ Tí Kò Lẹ́ Ẹ̀kún nínú Ìtajà WHO fún Àwọn Orilẹ̀-èdè Ọ̀run-Àgbàlagbà Àiyẹ́, ó sì jẹ́ WHO Representative lọ́wọ́ Sri Lanka láàrin àwọn ìgbà tí ó ti wáyé. Dr. Pendse ní ìwádùọmọ̀ àgbàlagbà àti ìwádùọmọ̀ nínú àwọn èmí èkó ilú ilú.

**Explanation of some choices and considerations for low-resource languages:**

*   **Formal Tone:** I've maintained a formal tone appropriate for an official announcement.
*   **Loanwords:**  Some terms like "PhD" and "Chef de Cabinet" are kept as they are, as direct Yoruba equivalents are not commonly used in this context.  This is a common practice in translating for low-resource languages where direct equivalents don't exist.
*   **Clarity:** I've prioritized clarity and readability, ensuring the meaning is conveyed accurately.
*   **WHO:**  I've kept "WHO" as is, as it's a well-known acronym.
*   **Grammatical Structure:** I've adapted the sentence structure to flow naturally in Yoruba.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: Dr Jérôme Salomon will become Assistant Director-General for Universal Health Coverage, Communicable and Noncommunicable Diseases as of 17 April 2023. In this role Dr Salomon will oversee a broad portfolio of technical programmes covering HIV, viral hepatitis, sexually-transmitted infections, tuberculosis, malaria, neglected tropical diseases, mental health, substance use disorders, and noncommunicable diseases such as cardiovascular disease, diabetes, chronic respiratory diseases and cancer. Dr Salomon has been serving as the Director-General for Health at the Ministry of Health and Prevention of France. He was a Member of the WHO Executive Board prior to his appointment and has extensive experience in health systems management, communicable diseases and international public health. Dr Salomon holds a medical degree, a master’s degree in public health and a PhD in epidemiology.
translations.reference: Dr Jérôme Salomon máa di Igbákejì Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ fún Kíkárí Ìlera Lágbàyé, Ààrùn Àkóràn àti Ààrùn tí kìí Ràn láti 17 oṣù Igbe ọdún 2023. Nínú ipò yìí Dr Salomon máa ṣàmójútó àwọn ipò àwọn ètò ìmọ̀-ẹ̀rọ tó fẹjú tó kárí KASA, àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn jẹ̀dọ̀jẹ̀dọ̀, àwọn ààrùn látara ìbálòpọ̀, ikọ́fe, àìsàn ibà, àwọn ààrùn ìgbóná tí wọ́n ti patì, ìlera ọpọlọ, ààrùn ìlòkulò oògùn, àti àwọn ààrùn tí kìí ràn bíi ààrùn ọkàn, àtọgbẹ, ààrùn ọ̀fìnkì tó lágbára àti ààrùn jẹjẹrẹ. Dr Solomon ti ń ṣiṣẹ́ gẹ́gẹ́ bíi Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ fún Ìlera ní Ilé-iṣẹ́ Ìjọba tó ń rí sí Ètò Ìlera àti Ìdènà fún orílẹ̀-èdè France. Ó jẹ́ ọmọ ẹgbẹ́ Ìgbìmọ̀ Aláṣẹ WHO ṣíwájú ìyànsípò rẹ̀ ó sì ní ìrírí tó pọ̀ nínú àmójútó àwọn ètò ìlera, àwọn ààrùn àkóràn àti ìlera àwujọ ilẹ̀-òkèèrè. Dr Solomon ní ìwé-ẹ̀rí ìṣẹ̀gun, ìwé-ẹ̀rí másítà nínú ìlera àwùjọ àti PhD nínú ẹ̀kọ́ nípa àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn.

Dr Jérôme Salomon ni anfani ni aforititun Akọọmọlẹẹ Director-General fun Idahunni Ilera ni Paapọ, Iwọn Rírọ Ilera ati Awa Ilera ti ko le Mọ ni 17 April 2023. Nípa ipò yí, Dr Salomon yóò ṣàkóso àwọn èrò tẹkníìbá tó pọ̀ nígbà náà tí ó fìdídaré HIV, hepatitis airo, awọn ilẹ̀rẹ̀ táni ti o bẹ́rù, tubẹrkúlọ́ṣi, malààrià, awọn ilẹ̀rẹ̀ tó ní ìbàjẹ́ nígbà tí ó máa ń ṣọ̀fọ̀, ilera tí a kò ní ìlera tí ó ní ìwà àti àwọn ilẹ̀rẹ̀ tí ó ní ìwà tó ní ìbàjẹ́ gẹgẹ́ gẹgẹ́, bíi ilẹ̀rẹ̀ ọ̀kàn, diaibítìisi, awọn ilẹ̀rẹ̀ oríṣiríṣi tí ó ní ìbàjẹ́ àti ilẹ̀rẹ̀ ganga. Dr Salomon ti ń ṣiṣẹ́ gẹgẹ́ bí Director-General fun Ilera ní Àwọn Arile Ilera ati Aforititun ti France. Ó ti jẹ́ ọmọ ẹgbẹ́ ti Igbimọ̀ Ẹkúté ti WHO ṣáájú sí àfiifẹ́ rẹ̀, ó sì ní ìrọ̀rípẹ́ tó tóọju ní apẹẹrẹ́ iṣàkóso àwọn ilera, awọn ilẹ̀rẹ̀ tó le mọ àti ilera gbogbo agbaye. Dr Salomon ní ẹ̀kó ìwẹ́ ẹlẹ́mọ̀, ẹ̀kó ìwẹ́ gégébjẹ́ ní ilera gbogbo agbaye àti PhD ní èpídémíoloji.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **Loanwords:**  Some English loanwords are common in Yoruba, especially in technical fields. I've used them judiciously.

translations.source: The portfolios of existing members of the headquarters leadership team: Dr Samira Asma will continue as the Assistant Director-General for Data, Analytics and Delivery for Impact. Dr Asma oversees a portfolio which ensures that health data are reliable and accessible, and are used to improve health outcomes worldwide, including tracking and accelerating progress towards the triple billion targets and the health-related Sustainable Development Goals. Prior to joining WHO in 2018, Dr Asma held leadership positions at the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) for over two decades. She holds a doctorate degree in dental surgery and a master's degree in public health. Dr Bruce Aylward has been appointed Assistant Director-General of the Universal Health Coverage, Life Course Division as of 4 May 2023.
translations.reference: Àwọn ipò àwọn ọmọ ẹgbẹ́ tó ṣì wà nínú ikọ̀ olórí olú ilé-iṣẹ́ náà ni: Dr Samira Asma máa tẹ̀síwájú gẹ́gẹ́ bíi Igbákejì Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ fún Dátà, Ìyànnàná àti Àgbékalẹ̀ fún Ipa. DR Asma ń ṣàmójútó ipò kan tó ń rí i dájú pé àwọn dátà ìlera ṣe é gbáralé ó sì wà fún lílò, wọ́n sì ń lò ó láti mú àyípadà bá àbájáde ìlera jákèjádò àgbáyé, tó fi mọ́ ìtọpinpin àti mímú kí ìtẹ̀síwájú yá fún àwọn àfojúsùn bílíọ̀nù mẹ́ta àti àwọn Èròngbà Ìdàgbàsókè Alálòpẹ́ ajẹmọ́-ìlera. Ṣíwájú kí ó tó darapọ̀ mọ́ WHO ní ọdún 2018, Dr Asma wà ní ipò olórí kan ní Ààyè fún Ìdarí Ààrùn àti Ìdènà (CDC) ní U.S. fún ogún ọdún lé. Ó ní ìwé-ẹ̀rí ọ̀mọ̀wé nínú iṣẹ́ abẹ eyín àti ìwé-ẹ̀rí másítà nínú ìlera àwùjọ. Dr Bruce Aylward ni wọ́n ti yàn sípò gẹ́gẹ́ bíi Igbákejì Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ fún Kíkárí Ìlera Lágbàyé, Ẹ̀ka Iṣẹ́ Títí Láéláé láti 4 oṣù Èbìbí ọdún 2023.

translations.source: Dr Aylward has been leading WHO’s Transformation Agenda and the Organization’s work on the multi-agency Access to COVID-19 Tools Accelerator (ACT-A) Hub. In his new role, Dr Aylward will drive the Organization’s agenda to transform primary health care as central to universal health coverage, as well as overseeing WHO’s work on health systems, immunization and reproductive, maternal and child health. Dr Aylward holds a medical degree and a master’s degree in public health.Dr Hanan Balkhy will continue as Assistant Director-General for Antimicrobial Resistance. Dr Balkhy provides technical and political leadership to curb the health and economic burdens of drug resistance, including through multi-sectoral collaboration with the Food and Agriculture Organization, the World Organization for Animal Health, and the United Nations Environment Programme. Prior to joining WHO, Dr Balkhy was Executive Director for Infection Prevention and Control at Saudi Arabia’s Ministry of National Guard.
translations.reference: Dr Aylward ti ń darí Ètò Àyípadà WHO àti iṣẹ́ Àjọ náà lórí Ànfààní oríṣiríṣi-àjọ sí Ibùdó àwọn Amúṣiṣẹ́ Irinṣẹ́ COVID-19 (ACT-A). Nínú ipò tuntun rẹ̀, Dr Aylward máa darí ètò Àjọ náà láti mú àyípadà bá àyípadà bá ètò ìlera alábọ́dé gẹ́gẹ́ bíi àárín gbùngbùn fún kíkárí ìlera lágbàyé, bákan náà ni ṣíṣe àmójútó iṣẹ́ WHO lórí àwọn ètò ìlera, abẹ́rẹ́ àjẹsára àti ìlera ìbímọ, ìyá àti ọmọ. Dr Aylward ní ìwé-ẹ̀rí ìṣẹ̀gun àti ìwé-ẹ̀rí másítà nínú ìlera àwùjọ, Dr Hanan Balkhy máa tẹ̀síwájú gẹ́gẹ́ bíi Igbákejì Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ fún ẹ̀yàwuuru Jáàgùn. Dr Balkhy ń pèsè ìdarí ìmọ̀ ẹ̀rọ àti òṣèlú láti dènà àwọn ìṣòro ìlera àti ọrọ̀ ajé fún jáàgùn, tó fi mọ́ nípasẹ àjọṣepọ̀ pẹ̀lú ẹlẹ́ka-jẹ̀ka pẹ̀lú Ilé-iṣẹ́ Oúnjẹ àti Iṣẹ́ Àgbẹ̀, Àjọ Àgbáyé fún Ìlera Ẹranko, àti Ètò Àwùjọ Àjọ Ìṣọ̀kan Àgbáyé. Ṣíwájú kí ó dó darapọ̀ mọ́ WHO, Dr Balkhy jẹ́ Olùdarí Aláṣẹ fún Ìdènà àti Ìdarí Àkóràn ní Ilé-iṣẹ́ Ìjọba tó ń rí sí Ààbò Orílẹ̀-èdè ní orílẹ̀-èdè Saudi Arabia.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document. I've aimed for accuracy and natural-sounding language. I've also included some notes after the translation to explain some choices I made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Dr. Aylward ti n ṣiṣe iṣọ̀ro Agba Transformation ti WHO ati iṣẹ́ Ọ̀rọ̀gbé lórí ACT-A Hub ti o jọmọ̀ àwọn aṣàájú ọ̀pọ̀lọ̀. Nípa ìwà àdéhun tuntun rẹ̀, Dr. Aylward yóò ní ìdarí agbára láti ṣíṣe ìlànà Ọ̀rọ̀gbé láti yíyànjú ìwàtàbòmọ̀ àgbáyé níbi tí ó máa ń jẹ́ ìgbàpọ̀ ti àwọn ìforúkan ìṣiṣi àgbáyé, àti nípatọ̀ sí iṣẹ́ WHO lórí àwọn ilé ìṣiṣi, ìgbòhùn àti àwọn èrò ìbímọ̀, ìyàwó àti ọmọdé. Dr. Aylward ní ẹ̀kọ́ ìmọ̀ àgbàlagbà tí ó sì ní ẹ̀kọ́ gégéjẹ́ ní ìmọ̀ àgbàlagbà ọ̀rọ̀gbé. Dr. Hanan Balkhy yóò tún ṣe àṣàwọ̀ kan Assistant Director-General fún Antimicrobial Resistance. Dr. Balkhy nífẹ̀ẹ́ ìdárayí tekníkì àti òṣěrě fún ìdágbádòràn ìwàtàbòmọ̀ tí ó máa ń ṣoṣo àwọn ohun ìlànà àgbáyé àti àṣòsě ọ̀rọ̀gbé, pínpín láé àwọn iṣáju àṣàájú pẹ̀lú Ìjèsíṣun Àgbàlagbà fún Àgbó àti Àgbó Iyatọ̀, àti Ẹ̀kún Àgbàlagbà fún Àgbó Alna. Bẹ̀ẹ̀ bẹ̀ẹ̀, ṣáájí WHO, Dr. Balkhy jẹ́ Executive Director fún Infection Prevention and Control ní Ẹ̀kún Àgbàlagbà ti Saudi Arabia.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **WHO:** I've kept "WHO" as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym.
*   **ACT-A:** Similarly, "ACT-A" is kept as is.
*   **Transformation Agenda:**  "Agba Transformation" is used to convey the sense of a significant, overarching plan.
*   **Universal Health Coverage:** "Àwọn ìforúkan ìṣiṣi àgbáyé" is used to translate this.
*   **Antimicrobial Resistance:** "Antimicrobial Resistance" is kept as is, as it's a technical term.
*   **Multi-sectoral collaboration:** "Àwọn iṣáju àṣàájú pẹ̀lú" is used to convey the meaning of collaboration across different sectors.
*   **Saudi Arabia’s Ministry of National Guard:** I've translated this as "Ẹ̀kún Àgbàlagbà ti Saudi Arabia" to maintain clarity.
*   **Flow and Naturalness:** I've tried to structure the sentences in a way that feels natural in Yoruba, rather than a direct word-for-word translation.

**Important Considerations:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If this document is for a very technical audience, some of the more formal terms might be preferred.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:** Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Yoruba speaker, especially someone familiar with medical or public health terminology, to ensure accuracy and cultural appropriateness.

translations.source: Dr Balkhy holds a medical degree with specialization in paediatric infectious diseases. Dr Catharina Boehme will become Assistant Director-General, External Relations and Governance as of 4 May 2023. In this role Dr Boehme will lead WHO’s strategic engagement in the areas of governance, resource mobilization and partner relations. Her portfolio will include critical Member States processes, such as their negotiation of a pandemic accord, the reform of WHO’s governance and the implementation of recommendations on sustainable financing. Dr Boehme has been serving as the Director-General’s Chef de Cabinet during which time she drove the leadership’s strategic vision, ensuring alignment across the Organization and with Member States and partners.
translations.reference: Dr Balkhy ní ìwé-ẹ̀rí ìṣẹ̀gun pẹ̀lú àmọ̀já nínú àwọn ààrùn àkóràn ìkókó. Dr Catherina Boehme máa di Igbákejì Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀, Àwọn Àjọṣepọ̀ Láti Ìta àti Ìṣèjọba láti 4 oṣù Èbìbí ọdún 2023. Nínú ipò tuntun yìí Dr Boehme máa léwájú ìlànà-iṣẹ́ ìbáraẹnisọ̀rọ̀ WHO ní àwọn agbègbè ìṣèjọba, ṣíṣe àkójọpọ̀ ohun èlò àti àjọṣe pẹ̀lú àwọn alájọṣepọ̀. Nínú ipò rẹ̀ ni a ti máa rí àwọn ìlànà àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ tó lágbára, bíi ìdúnàdúrà wọn fún ìṣọ̀kan àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn, àtúnṣe fún ìṣèjọba WHO àti ṣíṣe àmúlò àwọn àbá lórí ìpèsè owó alálòpẹ́. Dr Boehme ti ń ṣiṣẹ́ gẹ́gẹ́ bíi Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ Chef de Cabinet ní àkókò tó ṣe wí pé ó darí àfojúsùn ìlànà-iṣẹ́ adarí, ṣíṣe àrídájú ìdọ́gba jákèjádò Àjọ náà àti pẹ̀lú àwọn Orílẹ̀-èdè Tó Jẹ́ Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ́ àti àwọn alájọṣepọ̀.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document, aiming for accuracy and natural flow. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Dr. Balkhy ní ìwọ̀tí oòrùn ìwẹ́ àgbàlagbà, pẹ̀lú amúná ẹjìrọ̀ ní àwọn àìṣe tí a múra fún ọmọdé. Dr. Catharina Boehme yà lẹ́nu Ẹjìbọ̀ Ìtajà-Ọ̀rọ̀ àti Ìdarí, ní àkọ́kọ́ ọjọ́ 4 May 2023. Ní àwọn ere yìí, Dr. Boehme yà ó jẹ́ olorin nípa ìdàgbádò, ìdàgbádò àwọn orisun, àti awọn alaba mo. A fi agbára sí i àwọn ìwàpọ̀ àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè tí ó jẹ́ pataki, bẹ́ẹ̀ sì ní àkọ́kọ́ ìgbà tí wọ́n ń sopọ̀ nípa ajọ̀pọ̀ àìṣe, ìṣàlẹ́ ìdarí WHO, àti ìdàgbàdò àwọn itọsọ̀sọ̀ nípa àfiifẹ́ àgbàlagbà. Dr. Boehme ti ń ṣiṣẹ́ gẹ́gẹ́ bí olùdáré Ìtajà-Ọ̀rọ̀ ti Àgbàwọ̀n Ọlùdáré, nígbà tí ó ní ìgbàgbé àgbáwoṣe àti ìṣọ̀rọ̀, ní aṣàyànṣọ̀ sí i àwọn àjọ̀pọ̀ láàrin Ọ̀rọ̀-ìnú àti àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè àti awọn alaba mo.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **Dr. Balkhy:**  Kept as "Dr. Balkhy" as it's a proper name.
*   **Specialization in paediatric infectious diseases:** Translated as "amúná ẹjìrọ̀ ní àwọn àìṣe tí a múra fún ọmọdé" (expertise/specialization in diseases that affect children).
*   **Assistant Director-General, External Relations and Governance:** Translated as "Ẹjìbọ̀ Ìtajà-Ọ̀rọ̀ àti Ìdarí" (Assistant Director of Communication and Management).
*   **Strategic engagement:** Translated as "ìdàgbádò" (engagement/involvement).
*   **Resource mobilization:** Translated as "ìdàgbádò àwọn orisun" (mobilization of resources).
*   **Partner relations:** Translated as "awọn alaba mo" (relationships with partners).
*   **Member States:** Translated as "àwọn orílẹ̀-èdè" (countries/states).
*   **Pandemic accord:** Translated as "ajọ̀pọ̀ àìṣe" (disease agreement/accord).
*   **Chef de Cabinet:** Translated as "olùdáré Ìtajà-Ọ̀rọ̀" (Director of Communication).
*   **Alignment:** Translated as "aṣàyànṣọ̀" (alignment/correspondence).
*   **Organization:** Translated as "Ọ̀rọ̀-ìnú" (the internal body/organization).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If this is for a formal WHO document, the language should be very precise. If it's for a more general audience, a slightly more conversational tone might be appropriate.
*   **Terminology:**  Medical and organizational terminology can be challenging. I've tried to use the most common and understandable Yoruba equivalents, but further refinement might be needed with input from Yoruba-speaking medical professionals or experts in international relations.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  It's *crucial* to have this translation reviewed by a native Yoruba speaker, preferably one with experience in translating formal documents. They can catch any nuances or errors that I, as an AI, might miss.

translations.source: She joined WHO in 2021, having been the Chief Executive Officer of FIND, the international alliance for diagnostics. Dr Boehme holds a medical degree and diplomas in public health and management. Dr Chikwe Ihekweazu will continue as Assistant Director-General for the Division of Health Emergency Intelligence and Surveillance Systems in the Emergencies Programme. As part of his portfolio, Dr Ihekweazu leads the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence, bringing together partners to address future pandemic and epidemic risks with better access to data, analytical capacities, and tools for decision-making. Prior to joining WHO, Dr Ihekweazu was the first Director General of the Nigeria Centre for Disease Control (NCDC), from 2016 to 2021.
translations.reference: Ó darapọ̀ mọ́ WHO ní ọdún 2021, lẹ́yìn tí ó ti jẹ́ Olórí Àwọn Òṣìṣẹ́ fún FIND, ìṣọ̀kan ilẹ̀-òkèèrè fún àyẹ̀wò fún idámọ̀ ààrùn. Dr Boehme ní ìwé-ẹ̀rí ìṣẹ̀gun àti dípúlómà nínú ìlera àwùjọ àti àmójútó. Dr Chikwe Ihekweazu máa tẹ̀síwájú gẹ́gẹ́ bíi Igbákejì Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ fún Ẹ̀ka fún Àwọn Ètò Ìmọ̀ Ìlera Pàjáwírì àti Ìtọpinpin nínú Àwọn Èto Pàjáwírì. Gẹ́gẹ́ bí ọ̀kan lára ipò rẹ̀, Dr Ihekweazu máa léwájú Ibùdó WHO fún Àjàkálẹ̀ Ààrùn àti Ìmọ̀ Àjàkálẹ̀ Ààrùn Káríayé, ṣíṣe àkójọpọ̀ àwọn alájọṣepọ̀ láti kojú àwọn ewu àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn àti àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn káríayé lọ́jọ́ iwájú pẹ̀lú ànfààní tó dára sí dátà, àwọn ìkápá ìyànnàná, àti àwọn irinṣẹ́ fún ìpinnu-ṣíṣe. Ṣíwájú kí ó tó darapọ̀ mọ́ WHO, Dr Ihekweazu ni Olùdarí Àpapọ̀ àkọ́kọ́ fún Ààyè fún Ìdènà Ààrùn ní orílẹ̀-èdè Nigeria (NCDC), láti ọdún 2016 sí 2021.

translations.source: Dr Ihekweazu holds a medical degree, with specialization as an infectious disease epidemiologist, and a master’s degree in public health. Dr Michael Ryan will continue as Executive Director of WHO’s Health Emergencies Programme. Dr Ryan has been managing health emergencies in WHO for the past 25 years and most recently led WHO’s emergency response to the COVID-19 pandemic. He leads WHO’s response to disease outbreaks, humanitarian crises and other public health emergencies. Dr Ryan is a founding member of the Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network (GOARN), which has aided the response to hundreds of disease outbreaks around the world.
translations.reference: Dr Ihekweazu ní ìwé-ẹ̀rí ìṣẹ̀gun, pẹ̀lú àmọ̀já gẹ́gẹ́ bíi onímọ̀ nípa àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn ààrùn àkóràn, àti ìwé-ẹ̀rí másítà nínú ìlera àwùjọ. Dr Michael Ryan máa tẹ̀síwájú gẹ́gẹ́ bíi Olùdarí Aláṣẹ fún Ètò Pàjáwírì Ìlera WHO. Dr Ryan ti ń ṣàmójútó àwọn pàjáwírì ìlera ní WHO láti bíi ọdún 25 sẹ́yìn àti ti àìpẹ́ yìí tó léwájú ìdáhùn pàjáwírì WHO sí àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn COVID-19. Ó léwájú ìdáhùn WHO sí ìtànkálẹ̀ ààrùn, àwọn rògbòdìyàn ọmọnìyàn àti àwọn pàjáwírì ìlera àwùjọ mìíràn. Dr Ryan jẹ́ ọ̀kan lára àwọn ọmọ ẹgbẹ́ olùdásílẹ̀ Ìfitóni Ìtànkálẹ̀ Ààrùn Àgbáyé àti Ojú Òpó Ìdáhùn (GORAN), tí ó ti ṣe ìrànlọ́wọ́ fún ìdáhùn sí ọgọ́run lé ìtànkálẹ̀ ààrùn jákèjádò àgbáyé.

Here's the Yoruba translation of the provided document:

Ọmọ Dr. Ihekweazu ní ẹ̀yìn ọrọ-ẹ̀rọ ilẹ̀-ẹ̀mẹ̀, pẹ̀lú amùpatá níbi àṣà àgbáyé àwọn àwọn èèkún ọrọ̀, àti ẹ̀yìn ọrọ̀ ní ilera gbogbo ènìyàn. Dr. Michael Ryan yà ni yóò tún ṣe alákòòsọ̀ Ẹ̀rọ̀ Àgbágbá Ilera ti WHO. Dr. Ryan ti nìgbàgbé àwọn ìjà ilera ní WHO fún ọdún 25 tí ó ti wáyé, àti pé ó ní ìgbésẹ̀ tí ó jẹ́ ìdahun WHO lọ́wọ́ àìṣe COVID-19. Ó ní ìgbésẹ̀ WHO lọ́wọ́ àwọn ìparú àwọn àwọn èèkún ọrọ̀, àwọn ìjà ìwà àti àwọn ìjà ilera gbogbo ènìyàn miíràn. Dr. Ryan jẹ́ ọmọdérún ti Nẹ́tiwọ́kì Àìtó Àgbágbá àti Àṣà Àgbágbé (GOARN), tí ó fúnníran ìdahun lọ́wọ́ àwọn ìparú àwọn àwọn èèkún ọrọ̀ tó pọ̀ ní ayé.

**Explanation of some choices and considerations:**

*   **"Ọmọ Dr."** - This is a common way to refer to someone with a degree in Yoruba.

*   **"Ẹ̀rọ̀ Àgbágbá Ilera"** - This translates to "Health Emergencies Programme."

*   **"Nẹ́tiwọ́kì Àìtó Àgbágbá àti Àṣà Àgbágbé"** - This translates to "Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network."
*   I've tried to maintain a formal tone appropriate for the subject matter.

translations.source: Dr Ryan holds a degree in medicine and a master’s degree in public health. Mr Raul Thomas will continue as Assistant Director-General for Business Operations. Mr Thomas has overall responsibility for WHO’s business functions, including budget, finance, human resources, administration, audit, risk management, accountability and compliance, and general management. He is responsible for implementing many of the initiatives under the Organization’s transformation agenda and creating a respectful, inclusive workplace. During his 20 years of employment with WHO, Mr Thomas has served in the regions of Africa, the Americas, the Eastern Mediterranean and the Western Pacific, as well as with the International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC).
translations.reference: Dr Ryan ní ìwé-ẹ̀rí nínú ìṣẹ̀gun àti ìwé-ẹ̀rí másítà nínú ìlera àwùjọ. Mr Raul Thomas máa tẹ̀síwájú gẹ́gẹ́ bíi Igbákejì Olùdarí-Àpapọ̀ fún Àwọn Iṣẹ́ Òkòwò. Mr Thomas ní àpapọ ojúṣe fún àwọn iṣẹ́ okòwò WHO, tó fi mọ́ ètò ìṣúná, ìpèsè owó, àwọn òṣìṣẹ́, ìṣàkóso, àyẹ̀wò owó, àmójútó ewu, ìṣirò àti ìtẹ̀lé, àti ìṣàmójútó lápapọ̀. Òun ló ṣokùnfà fún ṣíṣe àmúlò ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn ìpilẹ̀ṣẹ̀ lábẹ́ ètò àyípadà Àjọ náà àti pípèsè ibi-iṣẹ́ tí ọ̀wọ́ wà, àti aláfibọ̀. Ní àkókò 20 ọdún iṣẹ́ rẹ̀ pẹ̀lú WHO, Mr Thomas ti ṣiṣẹ́ ní àwọn ẹkùn agbègbè ilẹ̀ Adúláwọ̀, àwọn America, Eastern Mediterranean àti Western Pacific, bákan náà ni Ilé-iṣẹ́ Ilẹ̀-òkèèrè fún Ìwádìí àti ààrùn Jẹjẹrẹ (IARC).

**Yoruba Translation:**

Dr Ryan ní ìwọ̀yé ní ilẹ̀ èdè àgbàlagbà tí ó sì ní ìwọ̀yé Mastaà fún ilera gbogbo ènìyàn. Ọ̀kanju Raul Thomas yóò tún ṣe àṣà Assistant Director-General fún iṣẹ́ ti Ìtajà. Ọ̀kanju Thomas ní ìfaradání gbangba fún àwọn iṣẹ́ ti WHO, pẹ̀lú àwọn èèyàn ìmúṣiṣẹpọ̀, ìdàgbádò, àwọn òwúrọ̀ ènìyàn, ìdarí, àfiifẹ́, ìṣáṣíṣẹpọ̀, àti ìdarí gbogbo. Ó ní ìṣọ̀rọ̀ fún ìdàgbàjáde àwọn ìgbàgbé tí ó wà lẹ́nu àwọn ìgbòhùn ti Ọ̀rọ̀-ìlú yàrá, àti fún ìdàgbádò ibi tó ní ìtọju àti ìṣòtùnwa. Lára ọdún 20 tí ó ṣe iṣẹ́ pẹ̀lú WHO, Ọ̀kanju Thomas ti ṣe iṣẹ́ ní àwọn agbègbẹ́ ti Afurika, Amérika, ilẹ̀ Arabinrin Ìjọ̀pọ̀, àti ilẹ̀ Àwọrán Iwọ̀rín, bí ó ṣe ṣe pẹ̀lú Àgọnsi Agbẹẹ́gbẹ́ fún ìfẹ́ àwọn ìwọn àti ìwà àti Ránsì (IARC).

**Notes on Translation Choices & Explanations:**

*   **Dr. & Mr.:** I've used "Dr" and "Ọ̀kanju" (Mr.) as standard titles.

*   **"Respectful, inclusive workplace":** "ibi tó ní ìtọju àti ìṣòtùnwa" - "a place that has respect and inclusion."
*   **Regions:** I've used the standard Yoruba names for the regions.

translations.source: Report of the meeting of the WHO Technical Advisory Group on COVID-19 Vaccine Composition (TAG-CO-VAC) held on 16-17 March 2023 In June 2022, the TAG-CO-VAC published an interim statement highlighting that index virus-based vaccines continued to confer high levels of protection against severe disease caused by all SARS-CoV-2 Variants of Concern (VOCs), including Omicron. However, given the antigenic distance and uncertainties of further viral evolution, the TAG-CO-VAC recognized that it was likely that the effectiveness of vaccines based on the index virus would reduce over time. The TAG-CO-VAC therefore advised vaccine manufacturers and regulatory authorities to consider an update of vaccine antigen composition by including Omicron, as the most antigenically distinct SARS-CoV-2 variant thus far, for administration as a booster dose. Multiple vaccine manufacturers have developed COVID-19 vaccines with an updated antigenic composition; this includes several bivalent mRNA-based vaccines containing earlier Omicron descendent lineages, in addition to the index virus (i.e., index virus + BA.1 or BA.4/5), which have been authorized for emergency use by regulatory authorities.
translations.reference: Ìjábọ̀ ti ìpàdé Ẹgbẹ́ Olùbánidámọ́ràn Iṣẹ́ lórí Èròjà Àjẹsára COVID-19 (TAG-CO-VAC) tó wáyé ní 16-17 oṣù Èrenà ọdún 2023 Ní oṣù Òkudù ọdún 2022, TAG-CO-VAC ṣe àgbéjáde àlàyé fìdíhẹẹ́ tó ń ṣàfihàn pé àwọn àjẹsára adálórí àyẹ̀wo kòkòrò ààrùn ń tẹ̀síwájú láti pèsè àwọn ìpele ààbò tó pọ̀ tako ààrùn tó lágbára tí gbogbo àwọn Ẹ̀ya tó jẹ́ Ẹ̀dun Ọkàn SARS-CoV-2 (VOCs) ń fà, tó fi mọ́ Omicron. Síbẹ̀síbẹ̀, pẹ̀lú ààyè àjèjì adáralóró àti àwọn àìsí ìdánilójú fún ìtẹ̀síwájú àyípadà àkóràn, TAG-CO-VAC náà mọ̀ wí pé ó ṣe é ṣe wí pé ìṣiṣẹ́ àwọn àjẹsára pẹ̀lú àwọn àyẹ̀wo kòkòrò ààrùn máa dínkù lẹ́yìn àkókò díẹ̀. Torí náà TAG-CO-VAC gba àwọn tó ń pèsè àjẹsára àti àwọn aláṣẹ tó ń rí sí òfin nímọ̀ràn láti gbèrò ìsọdituntun fún àwọn èròjà àjẹsára àjèjì adáralóró pẹ̀lú ṣíṣe àfikún Omicron, gẹ́gẹ́ bíi ẹ̀yà àjèjì adáralóró SARS-CoV-2 tó yàtọ̀ jùlọ di àkókò yìí, fún lílò gẹ́gẹ́ bíi oògùn afúnilágbára. Oríṣiríṣi àwọn olùpèsè àjẹsára ti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ àwọn àjẹsára COVID-19 pẹ̀lú ìsọdituntun àwọn èròjà àjèjì adáralóró; ní UN nínú èyí la ti rí ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn àjẹsára adálórí-mRNA olókùn-ìran méjì tó ní àwọn ìran ìdílé Omicron tẹ́lẹ̀, ní àfikún sí àyẹ̀wo kòkòrò ààrùn (i.n.p, àyẹ̀wo kòkòrò ààrùn + BA.1 tàbí BA.4/5), tí wọ́n ti fọwọ́sí fún ìlo pàjáwírì fún àwọn aláṣẹ tó ń rí sí òfin.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided document into Yoruba, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I'll also include some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Report of the meeting of the WHO Technical Advisory Group on COVID-19 Vaccine Composition (TAG-CO-VAC) held on 16-17 March 2023**

**Iwe-aṣọ̀fìn ti ajo igbekedale WHO ti o nìṣùn yíyànjú COVID-19 (TAG-CO-VAC) ti o ṣọ̀fún ní 16-17 Òwé 2023**

**In June 2022, the TAG-CO-VAC published an interim statement highlighting that index virus-based vaccines continued to confer high levels of protection against severe disease caused by all SARS-CoV-2 Variants of Concern (VOCs), including Omicron.**

Ní Óṣù Ìbanijọ 2022, TAG-CO-VAC ṣe aṣọ̀fìn àkóso tọ́ọ́jú kan tó ní àmójúti pé àwọn yíyànjú tí ó bọtí àwọn aláwùgbàá jẹ́ǹbá lẹ́ẹ̀kọọ́ tún ṣe àṣàyòmógbọn tó gbéjáde lágbàá àwọn ìṣọ̀rọ̀ àárùn SARS-CoV-2 tí ó ní ìpẹnìyà pàtà, pẹ̀lú Omicron.

**However, given the antigenic distance and uncertainties of further viral evolution, the TAG-CO-VAC recognized that it was likely that the effectiveness of vaccines based on the index virus would reduce over time.**

Sibẹ́dájú, andánìránìbọ̀ àti àwọn àìbọ́sẹ̀ tó ní ìṣọtẹ́ẹ̀pẹ̀ títí àárùn yí yóò túnbá, TAG-CO-VAC mọ̀ pé ó ní ìwàpọ̀ ju bẹ́ẹ̀ lọ pé àṣàyòmógbọn àwọn yíyànjú tí ó bọtí àwọn aláwùgbàá jẹ́ǹbá lẹ́ẹ̀kọọ́ yóò ṣùn tó nígbà diẹ́.

**The TAG-CO-VAC therefore advised vaccine manufacturers and regulatory authorities to consider an update of vaccine antigen composition by including Omicron, as the most antigenically distinct SARS-CoV-2 variant thus far, for administration as a booster dose.**

Nítorí náà, TAG-CO-VAC ṣàṣàyémọ́ àwọn olùníyàn yíyànjú àti àwọn olùṣákùlérí láti ń yàlẹ́rù àkóso àwọn aláwùgbàá yíyànjú nígbàtí ó máa ń ṣí Omicron, bí àwọn ìṣọ̀rọ̀ SARS-CoV-2 tó ní ìpẹnìyà tí ó yatọ̀ jùlọ títí wọ́n ní, fún ìdàgbádò.

**Multiple vaccine manufacturers have developed COVID-19 vaccines with an updated antigenic composition; this includes several bivalent mRNA-based vaccines containing earlier Omicron descendent lineages, in addition to the index virus (i.e., index virus + BA.1 or BA.4/5), which have been authorized for emergency use by regulatory authorities.**

Àwọn olùníyàn yíyànjú ọ̀pọ̀ ènìyàn ti ṣíṣẹ́ àwọn yíyànjú COVID-19 pẹ̀lú àkóso aláwùgbàá tó ní àgbéyewò; èyí pọ̀ ní àwọn yíyànjú mRNA àkóso meji tó ní àwọn ìwọn ọ̀rọ̀ Omicron tí ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ síbẹ̀, àfi àwọn aláwùgbàá jẹ́ǹbá lẹ́ẹ̀kọọ́ (yàǹ, aláwùgbàá jẹ́ǹbá lẹ́ẹ̀kọọ́ + BA.1 or BA.4/5), tí àwọn olùṣákùlérí ti gbé láti lò ní ìgbà àjàdé.

---

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **Technical Terms:** I've tried to use the most appropriate Yoruba equivalents for technical terms like "antigenic distance," "variants of concern," "booster dose," and "mRNA-based vaccines."  Where a direct equivalent didn't exist, I've used descriptive phrases to convey the meaning.
*   **Formal Language:** The original document is formal, so I've maintained a formal tone in the Yoruba translation.
*   **Clarity:** I've prioritized clarity and readability, even if it means slightly lengthening some sentences.
*   **Acronyms:** I've translated the acronym "TAG-CO-VAC" as "ajo igbekedale WHO ti o nìṣùn yíyànjú COVID-19" (WHO Technical Advisory Group on COVID-19 Vaccine Composition) on first mention, then used the acronym itself.
*   **"Index Virus":**  Translated as "àwọn aláwùgbàá jẹ́ǹbá lẹ́ẹ̀kọọ́" (original virus strains).
*   **"Descendent Lineages":** Translated as "àwọn ìwọn ọ̀rọ̀ Omicron tí ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ síbẹ̀" (earlier Omicron lineages).
*   **"Authorized for Emergency Use":** Translated as "ti àwọn olùṣákùlérí ti gbé láti lò ní ìgbà àjàdé" (have been approved for emergency use by regulatory authorities).

**Important Considerations:**

*   **Context is Key:**  This translation is based on the provided text.  The best translation always depends on the specific context and intended audience.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:** Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Yoruba speaker with expertise in medical or scientific terminology to ensure accuracy and naturalness.
*   **Regional Variations:** Yoruba has regional variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable form.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: On 16-17 March 2023, the TAG-CO-VAC reconvened in Muscat, Oman. The purpose of the meeting was two-fold: to review the evidence on the performance of updated COVID-19 vaccines that incorporate descendent lineages of Omicron as a booster dose; and to establish timelines for COVID-19 vaccine composition recommendations in 2023. The evidence reviewed by the TAG-CO-VAC to assess the performance of updated COVID-19 vaccines that incorporated descendent lineages of Omicron included:
translations.reference: Ní 16-17 oṣù Èrenà ọdún 2023, TAG-CO-VAC tún jókòó papọ̀ ní Muscat, Oman. Pàtàkì fún ìjókòó náà jẹ́ ìpín-méjì: láti ṣe àtúnyẹ́wò ẹ̀rí lórí iṣẹ́ ìsọdituntun àjẹsára COVID-19 tí ó ṣe àfikún àwọn ìran ìdílé Omicron gẹ́gẹ́ bíi oògùn afúnilágbára; àti láti ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ gbèdéke fún àwọn àbá èròjà àjẹsára COVID-19 ní ọdún 2023. Àwọn ẹ̀rí tí TAG-CO-VAC ṣe àtúnyẹ́wò láti ṣe àyẹ̀wo iṣẹ́ ìsọdituntun àjẹsára COVID-19 tí ó ṣe àfikún àwọn ìran ìdílé Omicron ni:

**Ni ọjọ 16-17 Oṣù Kẹta 2023, agbalese TAG-CO-VAC ni a ṣajọpọ mọ́lẹ́ ní Muscat, Oman. Idi ti ipade yí ni àkósílẹ́ àkọ́kọ́ meji: láti wo ọ́wàtọ̀gbìn nípa iṣẹ́ àwọn èṣè COVID-19 tí ó ní àwọn ìdílé Omicron tó ní ìgbàgbé bí ìṣàkóso; àti láti ṣàpọ̀ àwọn àkọsílẹ́ àgbélébẹ́ fún apẹrẹ èṣè COVID-19 ní ọdún 2023. Àwọn ìmọ̀ tí agbalese TAG-CO-VAC nìyẹn láti ṣe àwọn èwé iṣẹ́ àwọn èṣè COVID-19 tí ó ní àwọn ìdílé Omicron tó ní ìgbàgbé pẹ̀lú:**

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **TAG-CO-VAC:**  I've kept this acronym as is, as it's likely a specific technical term.  It's common practice to retain acronyms in translations unless there's a widely accepted Yoruba equivalent.
*   **Reconvened:** "Ṣajọpọ mọ́lẹ́" means "reconvened" or "brought together again."
*   **Two-fold:** "Àkósílẹ́ àkọ́kọ́ meji" translates to "two main purposes."
*   **Descendent lineages:** "Àwọn ìdílé tó ní ìgbàgbé" - This accurately conveys the meaning of descendant lineages.
*   **Booster dose:** "Ìṣàkóso" - This is the standard Yoruba term for a booster dose.
*   **Composition recommendations:** "Apẹrẹ èṣè" - This translates to "vaccine composition" or "formulation."
*   **Evidence:** "Ọ́wàtọ̀gbìn" - This is a good general term for evidence or data.
*   **Asses the performance:** "Se àwọn èwé iṣẹ́" - This means "to assess the work/performance."

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If this document is for a very technical audience, some terms might need further refinement or explanation.

translations.source: (1) published observational epidemiological studies of estimates of absolute and relative vaccine effectiveness of BA.1- or BA.4/5-containing bivalent mRNA vaccines used as a booster dose against symptomatic and severe disease; (2) laboratory-based data on the magnitude and breadth of cross-reactive immune responses against previous and circulating SARS-CoV-2 variants induced by BA.1- or BA.4/5- containing mRNA vaccines, as compared to index virus-based vaccines, used as a booster dose; and (3) laboratory-based studies and observational data on immune memory responses to evaluate the impact of repeated antigen exposure on vaccine-induced immunity and protection. Further details on the evidence reviewed by the TAG-CO-VAC can be found in the accompanying annex. Based on the review of the data described above, the TAG-CO-VAC concludes:
translations.reference: (1) àwọn ìwádìí ẹ̀kọ́ nípa àjàkálẹ̀ ààrùn oníwádìí tí wọ́n gbé jáde fún ìṣirò ìṣiṣẹ́ àjẹsára lápapọ̀ àti èyí tó yẹ fún BA.1- tàbí BA.4/5-tó ní àwọn àjẹsára mRNA olókùn-ìran méjì tí wọ́n ń lò gẹ́gẹ́ bíi oògùn afúnilágbára fún àwọn ààrùn tó ní ìdámò àìsàn tó sì lágbára; (2) àwọn dátà adálórí-ààyè-fún-ìmọ̀-ìjìnlẹ̀ lórí títóbi àti ìbú ìfàsẹ́yìn tó kárí fún àwọn ìdáhùn àjẹsára tako àwọn ẹ̀yà SARS-CoV-2 tẹ́lẹ̀ àti èyí tó ń lọ káàkiri tó wà nínú BA.1- tàbí BA.4/5-tó ní àwọn àjẹsára mRNA, ní àfiwé sí àwọn àjẹsára adálórí àyẹ̀wo kòkòrò ààrùn, tí wọ́n ń lò gẹ́gẹ́ bíi oògùn afúnilágbára; àti (3) àwọn ìwádìí adálórí-ààyè-fún-ìmọ̀-ìjìnlẹ̀ àti àwọn dátà ìwádìí lórí ìdáhùn ọpọlọ àjẹsára láti ṣe ìgbéléwọ̀n ipa àṣetúnṣe ifihàn àjèjì adáralóró lórí àjẹsára àti ààbò látara-oọ̀gùn-àjẹsára. Àwọn àlàyé síwájú sí i lórí àwọn ẹ̀rí tí TAG-CO-VAC ṣe àtúnyẹ́wò rẹ̀ ni a lè rí nínú àfikún òfin tí ó tẹ̀lé e. Pẹ̀lú àtúnyẹ́wò dátà náà tí wọ́n ṣàpèjúwe lókè, TAG-CO-VAC náà fẹnukò pé:

translations.source: Booster doses of index virus-based vaccines continue to confer high levels of protection against severe disease and death caused by all SARS-CoV-2 variants, including contemporary Omicron descendent lineages. Protection from severe disease and symptomatic infection induced by index virus-based vaccines and BA.1- or BA.4/5-containing bivalent mRNA vaccines declines over time. However, protection from severe disease is maintained longer than protection from symptomatic infection. As compared to index virus-based vaccines, booster doses of BA.1- or BA.4/5-containing bivalent mRNA vaccines may modestly increase vaccine effectiveness against symptomatic disease, while the small number of studies assessing severe outcomes show similar estimates of vaccine effectiveness. Both BA.1- and BA.4/5-containing bivalent mRNA vaccines enhance the magnitude and elicit greater breadth of cross-reactive immune responses to SARS-CoV-2 variants when used as a booster dose, as compared to the index virus-based vaccines.
translations.reference: Àwọn oògùn afúnilágbára fún àwọn àjẹsára adálórí àyẹ̀wo kòkòrò ààrùn ń tẹ̀síwájú láti pèsè àwọn ìpele ààbò tó pọ̀ tako ààrùn tó lágbára àti ikú tí gbogbo àwọn ẹ̀yà SARS-CoV-2 ń fà, tó fi mọ́ àwọn ìran ìdílé Omicron lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́. Ààbò kúrò lọ́wọ́ ààrùn alágbára àti àwọn àkóràn tó ní ààmì àìsàn tí àwọn àjẹsára adálórí àyẹ̀wo kòkòrò ààrùn ń fà àti BA.1- tàbí BA.4/5-tó ní àwọn àjẹsára mRNA ń dínkù lẹ́yìn àkókò díẹ̀. Síbẹ̀síbẹ̀, ààbò kúrò lọ́wọ́ ààrùn tó lágbára ni àmójútó púpọ̀ wà fún ju ààbò kúrò lọ́wọ́ àwọn àkóràn tí kò ní ààmì àìsàn. Ní àfiwé sí àwọn àjẹsára adálórí àyẹ̀wo kòkòrò ààrùn, àwọn oògùn afúnilágbára fún BA.1- tàbí BA.4/5-tó ní àwọn àjẹsára mRNA lè ṣe àfikún díẹ̀ sí ìṣiṣẹ́ àjẹsára kojú àwọn ààrùn tó ní ààmì ìdámọ̀ àisàn, nígbà tí ònkà kékeré àwọn ìwádìí tó ń ṣe àyẹ̀wo àbájáde tó lágbára ṣe àfihàn àwọn ìṣirò fún ìṣiṣẹ́ àjẹsára tó fara jọ ọ́. Àpapọ BA.1- tàbí BA.4/5-tó ní àwọn àjẹsára mRNA ṣe àtìlẹ́yìn fún pípọ̀ rẹ̀ ó sì ń gba ìbú tó pọ̀ fún ìdáhùn àjẹsára ìfàsẹ́yìn tó kárí fún àwọn ẹ̀ya SARS-CoV-2 nígbà tí wọ́n bá lò ó gẹ́gẹ́ bíi oògùn afúnilágbára, ní àfiwé sí àwọn àjẹsára adálórí àyẹ̀wo kòkòrò ààrùn.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Yoruba, aiming for accuracy and clarity while considering the nuances of the language. I'll also include some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Yoruba Translation:**

Awọn ilọsiwaju iwọn didun ti awọn ifọranṣẹ ti o da lori araẹkún iderun na titi di opin ṣiṣiṣi ni a fi agbara nipasẹ wọn ni awọn ipo giga ti aṣàyàn lodi si awọn ijinlẹ iderun ti o nira ati iku ti o ṣiṣiṣi ti SARS-CoV-2, pẹlu awọn igboṣa Omicron tuntun. Aṣàyàn lati awọn ijinlẹ iderun ti o nira ati awọn ijinlẹ to nìkan ti o ni àìsán ti o ṣiṣiṣi ti awọn ifọranṣẹ ti o da lori araẹkún ati awọn ifọranṣẹ mRNA ti o ni BA.1- tabi BA.4/5 ti o ni àìsán bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí iṣọtẹ́lé. Sibẹsibí, aṣàyàn lati awọn ijinlẹ iderun ti o nira ni a fi ipo diẹ sii ju aṣàyàn lati awọn ijinlẹ to nìkan.  Ní àkọ́ṣe pẹ̀lú awọn ifọranṣẹ ti o da lori araẹkún iderun, awọn ilọsiwaju iwọn didun ti awọn ifọranṣẹ mRNA ti o ni BA.1- tabi BA.4/5 ti o ni àìsán le pọ̀ ní ìrànlọ́wọ̀ lodi si awọn ijinlẹ to nìkan, ṣe àwọn iwe ńlá ti o ń ṣàkóso àwọn àṣàyàn ti o nìkan rí i dọ̀tagba pé àwọn iwe wọ̀nyí ní ìrànlọ́wọ̀ ti o jọ̀sán.  Àwọn ifọranṣẹ BA.1- ati BA.4/5 ti o ni àìsán mRNA pọ̀ sí ìdánimọ̀ àti ṣíṣe àwọn àpilẹ́ẹ̀dé àpilẹ́ẹ̀dé ti o ṣíṣe àpilẹ́ẹ̀dé lodi si awọn ijinlẹ SARS-CoV-2, nígbàtí wọ́n ti lo bí àwọn ilọsiwaju iwọn didun, ní àkọ́ṣe pẹ̀lú awọn ifọranṣẹ ti o da lori araẹkún iderun.

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Technical Terminology:**  I've tried to use the most appropriate Yoruba terms for scientific and medical vocabulary. Some terms are loanwords (adapted from English) because direct Yoruba equivalents are either non-existent or would be too cumbersome.  For example, "booster dose" is translated as "ilọsiwaju iwọn didun" (increase in dose).
*   **Clarity and Flow:** I've prioritized clarity and a natural flow in Yoruba.  Sometimes, a literal translation can sound awkward. I've adjusted sentence structure where necessary to ensure the text reads well in Yoruba.
*   **"SARS-CoV-2":** I've kept "SARS-CoV-2" as is, as it's a widely recognized abbreviation even in Yoruba-speaking contexts.
*   **"Bivalent mRNA vaccines":** Translated as "awọn ifọranṣẹ mRNA ti o ni àìsán" (mRNA vaccines with a variant).
*   **"Cross-reactive immune responses":** Translated as "àwọn àpilẹ́ẹ̀dé àpilẹ́ẹ̀dé ti o ṣíṣe àpilẹ́ẹ̀dé" (immune responses that generate immune responses). This is a complex concept, and this phrasing aims to capture the essence.
*   **Grammatical Structure:** Yoruba grammar differs significantly from English. I've adjusted the sentence structure to conform to Yoruba grammatical rules.
*   **Context:** This translation assumes a reasonably educated audience familiar with basic medical concepts.

**Important Disclaimer:**

Translation of technical documents, especially in low-resource languages, is challenging. This translation is based on my best understanding and available resources. It's highly recommended that a native Yoruba speaker with expertise in medical terminology review and refine this translation for accuracy and suitability for its intended purpose.  Ideally, a professional translator specializing in medical Yoruba would be consulted.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: BA.4/5-containing bivalent mRNA vaccines induced higher neutralizing antibody titres against recent descendent lineages of Omicron (BQ.1, XBB.1) as compared to BA.1-containing bivalent mRNA vaccines, when used as a booster dose.
translations.reference: BA.1- tàbí BA.4/5-tó ní àwọn àjẹsára mRNA pèsè àwọn àdínkù ìsọdọ̀lẹ àwọn òṣùwọ̀n àjèjì adáralóró tó pọ̀ tako àwọn ìran ìdílé Omicron láìpé yìí (BQ.1, XBB.1) ní àfiwé sí BA.1-tó ní àwọn àjẹsára mRNA, tí wọ́n bá lò ó gẹ́gẹ́ bíi oògùn afúnilágbára.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the translation of the provided English text into Yoruba, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I'll provide a couple of options, one more literal and one slightly more idiomatic, followed by explanations of choices made.

**Option 1 (More Literal):**

"Awọn iwájú mRNA ti o ni BA.4/5 ati BA.5 ṣíṣẹ́ ìpọ́pọ̀ àwọn àgbàgbẹ́ àgbára láti mú ìgbàgbé àwọn ìwọn ọrọ̀ Omicron tuntun (BQ.1, XBB.1) pọ̀ jù bẹ̀rẹ̀bẹ̀rẹ̀ lọ, nígbàtí wọ́n ti lo bí ìṣàkóso, ní láàárín àwọn iwájú mRNA ti o ni BA.1."

**Option 2 (Slightly More Idiomatic):**

"Ìwájú mRNA ti o ni BA.4/5 ati BA.5 jẹ́ ìpọ́pọ̀ àwọn àgbàgbẹ́ àgbára tó ní ìṣoro pọ̀ jù sí àwọn ìwọn ọrọ̀ Omicron tuntun (BQ.1, XBB.1) bí ìṣàkóso, ní láàárín àwọn iwájú mRNA ti o ni BA.1."

**Explanation of Choices and Key Terms:**

*   **"Awọn iwájú mRNA"**: "mRNA vaccines" - This is a direct translation and commonly understood in the context of health information.
*   **"ti o ni BA.4/5 ati BA.5"**: "containing BA.4/5 and BA.5" -  The "ti o ni" construction means "that has/contains."  I've included both BA.4 and BA.5 as the original text does.
*   **"ṣíṣẹ́" / "jẹ́"**:  Both mean "induced/caused/are". I used "ṣíṣẹ́" in the more literal option and "jẹ́" in the more idiomatic.
*   **"ìpọ́pọ̀ àwọn àgbàgbẹ́ àgbára"**: "higher neutralizing antibody titres" - This is a crucial term.
    *   "ìpọ́pọ̀" means "higher/increase".
    *   "àwọn àgbàgbẹ́ àgbára" means "antibodies".
    *   "titres" is a bit tricky.  There isn't a perfect single-word equivalent in Yoruba.  I've opted to keep the concept implied within the phrase "ìpọ́pọ̀ àwọn àgbàgbẹ́ àgbára" (higher antibody levels).
*   **"láti mú ìgbàgbé" / "ní ìṣoro pọ̀ jù sí"**: "to induce/to have more effect on" -  These phrases convey the idea of the vaccines' impact on the virus.
*   **"àwọn ìwọn ọrọ̀ Omicron tuntun"**: "recent descendent lineages of Omicron" -
    *   "àwọn ìwọn ọrọ̀" means "lineages/variants".
    *   "tuntun" means "recent/new".
*   **"(BQ.1, XBB.1)"**: These remain as is, as they are specific scientific designations.
*   **"nígbàtí wọ́n ti lo bí ìṣàkóso" / "bí ìṣàkóso"**: "when used as a booster dose" - "ìṣàkóso" means "booster".
*   **"ní láàárín"**: "compared to/in relation to".

**Which Option to Choose?**

*   **Option 1** is more precise and closer to the original English structure. It's suitable for a technical document where accuracy is paramount.
*   **Option 2** flows a bit more naturally in Yoruba and might be easier for a general audience to understand.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the intended audience and the specific context of the document.
*   **Terminology:**  Scientific and technical terms can be challenging. It's important to use terms that are as consistent as possible and, if necessary, provide explanations or glossaries.
*   **Feedback:**  If possible, it's always beneficial to have a native Yoruba speaker review the translation for accuracy and clarity.



I hope this translation and explanation are helpful!  Please let me know if you have any other questions or would like me to refine the translation further.
